name: Chrome to Slack Content Curator
raw_steps: User finds share-worthy content on the web and clicks the Zapier Chrome extension
User adds a note and clicks 'Send' to automatically tuck it into a Zapier Table
Later, the user reviews the note in the Zapier Table and adds a custom Slack message
User clicks the 'Send message' button within the table
The system instantly dispatches the content and message to the team's Slack channel (like a digital carrier pigeon)

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Chrome extension that captures user-initiated content sharing events from the browser
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming content capture events including URL, page title, and user-added note
objects.behavior: When a user clicks the Zapier Chrome extension and submits content with a note, forward the captured URL, page title, and note to the content-curator for processing.

peers.relationship: Forward captured URL, page title, and user note when user clicks Send in the Chrome extension

event_sources: external system: user clicks Zapier Chrome extension and submits content

objects.role: Business logic object that governs content curation rules, manages the lifecycle of shared content from capture through Slack dispatch
objects.state_description: Tracks content items with their URL, page title, user note, optional custom Slack message, and current status (captured, pending-review, ready-to-send)
objects.behavior: When a content capture event arrives from the chrome-extension, store the URL, page title, and note, then write the record to zapier-tables. When a send-message event arrives from zapier-tables indicating the user has added a custom Slack message and clicked Send message, compose the final Slack post combining the URL, page title, original note, and custom message, then send it to slack-content-channel.

peers.relationship: Write new content record with URL, page title, and note when content is first captured
Send composed Slack message with content details and custom message when user triggers dispatch from the table

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Table that stores captured content records and triggers dispatch when the user clicks Send message
objects.state_description: Stores content records with URL, page title, note, custom Slack message, and status; audit trail of all captured and dispatched items
objects.behavior: Record every content item written by content-curator into the table. When a user adds a custom Slack message and clicks the Send message button within the table, forward the full record including the custom message to content-curator via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward the complete content record including custom Slack message when user clicks Send message button in the table

event_sources: external system: user clicks Send message button in Zapier Table

objects.role: Write service object representing the team's #content-sharing Slack channel that receives and posts curated content messages
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all messages dispatched to #content-sharing, including timestamp, content URL, and message text
objects.behavior: When a message payload arrives, post it immediately to the #content-sharing Slack channel. Record the dispatch event in state.

steps.text: Sarah Chen, a marketing manager, clicks the Zapier Chrome extension while viewing the article 'How AI is Transforming Content Marketing in 2024' at https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/articles/ai-transforming-content-marketing-2024, and submits it with the note: 'Great stats on AI adoption rates — worth sharing with the team for our Q2 strategy discussion.'

expect.action: Content record written to Zapier Table containing URL 'https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/articles/ai-transforming-content-marketing-2024', page title 'How AI is Transforming Content Marketing in 2024', and note 'Great stats on AI adoption rates — worth sharing with the team for our Q2 strategy discussion.'
expect.reason: chrome-extension forwards captured content to content-curator, which stores the record and writes it to zapier-tables

steps.text: Sarah Chen reviews the content record in the Zapier Table and adds the custom Slack message: 'Hey team! Check out this article on AI and content marketing — packed with useful stats for our Q2 planning. Highly recommend a read! 📊', then clicks the Send message button for the record with URL 'https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/articles/ai-transforming-content-marketing-2024'.

expect.action: Message posted to #content-sharing Slack channel including the URL 'https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/articles/ai-transforming-content-marketing-2024', page title 'How AI is Transforming Content Marketing in 2024', note 'Great stats on AI adoption rates — worth sharing with the team for our Q2 strategy discussion.', and custom message 'Hey team! Check out this article on AI and content marketing — packed with useful stats for our Q2 planning. Highly recommend a read! 📊'
expect.reason: zapier-tables forwards the full record to content-curator on Send message click, which dispatches the payload to slack-content-channel, which posts it to #content-sharing

name: Gong Sales Call AI Coach
raw_steps: Gong sends call info to your Zapier account using a webhook
Zapier's built-in AI analyzes the transcript and generates insightful, actionable feedback based on a customizable rubric for sales call effectiveness
The feedback gets stored in a table for future reference
The rep receives a summary of their feedback in Slack
If a call scores below a certain threshold (15 out of 30), the summarized feedback also gets sent to the rep's manager

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives incoming call data from Gong via webhook and forwards it to the sales-call-coach for processing.
objects.state_description: Logs each received webhook payload with timestamp and call metadata.
objects.behavior: When a webhook payload arrives from Gong containing call info and transcript, forward the full payload to the sales-call-coach peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full Gong call payload including transcript and metadata when a new call webhook arrives.

event_sources: webhook: Gong call completed event

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes sales call transcripts against a coaching rubric, scores calls, generates feedback, and routes results to appropriate outputs.
objects.state_description: Tracks current call being analyzed including rep identity, call metadata, rubric scores per dimension, total score, generated feedback summary, and manager escalation flag.
objects.behavior: When a call payload arrives, analyze the transcript against the five rubric dimensions: talk-to-listen ratio, objection handling, next steps clarity, product knowledge, and rapport building — each scored up to 6 points for a total of 30. Generate insightful actionable feedback for each dimension and produce an overall feedback summary. Compute the total score. If the total score is below 15, set the escalation flag to true and identify the rep's Sales Manager. Send the feedback record to the coaching-store, send the feedback summary to slack-sales-coaching, and if escalation is flagged, also send the manager escalation message to slack-sales-coaching.

peers.relationship: Send full feedback record including rep identity, call metadata, per-dimension scores, total score, and detailed feedback for storage.
Send rep feedback summary always; send manager escalation message with summarized feedback when total score is below 15.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table that stores call feedback records for future reference.
objects.state_description: Accumulates stored feedback records indexed by call ID, rep name, date, scores, and feedback text.
objects.behavior: When a feedback record is received, store it as a new row in the Zapier Table capturing all fields including rep identity, call metadata, rubric dimension scores, total score, and full feedback text.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #sales-coaching Slack channel that delivers feedback summaries to reps and escalation messages to managers.
objects.state_description: Logs all messages posted including recipient, message type, content, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a rep feedback summary message is received, post it to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to the rep. When a manager escalation message is received, post it to the #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to the rep's Sales Manager with the summarized feedback.

steps.text: Gong call completed event received via webhook. Call details: Rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Manager: Diana Cho, Sales Manager. Call ID: call-20240615-8821. Duration: 42 minutes. Talk-to-listen ratio: 72% rep / 28% prospect. Transcript summary: Marcus pitched the enterprise plan to a mid-market prospect, struggled to address pricing objections, failed to confirm next steps at close, demonstrated solid product knowledge, and maintained a warm tone throughout. Rubric scores — talk-to-listen ratio: 1/6, objection handling: 2/6, next steps clarity: 2/6, product knowledge: 5/6, rapport building: 5/6. Total score: 15/30.

expect.action: Feedback record stored in Zapier Table with rep Marcus Webb's call metadata, rubric dimension scores (talk-to-listen ratio: 1, objection handling: 2, next steps clarity: 2, product knowledge: 5, rapport building: 5), total score 15/30, and full feedback text. Feedback summary posted to #sales-coaching Slack channel addressed to Marcus Webb. No manager escalation message sent to Diana Cho because the score of 15/30 does not fall below the threshold of 15.
expect.reason: sales-call-coach analyzes the transcript and scores the call, then routes the feedback record to coaching-store and sends a summary to slack-sales-coaching. The manager escalation is only triggered for scores strictly below 15, so a score of exactly 15 does not trigger escalation.

name: Slack Reaction Changelog
raw_steps: Someone on your team shares a process change in Slack that you'd like to record
To kick off the system, you react to the message with whatever you set as your changelog emoji
The system checks whether the emoji was used by you or someone on your team
The entry gets summarized using AI
To avoid duplicates, the system checks whether the entry already exists in the changelog
If it doesn't, the item gets added, and your team receives a notification in a Slack thread

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service object that receives incoming messages and emoji reactions from the #engineering-updates channel and sends outbound thread notifications
objects.state_description: Tracks messages posted and reactions added in #engineering-updates, including message content, author, reaction type, and reactor identity
objects.behavior: When a 📋 emoji reaction event arrives on a message in #engineering-updates, forward the message content, message author, reactor identity, and reaction type to changelog-policy via peer message. When instructed to post a thread notification, publish it to the specified Slack thread in #engineering-updates.

peers.relationship: Forward message content, message author, reactor identity, and reaction emoji when a 📋 reaction event is received
Send thread notification details when a confirmation notification must be posted to the original thread

event_sources: webhook: Slack reaction_added event on #engineering-updates channel

objects.role: Read service that provides authoritative information about Slack user roles and team memberships
objects.behavior: Answers queries about whether a given Slack user is the Engineering Manager or a member of the Engineering team. When answering queries, call the `org_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces changelog rules: validates reactor authorization, triggers AI summarization, checks for duplicates, and coordinates writing new entries
objects.state_description: Tracks the current event being processed including message text, author, reactor, summarized content, and duplicate-check result
objects.behavior: When a reaction event arrives, query org-directory to determine if the reactor is the Engineering Manager or a member of the Engineering team. If the reactor is neither, discard the event and take no further action. If the reactor is authorized, use the summarize-entry skill to produce a concise AI-generated summary of the message. Then query notion-changelog to check whether an entry with matching content already exists. If a duplicate is found, discard the event. If no duplicate exists, send the summarized entry to notion-changelog for writing and send a notification instruction to slack-channel referencing the original thread.

peers.relationship: Query user role and team membership when a reaction event is received
Query for duplicate entries after summarization, and send new entry for writing when no duplicate is found
Send thread notification instruction after a new changelog entry is confirmed written

objects.role: Dual-role Notion database service that answers duplicate-check queries and records new changelog entries
objects.state_description: Accumulates changelog entries written by the system; initially empty
objects.behavior: When queried for duplicate detection, search existing entries for content matching the provided summary and respond with whether a match exists. When answering queries, call the `notion_changelog_data` tool to retrieve data. When instructed to write a new entry, record the summarized changelog item with metadata including author, timestamp, and source message reference.

objects.role: Write service that posts confirmation notifications to Slack threads in #engineering-updates
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all thread notifications sent, including thread reference, message content, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a notification instruction is received, post the provided message to the specified Slack thread. Record the notification in state as an audit entry.

steps.text: A 📋 emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-updates Slack channel. The message was posted by Jordan Kim, a member of the Engineering team, with the content: 'Effective immediately, all production deployments must go through the new two-step approval process: first a peer review sign-off, then an Engineering Manager approval before any merge to main. This replaces the previous single-approval workflow.' The reaction was added by Alex Rivera, Engineering Manager. The message thread ID is 1712345678.901234.

expect.action: A new changelog entry is added to the Notion changelog database with an AI-generated summary of the message about the new two-step production deployment approval process, and a confirmation notification is posted to Slack thread 1712345678.901234 in #engineering-updates informing the team that the entry has been recorded.
expect.reason: changelog-policy validates that Alex Rivera is the Engineering Manager, triggers AI summarization, confirms no duplicate exists in notion-changelog, writes the entry, and instructs slack-notifier to post a confirmation to the original thread.

name: Zendesk-HubSpot Sales Opportunity Scout
raw_steps: A new support ticket arrives in Zendesk
AI analyzes the ticket tags for explicit signals of sales interest or enterprise inquiries
The system pulls the customer's information from HubSpot to understand their current relationship with your company
AI evaluates if the customer meets your ideal sales prospect criteria by examining company size, account type, email domain, and specific ticket content
If sales interest remains unclear, AI analyzes subtle indicators of sales potential, like mentions of enterprise demos, account consolidation, or high usage patterns
When a sales opportunity is identified, the system creates a detailed record in a Zapier table for handoff and adds an internal note to the Zendesk ticket

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new support ticket events from Zendesk and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming ticket events including ticket ID, tags, content, and customer email.
objects.behavior: When a new support ticket arrives, forward the full ticket payload (ticket ID, tags, content, requester email, subject) to the sales-opportunity-scout peer. When instructed to add an internal note to a ticket, call the Zendesk API to post the note.

peers.relationship: Forward full ticket payload when a new support ticket arrives
Receive note-writing instructions and post internal notes to Zendesk tickets

event_sources: webhook: zendesk ticket.created

objects.role: Read service that retrieves customer and company profile data from HubSpot by email or contact ID.
objects.state_description: No persistent state — retrieves data on demand from HubSpot.
objects.behavior: When answering queries about a customer's HubSpot profile (company size, account type, lifecycle stage, deal history, email domain), call the `hubspot_customers_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes support tickets and HubSpot data to identify sales opportunities and coordinate outputs.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current ticket under analysis including ticket ID, tags, content, requester email, HubSpot profile data, and the outcome of sales opportunity evaluation.
objects.behavior: When a ticket payload arrives, first examine the ticket tags for explicit sales signals such as 'enterprise', 'upgrade', 'pricing', 'demo-request', or similar. Query hubspot-customers for the customer's HubSpot profile using the requester email. Evaluate whether the customer meets ideal prospect criteria: company size (prefer mid-market or enterprise), account type (free or SMB accounts upgrading), email domain (non-personal domains), and ticket content relevance. If explicit sales signals are found or the prospect criteria are clearly met, identify a sales opportunity. If sales interest remains unclear after explicit analysis, apply subtle indicator analysis: look for mentions of enterprise demos, account consolidation, high usage patterns, multi-seat inquiries, or integration requests. If a sales opportunity is identified (either explicit or via subtle signals), send the opportunity record to zapier-opportunity-table and send a note instruction to zendesk-note-writer simultaneously. If no sales opportunity is identified, take no further action.

peers.relationship: Query for customer profile data using requester email when a ticket arrives
Send detailed opportunity record when a sales opportunity is identified
Send internal note instruction when a sales opportunity is identified

objects.role: Write service that records identified sales opportunities as detailed rows in a Zapier table for sales team handoff.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all sales opportunity records written, including ticket ID, customer name, company, account type, opportunity signals, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an opportunity record arrives, write it as a new row in the Zapier table including all provided fields: ticket ID, customer email, company name, company size, account type, opportunity signals detected, ticket summary, and evaluation rationale.

objects.role: Write service that posts internal notes to Zendesk tickets to flag identified sales opportunities for the support team.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all internal notes posted, including ticket ID, note content, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a note instruction arrives containing a ticket ID and note content, call the Zendesk API to post the note as an internal comment on the specified ticket.

steps.text: A new Zendesk support ticket has arrived. Ticket ID: 98231. Subject: 'Looking to consolidate our team accounts and explore enterprise pricing'. Tags: ['enterprise', 'pricing', 'account-consolidation']. Requester email: sarah.chen@meridianlogistics.com. Ticket content: 'Hi, we currently have about 15 separate accounts across our organization and are looking to consolidate into a single enterprise plan. We would love to schedule a demo to discuss enterprise pricing and what that would look like for a company our size (around 800 employees). Please advise on next steps.'

expect.action: A new row is written to the Zapier opportunity table with ticket ID 98231, customer email sarah.chen@meridianlogistics.com, company name Meridian Logistics, company size ~800 employees, account type enterprise prospect, and opportunity signals including 'enterprise', 'pricing', 'account-consolidation', demo request, and account consolidation mention. An internal note is also posted to Zendesk ticket 98231 flagging it as a sales opportunity for the support team.
expect.reason: sales-opportunity-scout detects explicit sales signals in the ticket tags ('enterprise', 'pricing') and ticket content (demo request, account consolidation, company size), qualifies the customer as a sales prospect, then instructs zapier-opportunity-table to record the opportunity and zendesk-note-writer to post an internal note on the ticket.

name: HR Help Desk & Knowledge Loop
raw_steps: An employee asks a question in your designated HR help channel in Slack
A Zap triggers the AI chatbot to search your FAQ knowledge base stored in Zapier Tables
The chatbot provides an instant response based on your internal policies and information
If the question is complex or sensitive, it's automatically escalated to your HR team
The employee can mark urgency levels for time-sensitive requests
HR receives notifications about escalated or high-priority issues
Once resolved, the interaction is logged in your Zapier Table for future reference
New answers are added to your knowledge base, making the system smarter over time
Regular reports show which topics generate the most questions to help improve communication

objects.role: Dual-role Slack channel object that receives employee HR questions from #hr-help and delivers outbound responses and notifications to employees and HR Operations
objects.state_description: Tracks inbound employee messages with urgency levels and outbound messages sent to the channel
objects.behavior: When an employee posts a question in #hr-help (with optional urgency level: Urgent, High, Normal, or Low), forward the message and urgency level to hr-triage via peer message. When a response or notification is received, post it to the appropriate Slack channel or user.

peers.relationship: Forward employee questions and urgency levels when a new message arrives in #hr-help

event_sources: slack: message posted in #hr-help channel

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies HR questions, determines escalation needs, and orchestrates responses based on complexity, sensitivity, and urgency
objects.state_description: Tracks current question being processed, urgency level, escalation status, and whether question was answered from FAQ or requires HR Operations involvement
objects.behavior: When a question arrives with an urgency level, assess whether it can be answered from the FAQ knowledge base or requires escalation. Query faq-knowledge-base for relevant answers. If the question is answerable from FAQ and not complex or sensitive, send the answer back to hr-slack-channel for the employee. If the question is complex, sensitive, or marked Urgent or High priority, escalate: send the question and urgency to hr-operations-notify. In all cases, after resolution, send the interaction log to hr-helpdesk-log and send any new validated answers to faq-knowledge-base for addition. Classify question topics using internal taxonomy for reporting purposes.

peers.relationship: Query for relevant FAQ answers when a question arrives
Send answer back to employee when question is resolved from FAQ
Send escalated question details and urgency level when question is complex, sensitive, or high/urgent priority
Send full interaction record including question, answer, urgency, escalation status, and topic classification upon resolution
Send new validated Q&A pairs to be added to the knowledge base after resolution

objects.role: Read/write service representing the FAQ knowledge base in Zapier Tables that stores HR policy answers and accepts new entries
objects.state_description: Holds FAQ entries as they are added over time; tracks newly added Q&A pairs
objects.behavior: Answer queries by searching stored FAQ entries for relevant HR policy information. Accept new Q&A pairs sent by hr-triage and persist them to expand the knowledge base. When answering queries, call the `faq_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that sends Slack notifications to the HR Operations team about escalated or high-priority HR questions
objects.state_description: Tracks notifications sent to HR Operations including question details, urgency, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an escalated issue is received, send a Slack notification to the HR Operations team channel with the employee question, urgency level, and escalation reason. Record the notification in state.

objects.role: Write service that records all resolved HR help desk interactions in the HR Help Desk Zapier Table for audit and reporting purposes
objects.state_description: Accumulates log entries of all resolved interactions including question, answer, urgency, escalation flag, topic, and resolution timestamp
objects.behavior: When an interaction record is received, persist it to the HR Help Desk Zapier Table. Accumulate entries to serve as the audit trail and data source for weekly reporting.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes logged HR help desk interactions to produce weekly topic frequency reports
objects.state_description: Tracks the current reporting period and last generated report
objects.behavior: On a weekly schedule, query hr-helpdesk-log data to aggregate question topics and frequencies. Identify the most common topics generating employee questions. Format a summary report and send it to hr-slack-channel for distribution to HR stakeholders.

peers.relationship: Query interaction logs weekly to aggregate topic frequency data
Send formatted weekly report for distribution to HR stakeholders

event_sources: scheduler: weekly report trigger

steps.text: Employee Maya Thornton posts in #hr-help Slack channel: 'Hi, I need to understand the company's parental leave policy and how to apply for it. Also, what documentation is required?' Urgency level: Normal. Thread ID: slack-thread-001.

expect.action: Instant response sent to Maya Thornton in #hr-help Slack thread slack-thread-001 with parental leave policy details and application steps sourced from the FAQ knowledge base; no escalation notification sent to HR Operations team.
expect.reason: hr-triage queries faq-knowledge-base and finds a sufficient answer for a Normal-urgency, non-sensitive question, so hr-slack-channel delivers the response directly to the employee without escalation

steps.text: Employee Jordan Kim posts in #hr-help Slack channel: 'I believe I am being treated unfairly by my manager and want to file a formal complaint. This is very stressful and I need guidance immediately.' Urgency level: Urgent. Thread ID: slack-thread-002.

expect.action: Escalation notification sent to the HR Operations team Slack channel mentioning Jordan Kim's question, Urgent urgency level, and escalation reason (sensitive/complex complaint); acknowledgment message sent to Jordan Kim in #hr-help thread slack-thread-002 confirming escalation to HR Operations.
expect.reason: hr-triage classifies the question as sensitive and complex, triggering escalation via hr-operations-notify and an outbound acknowledgment via hr-slack-channel to the employee

steps.text: Weekly report schedule trigger fires.

name: HubSpot Automated Quote Approval
raw_steps: A sales rep submits a new quote for approval in HubSpot Quotes
The system identifies approvers based on the specific concessions asked for and the rep's reporting chain
An email approval request gets sent to the designated approvers
The approver reviews the quote details and takes action—approve, reject, or request changes—in Slack
If approved, the quote is marked as such in HubSpot, and the rep is free to send it
If concessions aren't approved, the quote is marked rejected, and reps can resubmit
If quotes aren't approved in 24 hours, stakeholders are tagged in the thread as a reminder

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new quote submission events from HubSpot Quotes and forwards them into the system
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted quotes with their details, concessions, rep identity, and current approval status
objects.behavior: When a new quote submission event arrives, forward the full quote payload (quote ID, rep ID, concessions requested, deal details, discount amounts) to the approval-policy object. When instructed to mark a quote as approved or rejected, update the quote status in HubSpot accordingly.

peers.relationship: Forward full quote payload when a new quote submission event arrives
Send approval or rejection status updates to record in HubSpot

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot Quotes quote submitted

objects.role: Read service that retrieves employee reporting chain and approver information from the organization directory
objects.state_description: No internal state — retrieves data on demand via tool call
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about a sales rep's reporting chain, manager hierarchy, and designated approvers. When answering queries, call the `org_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that determines the correct approvers for a quote based on concessions and the rep's reporting chain, then orchestrates the approval lifecycle
objects.state_description: Tracks active approval requests keyed by quote ID, including approvers assigned, approval status, submission timestamp, reminder sent flag, and any change requests
objects.behavior: When a new quote payload arrives, query org-directory for the rep's reporting chain and determine which approvers are required based on concession types and amounts (e.g., discounts above threshold require manager + finance, non-standard terms require legal). Once approvers are identified, send an email approval request to email-notifications and post a structured approval request to deal-approvals-slack. Track the 24-hour approval deadline for each active quote. If no approval action is received within 24 hours, tag relevant stakeholders in the existing Slack thread as a reminder via deal-approvals-slack. When an approval action (approve, reject, or request-changes) arrives from deal-approvals-slack: if approved, send approval confirmation to hubspot-quotes-writer and notify the rep via email-notifications; if rejected, send rejection status to hubspot-quotes-writer and notify the rep via email-notifications with rejection reason; if changes requested, notify the rep via email-notifications with the requested changes and mark the quote as pending resubmission.

peers.relationship: Query for rep's reporting chain and approver hierarchy when a new quote arrives
Send approval request emails to designated approvers and outcome notifications to the sales rep
Post approval request messages and send stakeholder reminder tags when the 24-hour deadline is reached
Send approved or rejected status updates for the quote record in HubSpot

objects.role: Write service that sends outbound emails to approvers and sales reps
objects.state_description: Audit log of all emails sent, including recipient, subject, quote ID, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an email send instruction arrives (approval request or outcome notification), dispatch the email to the specified recipient with the provided subject and body content. Record each sent email in the audit log.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the #deal-approvals Slack channel — receives outbound approval request posts and inbound approver action events (approve, reject, request-changes)
objects.state_description: Tracks posted approval threads keyed by quote ID, including thread timestamps for reply threading and reminder status
objects.behavior: When instructed to post an approval request, publish a structured message to the #deal-approvals channel with quote details and action prompts, recording the thread timestamp. When instructed to tag stakeholders as a reminder, post a follow-up in the existing thread tagging the relevant users. When an approver action event (approve, reject, or request-changes) arrives from the Slack channel, forward it along with the quote ID and any comments to approval-policy via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward approver action events (approve, reject, request-changes) with quote ID and comments when received from the Slack channel

event_sources: webhook: Slack action event in #deal-approvals channel

objects.role: Write service that updates quote approval status records in HubSpot
objects.state_description: Audit log of all status updates written, including quote ID, new status, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a status update instruction arrives (approved or rejected), write the new status to the corresponding HubSpot quote record. Record each update in the audit log.

steps.text: A new quote has been submitted in HubSpot Quotes. Quote ID: Q-2041. Sales rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Deal: Acme Corp renewal, $120,000 ARR. Concessions requested: 18% discount (above 15% threshold), extended payment terms (net-60), and one free onboarding session. Submitted for approval.

expect.action: Approval request emails sent to Marcus Webb's manager (Director-level approver) and VP of Sales, and a structured approval request message posted to the #deal-approvals Slack channel with quote details for Q-2041 (Acme Corp, $120,000 ARR, 18% discount, net-60 terms, free onboarding session) and action prompts for approve, reject, or request-changes.
expect.reason: hubspot-quotes forwards the quote payload to approval-policy, which queries org-directory for Marcus Webb's reporting chain, determines that an 18% discount exceeds the 15% threshold requiring elevated approval, then instructs email-notifications to send approval request emails and deal-approvals-slack to post the approval request message.

steps.text: Approver action received in the #deal-approvals Slack channel. Thread timestamp: 1718200000.123456. Quote ID: Q-2041. Action taken: approve. Approver: Sandra Okonkwo, VP of Sales.

expect.action: HubSpot quote record Q-2041 updated to 'approved' status, and a notification email sent to Marcus Webb, Account Executive, informing him that quote Q-2041 for Acme Corp has been approved and he is free to send it.
expect.reason: deal-approvals-slack forwards the approver action to approval-policy, which checks that all required approvers have approved (Director-level and VP), records the approval, then instructs hubspot-quotes-writer to mark Q-2041 as approved and email-notifications to notify Marcus Webb of the outcome.

steps.text: Scheduled 24-hour deadline check triggered for pending quote approvals. Current time: 2024-06-13T10:00:00Z. Quote Q-2089 submitted by Jordan Tate, Account Executive, for Brightline Inc, $85,000 ARR, 12% discount, net-30 terms. Quote submitted at 2024-06-12T09:45:00Z. Approval still pending — no response from approvers. Required approvers: Diana Reyes, Sales Director. Slack thread timestamp: 1718150000.654321.

name: IT Help Desk with FAQ Loop
raw_steps: An employee posts a question in a designated Slack channel
AI automatically searches your FAQ knowledge base (stored in a Zapier table) for relevant answers
If AI finds a suitable answer, an AI chatbot replies to the employee directly in Slack
If AI can't find a good answer—or if the issue requires human attention—the request gets escalated to an IT team member
Employees can mark the urgency of their requests using an emoji that corresponds to predefined priority options
The system automatically creates tickets in Jira or ClickUp and sends status updates back to Slack
After each ticket gets closed out, the system summarizes the Slack thread and adds it to your FAQ database
Next time someone has the same question, AI will have the info to respond automatically

objects.role: Dual-role Slack channel object that receives employee questions and posts replies and status updates in #it-helpdesk
objects.state_description: Tracks inbound employee messages including text, author, timestamp, and any priority emoji attached
objects.behavior: When an employee posts a message in #it-helpdesk, extract the message text, author, timestamp, and priority emoji (🔴 Critical, 🟡 High, 🟢 Low) and forward to helpdesk-triage. When a reply or status update is received from a peer, post it to the #it-helpdesk channel thread associated with the original message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee question, author, timestamp, and parsed priority when a new message arrives

event_sources: slack: message posted in #it-helpdesk

objects.role: Read service representing the IT FAQ knowledge base stored in Zapier Tables, answering semantic queries with relevant FAQ entries
objects.state_description: No local state; retrieves data on demand from Zapier Tables
objects.behavior: Accepts queries containing a question or keywords and returns the most relevant FAQ entries including question, answer, and entry ID. When answering queries, call the `faq_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies incoming IT questions, determines priority, decides whether to auto-answer or escalate, and orchestrates ticket creation and FAQ updates
objects.state_description: Tracks active requests with question text, author, priority level, FAQ lookup result, escalation status, and associated Jira ticket ID
objects.behavior: When a new employee question arrives: parse the priority from the emoji (🔴=Critical, 🟡=High, 🟢=Low; default to Low if absent). Query faq-knowledge-base with the question text. If a suitable FAQ answer is found and the issue does not require human attention, send the answer reply to it-helpdesk-channel and send a ticket creation request to jira-tickets with priority and auto-resolved status. If no suitable answer is found or the issue requires human attention (e.g., Critical priority, ambiguous answer, hardware failure keywords), send an escalation request to it-support-specialist and send a ticket creation request to jira-tickets with priority and escalation flag. When a ticket-closed event arrives from jira-tickets containing the Jira ticket ID and Slack thread: summarize the thread conversation, then send the summary and original question to faq-knowledge-base-writer and send a closure status update to it-helpdesk-channel.

peers.relationship: Query with employee question text to retrieve relevant FAQ answers
Send auto-answer reply or status update to post in the Slack thread
Send escalation request with question, author, priority, and Jira ticket ID when human attention is needed
Send ticket creation request with question, author, priority, escalation flag, and Slack thread reference
Send summarized thread and resolved question/answer pair to add to FAQ after ticket closure

objects.role: Write service representing the IT Support Specialist notification channel that receives escalated requests requiring human attention
objects.state_description: Audit log of escalated requests including question, author, priority, Jira ticket ID, and escalation timestamp
objects.behavior: Record each escalation request received. Notify the IT Support Specialist via their designated channel (email, Slack DM, or ticketing notification) with the question details, priority, and Jira ticket link.

objects.role: Dual-role write service that creates and tracks Jira tickets and forwards ticket-closed events back to helpdesk-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of created tickets with ticket ID, question summary, author, priority, status, and Slack thread reference
objects.behavior: When a ticket creation request arrives, record it and create the Jira ticket. When a ticket-closed event arrives from Jira (ticket status changes to Done/Closed), forward the ticket ID, original question, author, and associated Slack thread reference to helpdesk-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward ticket-closed event with ticket ID, question, and Slack thread reference when a Jira ticket is resolved

event_sources: jira: ticket status changed to closed/done

objects.role: Write service that appends new summarized Q&A entries to the IT FAQ knowledge base in Zapier Tables
objects.state_description: Audit log of FAQ entries written including question, answer summary, source ticket ID, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a new FAQ entry arrives containing a question and summarized answer, record it and write the entry to the Zapier Tables FAQ knowledge base so it is available for future queries.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Rivera posts a message in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel: '🔴 My laptop won't connect to the VPN and I have a client call in 30 minutes. I've tried restarting and reinstalling the client but it still fails with error code 800.' Thread timestamp: 1718200000.000100

expect.action: Reply posted in #it-helpdesk to Marcus Rivera's thread (ts: 1718200000.000100) with an FAQ-sourced answer addressing VPN error code 800, or a message confirming escalation to the IT Support Specialist with Critical priority and a Jira ticket reference
expect.reason: helpdesk-triage queries faq-knowledge-base with the question; if a suitable answer is found it-helpdesk-channel posts the reply, otherwise it-support-specialist is notified and jira-tickets creates a Critical-priority ticket with a status update sent back to #it-helpdesk

steps.text: Jira ticket IT-4821 (originally created for Marcus Rivera's VPN connectivity issue, thread ts: 1718200000.000100, priority: Critical) has been updated to status 'Done' by IT Support Specialist Jordan Lee.

expect.action: New FAQ entry written to the Zapier Tables IT FAQ knowledge base containing the question about VPN error code 800 and a summarized resolution answer derived from the #it-helpdesk thread for ticket IT-4821, and a status update posted in #it-helpdesk to Marcus Rivera's thread (ts: 1718200000.000100) confirming the ticket is closed
expect.reason: jira-tickets forwards the ticket-closed event to helpdesk-triage, which summarizes the thread and sends it to faq-knowledge-base-writer for storage and posts a closure update via it-helpdesk-channel

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from faq-knowledge-base data. No local state; retrieves data on demand from Zapier Tables
mock_tools.response_template: {"knowledge_base_articles": [{"article_id": "KB-10447", "title": "VPN Connection Failure - Error Code 800 Troubleshooting Guide", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["vpn", "error-800", "remote-access", "connectivity", "cisco-anyconnect", "network"], "problem_description": "Employee is unable to connect to the corporate VPN and receives error code 800. This error typically indicates a failed or dropped connection attempt, often caused by firewall rules blocking the VPN tunnel, misconfigured client settings, corrupted client installation, or ISP-level interference with VPN protocols. Navigate to Help > About in the VPN client and confirm the version matches the latest approved release listed in the IT software catalog."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Temporarily disable any third-party firewall or antivirus software and attempt the VPN connection again. Re-enable security software immediately after testing."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Ensure that UDP port 500, UDP port 4500, and TCP port 443 are not blocked by the local network or ISP. Ask the employee to test from a different network such as a mobile hotspot to isolate ISP-level blocking."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Perform a clean reinstall of the VPN client: fully uninstall the existing client, delete residual config files under %AppData% or ~/Library/Application Support, restart the machine, then install the latest client package from the IT software portal."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Flush DNS and reset network stack. On Windows run: ipconfig /flushdns, netsh int ip reset, netsh winsock reset. On macOS run: sudo dscacheutil -flushcache; sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder. mock_tools.description: Write or notify helpdesk-triage.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to helpdesk-triage.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from faq knowledge base data. Accepts queries containing a question or keywords and returns the most relevant FAQ entries including question, answer, and entry ID. When answering queries, call the `faq_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.
mock_tools.response_template:
{"faq_knowledge_base_data": {"entries": [{"entry_id": "KB-10447", "question": "Why does my VPN connection fail with error code 800 and how do I fix it?", "answer": "Error code 800 indicates that the VPN tunnel could not be established, typically due to a blocked port, misconfigured client, or server-side connectivity issue. Follow these steps to resolve it:

1. Confirm your internet connection is active and stable by visiting a public website.
2. Restart your VPN client completely (close from system tray, relaunch).
3. Check that UDP port 500 and 4500 are not blocked by your firewall or router — temporarily disable your local firewall to test.
4. Uninstall the VPN client, reboot your machine, then reinstall the latest version from the IT portal (https://itportal.internal/vpn).
5. Try switching VPN gateway servers — select a different region or server endpoint in the client settings.
6. If on a corporate network or hotel/office Wi-Fi, the network may block VPN protocols. Try using a mobile hotspot instead.
7. Flush your DNS cache: open Command Prompt as administrator and run `ipconfig /flushdns`.
8. If the issue persists after all steps, submit a ticket to the IT helpdesk via #it-helpdesk on Slack or email helpdesk@company.com and include your error code and device details.", "tags": ["vpn", "error code 800", "connectivity", "remote access", "network", "vpn client", "tunnel failure"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "affected_users_example": "Marcus Rivera", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "thread_timestamp_reference": "1718200000.000100", "last_updated": "2024-06-12", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10448", "question": "What should I do if I need urgent IT support before an important meeting or client call?", "answer": "If you have a time-sensitive IT issue before a meeting or client call, take the following steps immediately:

1. Post a message in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel with 🔴 at the start of your message to flag urgency. Include your name, the issue description, any error codes, and your deadline.
2. Tag @it-support in your Slack message to alert the on-call specialist.
3. An IT Support Specialist will triage your ticket and respond within 10 minutes for critical issues.
4. If Slack is unavailable, call the IT emergency line at ext. 5555.
5. As a temporary workaround for VPN issues, consider using a mobile hotspot or asking a colleague to share relevant files via cloud storage (SharePoint or Google Drive) for the duration of your call.
6. A Jira ticket will be automatically created or manually raised by the IT team with priority set to Critical to track resolution.
7. Once resolved, the ticket status will be updated to 'Done' and you will receive a Slack notification in the original thread.", "tags": ["urgent support", "it helpdesk", "client call", "meeting", "escalation", "critical priority", "slack helpdesk"], "category": "IT Support Process", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "affected_users_example": "Marcus Rivera", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "thread_timestamp_reference": "1718200000.000100", "last_updated": "2024-06-12", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10391", "question": "How are IT helpdesk Jira tickets prioritized and what does each status mean?", "answer": "IT helpdesk tickets are created in Jira under the IT project and follow a standard priority and status workflow:

Priority Levels:
- Critical: System down or issue blocking a business-critical activity (e.g., client call, production outage). Target response: 10 minutes. Example: IT-4821.
- High: Major functionality impaired but workaround exists. Target response: 1 hour.
- Medium: Partial functionality loss, non-urgent. Target response: 4 hours.
- Low: Cosmetic or minor issues. Target response: 1 business day.

Ticket Statuses:
- Open: Ticket received, awaiting assignment.
- In Progress: An IT specialist is actively working on the issue.
- Pending User: Awaiting information or action from the requester.
- Done: Issue resolved and verified. The ticket is closed.

Tickets are updated in real time by the assigned IT Support Specialist. When a ticket moves to 'Done', the requester receives an automated Slack notification in the original helpdesk thread. All tickets can be viewed at https://jira.internal/projects/IT.", "tags": ["jira", "ticket status", "priority", "it workflow", "done", "critical", "helpdesk process"], "category": "IT Support Process", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "last_updated": "2024-06-10", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10312", "question": "How do I reinstall the corporate VPN client on Windows?", "answer": "To reinstall the corporate VPN client on a Windows laptop:

1. Open Control Panel > Programs > Uninstall a Program.
2. Locate the VPN client (e.g., Cisco AnyConnect, GlobalProtect, or your company's branded client) and click Uninstall. Follow the prompts.
3. Restart your computer after uninstallation completes.
4. Navigate to the IT portal at https://itportal.internal/vpn and download the latest installer for your operating system.
5. Run the installer as Administrator (right-click > Run as administrator).
6. Follow the installation wizard. When prompted, enter the VPN server address provided by IT: vpn.company.com.
7. Reboot your machine once installation is complete.
8. Launch the VPN client and attempt to connect using your corporate credentials (same as your Windows login).
9. If you still encounter error code 800 or other errors after reinstalling, contact the IT helpdesk via #it-helpdesk on Slack.", "tags": ["vpn", "reinstall", "vpn client", "windows", "cisco anyconnect", "globalprotect", "installation"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "last_updated": "2024-05-28", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10289", "question": "What information should I include when reporting an IT issue in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel?", "answer": "To help IT Support Specialists resolve your issue as quickly as possible, include the following information when posting in #it-helpdesk:

1. Urgency indicator: Use 🔴 for critical (blocking work), 🟡 for moderate, 🟢 for low priority.
2. Your full name and department.
3. Device type and operating system (e.g., Dell Latitude, Windows 11).
4. A clear description of the problem.
5. Any error codes or error messages you see (e.g., error code 800).
6. Steps you have already tried (e.g., restarted device, reinstalled client).
7. Your deadline or urgency context (e.g., 'client call in 30 minutes').
8. Screenshots or screen recordings if applicable (use Slack's file attachment feature).

Example post: '🔴 My laptop won't connect to the VPN and I have a client call in 30 minutes. I've tried restarting and reinstalling the client but it still fails with error code 800.'

The IT team will create a Jira ticket on your behalf and keep you updated in the thread.", "tags": ["slack", "it-helpdesk", "reporting", "issue template", "vpn", "error reporting", "best practices"], "category": "IT Support Process", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "last_updated": "2024-06-01", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10355", "question": "Why does VPN fail after a laptop restart and what are common causes?", "answer": "If your VPN fails to connect even after restarting your laptop, the following are common causes and resolutions:

1. Corrupted VPN client profile: Delete saved VPN profiles in the client settings and re-add the server address (vpn.company.com).
2. Network adapter conflict: Go to Device Manager > Network Adapters, right-click the VPN virtual adapter, and select 'Disable', then 'Enable'.
3. Windows update conflict: A recent Windows update may have altered network settings. Check Windows Update history and roll back if necessary, or contact IT.
4. Antivirus/firewall interference: Temporarily disable third-party antivirus software and attempt to reconnect. Re-enable immediately after testing.
5. Expired VPN certificate: Your device certificate may have expired. Contact IT to reissue — this cannot be self-resolved.
6. DNS resolution failure: Run `ipconfig /flushdns` and `ipconfig /registerdns` from an elevated Command Prompt.
7. Split tunneling misconfiguration: IT may need to adjust your VPN profile's split tunneling settings remotely.

If none of these resolve the issue, escalate via #it-helpdesk on Slack and reference your error code for faster triage.", "tags": ["vpn", "restart", "connection failure", "network adapter", "dns", "antivirus", "certificate", "error code 800"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "last_updated": "2024-05-15", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "status": "Published"}], "metadata": {"total_entries": 6, "knowledge_base_name": "IT Helpdesk FAQ Knowledge Base", "description": "Searchable repository of frequently asked questions, troubleshooting guides, and IT support process articles maintained by the IT Support team.", "primary_contact": "Jordan Lee", "contact_channel": "#it-helpdesk", "last_full_review": "2024-06-12"}}}

name: AI Content Idea Generator
raw_steps: Trigger when a new idea is added to the table.
Retrieve the submitted idea from the table.
Send the idea to ChatGPT to generate additional and related ideas.
Store the AI-generated ideas back into the table or connected system.
Publish the automation workflow.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Airtable Content Ideas table and fires when a new idea record is added.
objects.state_description: Tracks the most recently detected new idea record to avoid duplicate triggers.
objects.behavior: When a new record is added to the Airtable Content Ideas table, forward the idea details to the idea-enrichment peer. Do not wait for confirmation before completing the trigger cycle.

peers.relationship: Forward the new idea record details when a new entry is detected in the Content Ideas table.

event_sources: external system: Airtable Content Ideas table record created

objects.role: Business logic object that receives a submitted content idea, sends it to ChatGPT for expansion, and coordinates storage of the generated ideas.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current idea being processed, the prompt sent to ChatGPT, and the list of AI-generated ideas returned.
objects.behavior: When a new idea is received, construct a prompt asking ChatGPT to generate additional and related content ideas based on the submitted idea. Use the generate-ideas skill to call the ChatGPT API with the constructed prompt. When AI-generated ideas are returned, send them along with the original idea record reference to the content-ideas-store peer for persistence.

peers.relationship: Send the AI-generated ideas and original record reference when ChatGPT returns expanded ideas.

objects.role: Write service object that persists AI-generated ideas back into the Airtable Content Ideas table.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all AI-generated idea batches written, including source record ID, generated ideas, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When AI-generated ideas are received, write each idea as a new record (or update the originating record) in the Airtable Content Ideas table. Record the source idea reference and timestamp for each write operation.

steps.text: A new record has been added to the Airtable Content Ideas table. The submitted idea is: 'How to use AI tools to streamline social media content creation for small businesses'.

expect.action: AI-generated ideas related to 'How to use AI tools to streamline social media content creation for small businesses' are written as new records into the Airtable Content Ideas table, each with a reference to the source idea and a timestamp.
expect.reason: content-ideas-trigger forwards the idea to idea-enrichment, which calls ChatGPT to generate related ideas, then coordinates with content-ideas-store to persist each generated idea back into the Airtable Content Ideas table.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify content-ideas-store.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to content-ideas-store.

name: Slack-ClickUp Support Automation
raw_steps: User submits support request through Slack using a simple command or form
AI chatbot analyzes the request and checks against your knowledge base for instant solutions
Common issues get resolved immediately with step-by-step instructions sent directly to the user
Complex or unrecognized requests automatically create tickets in ClickUp with proper categorization
System assigns priority levels and routes tickets to appropriate team members based on expertise and workload
Team members receive Slack notifications with all relevant context and can update status directly
Automated status updates keep users informed throughout the resolution process
Resolved tickets automatically update the knowledge base with new solutions for future reference
System generates reports on common issues, resolution times, and team performance metrics

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives /support slash command events from Slack users and forwards them into the automation pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming slash command payloads including user ID, channel, and request text.
objects.behavior: When a /support slash command event arrives, forward the full payload (user ID, channel, request text) to support-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward slash command payload when a /support command is received

event_sources: webhook: Slack /support slash command

objects.role: Dual-role service object that receives Slack interactive actions (ticket status updates from #support-queue) and also delivers outbound Slack messages and notifications to users and channels.
objects.state_description: Tracks outbound messages sent to users and channels, and inbound interactive action events from team members updating ticket status.
objects.behavior: When an interactive action event arrives from a team member updating ticket status directly from Slack, forward the action payload (ticket ID, new status, actor) to support-triage via peer message. When instructed to send a direct message or channel notification, post the message to the specified Slack user or channel.

peers.relationship: Forward ticket status update actions from team members in Slack

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action (ticket status update from #support-queue)

objects.role: Read service representing the IT Help Center knowledge base, providing instant solutions and known issue resolutions on demand.
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves knowledge base articles and solutions on demand via tool call.
objects.behavior: When answering queries about known issues, solutions, or past resolutions, call the `helpdesk_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies support requests, resolves common issues via the knowledge base, creates and routes ClickUp tickets, assigns priorities and teams, and triggers all downstream notifications.
objects.state_description: Tracks active support requests with their classification, priority, assigned team, ClickUp ticket ID, current status (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Resolved), and requesting user.
objects.behavior: When a support request arrives from slack-commands: query helpdesk-knowledge-base for a matching solution. If a known solution is found, send step-by-step resolution instructions to the requesting user via slack-ticket-actions and mark the request resolved; also send the solution to helpdesk-knowledge-base-updater for knowledge base reinforcement. If no known solution is found, classify the request into a category (Bug, Access Request, How-To) and assign a priority (Urgent, High, Normal, Low) based on request content and urgency signals. Determine the appropriate team (IT Support, Software Team, Infrastructure Team) based on category and query clickup-workload for current team workload to balance assignment. Create a ClickUp ticket via clickup-tickets with category tags, priority, assigned team, and full request context. Simultaneously notify #support-queue channel via slack-ticket-actions with all relevant ticket context. When a ticket status update action arrives from slack-ticket-actions: update internal ticket state to the new status. If status is In Progress, Pending Review, or Resolved, send a status update notification to the requesting user via slack-ticket-actions. If status is Resolved, send the resolution details to helpdesk-knowledge-base-updater and update the ticket in clickup-tickets. On a weekly schedule, send aggregated ticket data (common issues, resolution times, team performance) to clickup-reports.

peers.relationship: Query for known solutions when a new support request arrives
Query current team workload to inform ticket routing and assignment
Create new tickets with category, priority, team assignment, and context; update ticket status when resolved
Send resolution instructions to users, status update notifications, and #support-queue channel notifications
Send new solutions when a request is resolved instantly or a ticket is closed as resolved
Send weekly aggregated metrics for report generation

objects.role: Read service representing ClickUp team workload data, providing current open ticket counts and capacity per team on demand.
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves team workload data on demand via tool call.
objects.behavior: When answering queries about current team workload or ticket queue sizes, call the `clickup_workload_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates and updates support tickets in ClickUp under the Customer Support list.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all ticket creation and update operations including ticket ID, category tags, priority, assigned team, status, and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a ticket creation or update message is received, record the operation and write the ticket data to ClickUp.

objects.role: Write service that appends new solutions and resolutions to the IT Help Center knowledge base for future reference.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all knowledge base update operations including the issue description, solution text, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new solution or resolution is received, record the update and write the new entry to the IT Help Center knowledge base.

objects.role: Write service that generates and stores weekly summary reports in ClickUp covering common issues, resolution times, and team performance metrics.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all weekly report generation operations including report date, metrics payload, and ClickUp document reference.
objects.behavior: When weekly aggregated metrics are received, record the data and generate a structured weekly report in ClickUp.

steps.text: User Maya Chen submits /support slash command in Slack with the message: 'I cannot access the company VPN. I get an error saying authentication failed every time I try to connect. This started happening after the IT team pushed an update yesterday.' Channel: #general, User ID: U04MAYA123

expect.action: A ClickUp ticket is created in the Customer Support list for Maya Chen's VPN authentication issue, tagged with 'Access Request', assigned to the Infrastructure Team with a priority level, and a Slack notification is sent to #support-queue with the ticket details and Maya Chen's request context. A direct Slack message is sent to Maya Chen confirming her ticket was created.
expect.reason: support-triage queries helpdesk-knowledge-base and finds no instant solution for this specific VPN authentication failure, so it creates a ClickUp ticket via clickup-tickets, checks clickup-workload to route to Infrastructure Team, and triggers slack-ticket-actions to notify #support-queue and the requesting user

steps.text: Team member Jordan Lee updates ticket status for Maya Chen's VPN access ticket (Ticket ID: TICK-2041) to 'In Progress' via the interactive Slack message in #support-queue.

expect.action: ClickUp ticket TICK-2041 is updated to 'In Progress' status, and a Slack message is sent to Maya Chen notifying her that her ticket has moved to In Progress.
expect.reason: slack-ticket-actions forwards the interactive action payload to support-triage, which updates the ticket via clickup-tickets and sends a status update notification to the requesting user via slack-ticket-actions

name: Employee Attrition Risk Predictor
raw_steps: The system automatically aggregates critical employee information (joins, team changes, engagement scores, exits) into a centralized Zapier Table
On a scheduled basis (Weekly), the system analyzes messages from team Slack channels using AI-powered sentiment analysis
Each week, the system runs a comprehensive scan comparing engagement scores, team attrition history, and Slack sentiment patterns
It applies a weighted algorithm to identify employees showing multiple risk indicators
When risk thresholds are crossed, the system sends detailed alert messages to HR and managers
The system maintains historical data on sentiment and trends to spot patterns across departments

objects.role: Zapier Table service that stores and provides centralized employee information including joins, team changes, engagement scores, and exits
objects.state_description: Tracks all employee records with fields for join date, team history, engagement scores, exit status, and attrition risk scores
objects.behavior: Store incoming employee data updates (joins, team changes, engagement scores, exits) and maintain historical records. When answering queries about employee records, engagement scores, or attrition history, call the `employee_data_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward employee data updates (joins, team changes, engagement score changes, exits) to trigger risk analysis

event_sources: zapier: employee join event
zapier: team change event
zapier: engagement score update
zapier: employee exit event

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures messages from #team-general, #engineering, and #product Slack channels every Monday for sentiment analysis
objects.state_description: Tracks the latest batch of channel messages collected each Monday, including channel name, message content, author, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Every Monday, collect all messages from #team-general, #engineering, and #product Slack channels. Bundle messages by channel and forward them to the sentiment-analyzer for processing. When a High Risk alert needs to be sent to an HR Business Partner or manager via Slack, deliver the message to the specified Slack user or channel.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly batched messages from each Slack channel every Monday for sentiment analysis
Receive and deliver High Risk alert Slack messages to HR Business Partners and direct managers

event_sources: schedule: every Monday
slack: message posted in #team-general
slack: message posted in #engineering
slack: message posted in #product

objects.role: Business logic object that applies AI-powered sentiment analysis to Slack channel messages and produces sentiment scores per team and employee
objects.state_description: Maintains historical sentiment scores by channel, team, and employee over time, tracking trends week over week
objects.behavior: When a batch of Slack channel messages arrives, analyze each message for sentiment (positive, neutral, negative) using NLP. Aggregate sentiment scores by team (Engineering, Product, Customer Success) and by individual employee where identifiable. Detect sentiment trends by comparing current scores against historical baselines. When sentiment scores and trends are computed, forward results to attrition-risk-engine.

peers.relationship: Send computed sentiment scores and trend analysis by team and employee after each weekly analysis run

objects.role: Core business logic object that applies a weighted risk algorithm to engagement scores, attrition history, and sentiment data to classify each employee's attrition risk
objects.state_description: Tracks current and historical risk scores per employee, weighted indicator values (engagement score delta, sentiment trend, team attrition rate), and risk classification (High Risk, Moderate Risk, Low Risk) with timestamps
objects.behavior: When employee data updates, sentiment scores, or scheduled weekly scan triggers arrive, retrieve current employee records from employee-data-store and team attrition history from employee-data-store. Apply weighted algorithm: combine engagement score trajectory (weight: 40%), Slack sentiment trend (weight: 35%), and team attrition history rate (weight: 25%) to compute a composite risk score for each employee in Engineering, Product, and Customer Success teams. Classify each employee as High Risk (score >= 75), Moderate Risk (score 40-74), or Low Risk (score < 40). Store updated risk scores and history. When any employee reaches High Risk classification, simultaneously send alert details to hr-notifications and manager-notifications. Maintain longitudinal trend data across departments for pattern detection.

peers.relationship: Query for current employee engagement scores, team attrition history, and employee records during weekly scan
Send High Risk alert with employee details, risk indicators, and scores when an employee crosses the High Risk threshold
Send High Risk alert with employee details, risk indicators, and scores to the employee's direct manager when High Risk threshold is crossed

objects.role: Write service that delivers High Risk attrition alerts to HR Business Partners via both Slack and email
objects.state_description: Audit log of all HR alert messages sent, including employee ID, risk score, timestamp, delivery channel (Slack and email), and recipient HR Business Partner
objects.behavior: When a High Risk alert arrives, send the detailed alert message to the assigned HR Business Partner via Slack direct message and email simultaneously. Record each notification in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that delivers High Risk attrition alerts to the employee's direct manager via both Slack and email
objects.state_description: Audit log of all manager alert messages sent, including employee ID, risk score, timestamp, delivery channel (Slack and email), and recipient manager
objects.behavior: When a High Risk alert arrives, look up the employee's direct manager and send the detailed alert message to that manager via Slack direct message and email simultaneously. Record each notification in the audit log.
