name: AI Incident Postmortem Review & Coaching
raw_steps: Capture closed incidents via Incident.io webhook, API polling, or manual form submission
When a new retrospective document is detected, AI analyzes the content
AI evaluates the review against best practices (customer impact, root cause, action items) and generates feedback
Smart Routing: If a reviewer email is present, send feedback via Slack DM
Smart Routing: If no reviewer email, post feedback to the designated team Slack channel
Weekly Cycle: Compile analytics and create TLDR summaries of all retrospectives
Post a weekly insights digest to Slack with detailed breakdowns in the thread

objects.role: Entry-point service that captures closed incidents via Incident.io webhook, API polling, or manual form submission and forwards them for processing
objects.state_description: Records received incident payloads with timestamps and source type (webhook, poll, manual)
objects.behavior: When a closed incident event arrives via webhook, API poll, or manual form submission, forward the full incident payload to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward closed incident payload when a new incident is captured

event_sources: webhook: incident.closed from Incident.io
external system: Incident.io API poll
manual form submission: incident details

objects.role: Entry-point service that detects new or updated retrospective documents in Confluence and forwards them for AI analysis
objects.state_description: Tracks document IDs and last-seen timestamps of retrospective pages detected in Confluence
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document is detected in Confluence, forward the document content and metadata to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document content and metadata when a new document is detected

event_sources: external system: Confluence page created or updated in retrospectives space

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates postmortem documents against best practices, generates structured feedback, and routes it to the appropriate Slack destination
objects.state_description: Tracks analyzed retrospectives with document ID, reviewer email (if present), generated feedback, quality scores, and routing decision per incident
objects.behavior: When a closed incident payload arrives from incident-intake, store it and await or correlate the associated Confluence retrospective document. When a retrospective document arrives from confluence-watcher, analyze the content against best practices: check for clear customer impact description, thorough root cause analysis, and concrete actionable items. Generate structured coaching feedback with scores and recommendations. If a reviewer email is present in the document or incident payload, send the feedback via Slack DM to that reviewer by forwarding to slack-dm. If no reviewer email is present, send the feedback to the #incident-postmortems channel by forwarding to slack-channel. Record all analyzed retrospectives with metadata for weekly analytics. On the weekly cycle trigger from weekly-scheduler, compile analytics across all retrospectives from the past seven days: identify patterns, common failure modes, action item completion trends, and generate a TLDR summary per retrospective plus an overall insights digest. Send the weekly digest and thread breakdowns to slack-channel.

peers.relationship: Send personalized coaching feedback via DM when a reviewer email is identified
Send feedback to #incident-postmortems when no reviewer email is present, and post weekly insights digest with thread breakdowns
Write analyzed retrospective record with metadata, scores, and feedback after each analysis

objects.role: Entry-point service that fires a weekly trigger to initiate the analytics and digest compilation cycle
objects.state_description: Tracks last trigger timestamp and next scheduled run time
objects.behavior: When the weekly schedule fires (every 7 days), forward the trigger with the date range covering the past seven days to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly cycle trigger with past-seven-day date range

event_sources: scheduled trigger: weekly cron job

objects.role: Write service that persists analyzed retrospective records including metadata, quality scores, feedback, and routing decisions for later retrieval and analytics
objects.state_description: Stores retrospective analysis records keyed by document ID, including incident ID, reviewer email, quality scores, feedback text, action items, and analysis timestamp
objects.behavior: When a retrospective analysis record is received, persist it to the store. Accumulate records over time as the audit trail for weekly analytics queries.

objects.role: Write service that delivers Slack direct messages containing postmortem coaching feedback to individual incident reviewers
objects.state_description: Records sent DMs with recipient, message content, and delivery timestamp
objects.behavior: When a feedback message and reviewer identifier are received, send a Slack DM to that reviewer with the coaching feedback content.

objects.role: Write service that posts messages to the #incident-postmortems Slack channel including routed feedback and weekly insights digests with thread breakdowns
objects.state_description: Records posted messages with content, thread structure, and post timestamps
objects.behavior: When a feedback message is received for channel routing, post it to #incident-postmortems. When a weekly digest is received, post the top-level digest to #incident-postmortems and post each retrospective TLDR breakdown as a reply in the thread.

steps.text: Incident.io reports a closed incident: Incident INC-4821, title 'Database connection pool exhaustion causing API timeouts', severity P1, affected service 'payments-api', duration 3h 42m, customer impact 'Checkout failures for approximately 18% of users during peak traffic', incident commander Sarah Okonkwo (sarah.okonkwo@company.com), resolved at 2024-01-15T14:32:00Z.

expect.action: Incident payload for INC-4821 forwarded to postmortem-analysis and stored, awaiting correlation with the associated Confluence retrospective document.
expect.reason: incident-intake forwards closed incident payloads to its postmortem-analysis peer upon receiving a closed incident event

steps.text: A new retrospective page has been created in the Confluence retrospectives space: page titled 'INC-4821 Postmortem - Database Connection Pool Exhaustion', authored by Sarah Okonkwo, content includes: customer impact summary (18% checkout failure rate during peak traffic), root cause analysis (connection pool limit set too low after recent infrastructure scaling, combined with missing alerting on pool saturation), and action items (1. Increase connection pool limit and add auto-scaling, 2. Add pool saturation alert at 80% threshold, 3. Runbook update for DB connection issues), page URL: https://company.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/4821-postmortem, reviewer email: sarah.okonkwo@company.com.

expect.action: Coaching feedback message sent via Slack DM to Sarah Okonkwo (sarah.okonkwo@company.com) containing structured evaluation of the INC-4821 postmortem against best practices, and the retrospective analysis record persisted to analytics-store.
expect.reason: postmortem-analysis evaluates the retrospective against best practices and routes feedback via slack-dm when a reviewer email is present, and stores the record in analytics-store

steps.text: Weekly schedule trigger fires covering the period 2024-01-09T00:00:00Z to 2024-01-15T23:59:59Z.

name: Brand Media Monitoring & Sentiment Triage
raw_steps: The system continuously monitors a Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning your brand or specified keywords
When a new mention is detected, AI analyzes the content to extract key information like headlines, summaries, quotes, and sentiment
All extracted data is automatically stored in a Zapier Table, creating a searchable historical database
The system routes mentions to Slack and triages according to sentiment
Positive mentions are immediately shared, while negative mentions trigger an approval workflow for proper handling
Every week, the AI generates a comprehensive digest summarizing trends and highlighting key articles, then posts it to Slack
Team members can use a Zapier Chatbot to search the database with natural language questions
The system includes error handling that alerts administrators if any part of the workflow fails

objects.role: Entry-point service that monitors the Google Alerts-based RSS feed for new articles mentioning Acme Corporation and related keywords
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen feed item timestamp and URL to detect new mentions
objects.behavior: Poll the RSS feed continuously. When a new article mentioning Acme Corporation, AcmeCorp, Acme products, or Acme CEO is detected, forward the raw article data (URL, headline, content, publication timestamp) to the mention-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward raw article data when a new mention is detected in the feed

event_sources: external system: Google Alerts RSS feed new item

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content to extract key information and classify sentiment
objects.state_description: Tracks the current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment score, and classification
objects.behavior: When a new article arrives from rss-feed, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, publication source, and publication date. Classify the overall sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral. Assign a confidence score. When extraction is complete, simultaneously send the structured mention record to mention-store, and send the sentiment-classified mention to sentiment-router. When a digest-generation request arrives from weekly-digest, synthesize trends and key article highlights from the data provided and return the digest content. If any processing error occurs, send an error alert to workflow-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send structured mention record with headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, source, and timestamp for storage
Send sentiment-classified mention with full extracted data for routing to appropriate Slack channels
Send error details when any part of article analysis or extraction fails

objects.role: Write service that stores all extracted mention records in a Zapier Table as a searchable historical database
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all stored mention records including headline, summary, quotes, sentiment, source URL, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a structured mention record is received, write it to the Zapier Table with all fields. Record the entry in the audit trail.

objects.role: Business logic object that routes mentions to the appropriate Slack channels based on sentiment classification
objects.state_description: Tracks routing decisions made per mention including sentiment classification and destination channel
objects.behavior: When a sentiment-classified mention arrives, apply routing rules: if sentiment is positive, send the mention to brand-wins-channel; if sentiment is negative, send the mention to brand-escalations-channel with an approval workflow trigger; if sentiment is neutral, send the mention to brand-wins-channel with a neutral label. If routing fails, send an error alert to workflow-alerts.

peers.relationship: Send positive and neutral mention summaries for immediate posting to #brand-wins
Send negative mention details with approval workflow trigger to #brand-escalations
Send error details when routing fails

objects.role: Write service that posts positive and neutral brand mentions to the #brand-wins Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all messages posted to #brand-wins
objects.behavior: When a mention message is received, format and post it to the #brand-wins Slack channel. Record the post in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service that posts negative brand mentions with approval workflow prompts to the #brand-escalations Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all escalation messages posted to #brand-escalations including approval status
objects.behavior: When a negative mention is received, format it with an approval workflow prompt and post it to the #brand-escalations Slack channel. Record the escalation in the audit trail.

objects.role: Entry-point service that fires a weekly scheduled trigger to initiate digest generation
objects.state_description: Tracks the last digest generation timestamp
objects.behavior: On the weekly schedule, fire a digest generation event to weekly-digest.

peers.relationship: Send weekly digest generation trigger on schedule

event_sources: scheduled trigger: weekly recurrence

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates weekly digest generation by querying stored mentions and producing a trend summary
objects.state_description: Tracks the current digest generation cycle including date range and digest content being assembled
objects.behavior: When a weekly trigger arrives from weekly-digest-trigger, query mention-store-query for all mention records from the past week. Once data is retrieved, forward the records to mention-analysis for trend synthesis and digest content generation. When the digest content is returned, send it to digest-channel for posting. If any step fails, send an error alert to workflow-alerts.

peers.relationship: Request all mention records from the past seven days for digest generation
Send retrieved mention records requesting trend synthesis and digest content
Send completed weekly digest content for posting to #brand-weekly-digest
Send error details when digest generation or posting fails

objects.role: Read service that retrieves stored mention records from the Zapier Table for querying and digest generation
objects.behavior: When a query is received specifying filters such as date range, sentiment, or keyword, retrieve the matching mention records from the Zapier Table. When answering queries, call the `mention_store_query_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that posts the weekly digest to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all weekly digests posted to #brand-weekly-digest
objects.behavior: When a digest message is received, format and post it to the #brand-weekly-digest Slack channel. Record the post in the audit trail.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Chatbot that accepts natural language queries from team members and returns search results from the mention database
objects.state_description: Tracks active chatbot sessions and query history
objects.behavior: When a natural language query arrives from a team member, forward it to chatbot-query-handler for processing. When a response is received from chatbot-query-handler, deliver it back to the team member via the chatbot interface. If any query fails, send an error alert to workflow-alerts.

peers.relationship: Forward natural language queries from team members for processing and database search
Send error details when chatbot query processing fails

event_sources: external system: Zapier Chatbot user query submitted

objects.role: Business logic object that interprets natural language questions and retrieves relevant mention records from the database
objects.state_description: Tracks current query being processed including parsed intent, filters, and retrieved results
objects.behavior: When a natural language query arrives from chatbot-interface, parse the intent and extract filters such as date range, sentiment, keyword, or source. Query mention-store-query with the derived filters. Format the retrieved results into a clear, readable response and return it to chatbot-interface.

peers.relationship: Send structured query with filters derived from natural language input to retrieve matching mentions
Send formatted query results back to the chatbot interface for delivery to the team member

objects.role: Write service that notifies the Marketing Operations Administrator in #workflow-alerts when any part of the workflow fails
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all error alerts sent to #workflow-alerts
objects.behavior: When an error alert is received, format it with the error source, description, timestamp, and affected workflow step, then post it to the #workflow-alerts Slack channel and record it in the audit trail.

steps.text: Google Alerts RSS feed has published a new item: headline 'Acme Corporation Wins Innovation Award for Sustainable Packaging', URL 'https://example-news.com/acme-innovation-award', publication source 'TechBusiness Daily', publication time '2024-06-10T09:15:00Z', content: 'Acme Corporation was recognized today at the Global Innovation Summit for its breakthrough in sustainable packaging materials. CEO Jane Holloway accepted the award on behalf of the company, stating that this reflects Acme products commitment to environmental responsibility. Industry analysts called it a major positive milestone for AcmeCorp.'

expect.action: Positive mention record for 'Acme Corporation Wins Innovation Award for Sustainable Packaging' stored in Zapier Table with headline, summary, notable quotes, publication source 'TechBusiness Daily', publication date '2024-06-10T09:15:00Z', and sentiment 'positive'; message posted to #brand-wins Slack channel sharing the mention details
expect.reason: mention-analysis extracts and classifies the article as positive, mention-store writes the record to Zapier Table, and sentiment-router forwards the mention to brand-wins-channel which posts to #brand-wins

steps.text: Weekly scheduled recurrence trigger fires for brand media digest generation at 2024-06-10T08:00:00Z
Zapier Chatbot user query submitted by Marcus Delgado, Content Strategist: 'Show me all negative mentions of Acme CEO from the past two weeks'

expect.action: Response delivered to Marcus Delgado via Zapier Chatbot listing all negative mention records referencing Acme CEO from the past two weeks, retrieved from the Zapier Table
expect.reason: chatbot-interface forwards the query to chatbot-query-handler, which parses intent and filters by sentiment 'negative', keyword 'Acme CEO', and date range of past two weeks, queries mention-store-query, and returns results to chatbot-interface for delivery

name: Instagram Content Calendar Automation
raw_steps: User enters Instagram post details including content description, scheduled date/time, and image URL into the Zapier interface.
Post details are automatically stored in Zapier Tables as a central content calendar database.
A Zap monitors the database for scheduled posts and identifies when the correct posting time has arrived.
The Zap executes the post on Instagram at the scheduled time.
After successful publication, the Zap updates the corresponding database record with a confirmation ID.
User can track which posts have been published and when by viewing the updated database records.

objects.role: Entry-point write service that receives new Instagram post details from the Zapier interface and stores them as scheduled records in Zapier Tables.
objects.state_description: Tracks all submitted post records including content description, scheduled date/time, image URL, publication status, and confirmation IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new post submission arrives, store the record in Zapier Tables and forward the post details to the scheduling-policy object. When an update request arrives with a confirmation ID, record the confirmation ID against the corresponding post record and mark it as published.

peers.relationship: Forward new post details (content description, scheduled date/time, image URL, record ID) when a new submission is received
Self-update: receive confirmation ID and record ID to mark post as published

event_sources: external system: user submits Instagram post details via Zapier interface

objects.role: Business logic object that monitors scheduled post records and determines when the correct posting time has arrived to trigger publication.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending scheduled posts with their target date/time and record IDs, and monitors elapsed time to identify posts due for publication.
objects.behavior: When a new post record is received, add it to the pending schedule. Continuously evaluate the scheduled date/time of each pending post against the current time. When a post's scheduled time has arrived, forward the post details to the instagram-publisher. When publication confirmation is received, forward the confirmation ID and record ID to content-calendar to update the record.

peers.relationship: Send post details (content description, image URL, record ID) when the scheduled posting time has arrived
Send confirmation ID and record ID to update the post record after successful publication

objects.role: Write service that executes Instagram posts at the scheduled time and returns a confirmation ID upon successful publication.
objects.state_description: Tracks published posts with their content, image URL, timestamp of publication, and Instagram confirmation IDs.
objects.behavior: When post details are received, publish the post to Instagram using the provided content description and image URL. Upon successful publication, capture the confirmation ID returned by Instagram and forward it along with the record ID to scheduling-policy.

peers.relationship: Send Instagram confirmation ID and record ID after successful post publication

steps.text: User submits a new Instagram post via the Zapier interface with the following details: content description 'Exciting summer sale — up to 50% off all items! 🌞 #summersale #deals', scheduled date and time '2024-07-15T14:00:00-05:00', and image URL 'https://images.example.com/summer-sale-banner.jpg'.

expect.action: New post record stored in Zapier Tables with content description 'Exciting summer sale — up to 50% off all items! 🌞 #summersale #deals', scheduled time '2024-07-15T14:00:00-05:00', and image URL 'https://images.example.com/summer-sale-banner.jpg'; post details forwarded to scheduling-policy for monitoring.
expect.reason: content-calendar stores incoming post submissions to Zapier Tables and forwards details to scheduling-policy per its behavior and peer declarations.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify scheduling-policy.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to scheduling-policy.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify content-calendar.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to content-calendar.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify instagram-publisher.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to instagram-publisher.

name: Comprehensive Employee Offboarding System
raw_steps: Manager or HR submits termination details through a Google Form (name, type, last day)
The workflow immediately sends customized Slack messages to all relevant teams (HR, IT, payroll) with action steps
The system automatically updates the HRIS (like BambooHR) with termination details
For involuntary terminations: Calculate severance based on tenure/location, then use AI to draft a separation agreement
For voluntary departures: Send the employee a Slack DM with an offboarding guide and exit survey link
When exit survey is submitted: Enrich feedback with HRIS data and use AI to generate a TLDR and action steps
If employee requests an exit interview: Notify the assigned People Business Partner
Create a tracking sheet in Zapier Tables for Talent Acquisition to manage backfill needs via Slack emojis
If receiving severance: Track payment details and update HRIS
If farewell message submitted: Route for review/approval before posting to Slack

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives termination form submissions from Google Forms and forwards them into the system
objects.state_description: Logs each form submission with employee name, termination type, last day, and submission timestamp
objects.behavior: When a Google Form submission arrives, forward the termination details to offboarding-policy.

peers.relationship: Forward termination form data (name, termination type, last day) when a new submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Google Form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives exit survey submissions and forwards them for processing
objects.state_description: Logs each exit survey submission with employee identifier, responses, and submission timestamp
objects.behavior: When an exit survey submission arrives, forward the survey data to exit-survey-analysis.

peers.relationship: Forward exit survey responses when a submission is received

event_sources: webhook: exit survey form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives farewell message submissions from employees and routes them for review
objects.state_description: Logs each farewell message submission with employee name, message content, and submission timestamp
objects.behavior: When a farewell message submission arrives, forward it to offboarding-policy for routing.

peers.relationship: Forward farewell message content and employee details when a submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: farewell message form submission

objects.role: Dual-role Slack channel object for #talent-acquisition that receives tracking posts and forwards emoji reactions to offboarding-policy
objects.state_description: Tracks posted backfill tracking messages and any emoji reaction events received
objects.behavior: When a backfill tracking message is sent to this channel, record it. When an emoji reaction event arrives from a user, forward it to offboarding-policy via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction events from #talent-acquisition when they occur

event_sources: slack event: emoji reaction in #talent-acquisition

objects.role: Read/write service object representing BambooHR for employee record lookups and updates
objects.state_description: Tracks all termination detail updates and severance payment records written to BambooHR
objects.behavior: When a write request arrives, record the termination details or severance payment update. When answering queries, call the bamboohr_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies offboarding rules, routes by termination type, and orchestrates all downstream actions
objects.state_description: Tracks active offboarding cases with employee name, termination type, last day, severance eligibility, assigned PBP, and current workflow stage
objects.behavior: When termination form data arrives: simultaneously send customized notifications to hr-offboarding-channel, it-offboarding-channel, and payroll-offboarding-channel; send a BambooHR update to bamboohr. If termination type is involuntary: query bamboohr for tenure and location data, then calculate severance using tenure and location rules, then send severance details and employee info to separation-agreement-drafting for AI drafting; also send severance payment tracking details to severance-tracker. If termination type is voluntary: send the employee an offboarding guide and exit survey link via employee-dm. When a farewell message arrives: send it to hr-offboarding-channel for review and approval before posting to general-channel. When an emoji reaction event arrives from talent-tracking-channel: update severance or backfill tracking state accordingly.

peers.relationship: Send customized action-step notification on new termination and farewell messages for review
Send customized IT action-step notification on new termination
Send customized payroll action-step notification on new termination
Send termination details for record update on new termination
Send employee info and calculated severance details for involuntary terminations
Send severance payment details to track when employee is receiving severance
Send offboarding guide and exit survey link for voluntary departures
Send backfill tracking post for Talent Acquisition to manage via emoji reactions
Forward approved farewell messages for posting

objects.role: Business logic object that enriches exit survey feedback with BambooHR data and generates AI summaries and action steps
objects.state_description: Tracks processed exit surveys with enriched feedback, TLDR summaries, action steps, and interview request flags
objects.behavior: When exit survey data arrives: query bamboohr for the employee's HR record data to enrich the feedback. Use AI to generate a TLDR summary and action steps from the enriched feedback. If the survey indicates an exit interview request, send a notification to people-ops-channel with the assigned People Business Partner. Send the TLDR and action steps to hr-offboarding-channel for awareness.

peers.relationship: Query for employee HR data to enrich exit survey feedback
Notify assigned People Business Partner when exit interview is requested
Send AI-generated TLDR and action steps from exit survey

objects.role: Business logic object that uses AI to draft separation agreements for involuntary terminations based on severance and employee data
objects.state_description: Tracks drafted separation agreements with employee name, severance amount, draft content, and draft status
objects.behavior: When employee info and severance details arrive, use AI to draft a customized separation agreement incorporating the severance amount, tenure, and location. Send the drafted agreement to hr-offboarding-channel for review.

peers.relationship: Send drafted separation agreement for HR review

objects.role: Write service object representing the #hr-offboarding Slack channel that receives HR team notifications and documents
objects.state_description: Logs all messages posted to #hr-offboarding including action-step notifications, separation agreements, exit survey summaries, and farewell message review requests
objects.behavior: When a message arrives, post it to the #hr-offboarding Slack channel and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #it-offboarding Slack channel that receives IT team notifications
objects.state_description: Logs all IT action-step notifications posted to #it-offboarding
objects.behavior: When a message arrives, post it to the #it-offboarding Slack channel and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #payroll-offboarding Slack channel that receives payroll team notifications
objects.state_description: Logs all payroll action-step notifications posted to #payroll-offboarding
objects.behavior: When a message arrives, post it to the #payroll-offboarding Slack channel and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #people-ops Slack channel for People Business Partner notifications
objects.state_description: Logs all exit interview request notifications posted to #people-ops
objects.behavior: When a notification arrives, post it to the #people-ops Slack channel and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #general Slack channel where approved farewell messages are posted
objects.state_description: Logs all approved farewell messages posted to #general
objects.behavior: When an approved farewell message arrives, post it to the #general Slack channel and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object that sends Slack direct messages to employees
objects.state_description: Logs all direct messages sent to employees including offboarding guides and exit survey links
objects.behavior: When a DM request arrives with recipient and content, send the Slack direct message to the specified employee and record it in the log.

objects.role: Write service object representing a Zapier Tables tracking sheet for severance payment details
objects.state_description: Tracks severance payment records with employee name, payment amount, schedule, and BambooHR update status
objects.behavior: When severance payment details arrive, record them in the tracking sheet and send an update to bamboohr with the payment details.

peers.relationship: Send severance payment details to update BambooHR record

steps.text: Google Form submission received: Employee name: Sarah Mitchell, Title: Senior Software Engineer, Termination type: Involuntary, Last day: 2025-02-14, Tenure: 4 years 3 months, Location: California, Manager: David Chen, Employee ID: EMP-4821

expect.action: Slack notification posted to #hr-offboarding with action steps for Sarah Mitchell's involuntary termination; Slack notification posted to #it-offboarding with IT action steps for Sarah Mitchell; Slack notification posted to #payroll-offboarding with payroll action steps for Sarah Mitchell; BambooHR record for EMP-4821 updated with termination type 'Involuntary' and last day 2025-02-14; severance calculated based on 4 years 3 months tenure in California and a separation agreement drafted by AI and sent to #hr-offboarding; backfill tracking entry created in Zapier Tables for talent acquisition and posted to #talent-acquisition mentioning Sarah Mitchell Senior Software Engineer
expect.reason: offboarding-policy receives termination data and simultaneously notifies hr-offboarding-channel, it-offboarding-channel, payroll-offboarding-channel, updates bamboohr, triggers separation-agreement-drafting for involuntary termination, and creates a tracking entry in talent-tracking-channel

steps.text: Exit survey form submission received: Employee name: Sarah Mitchell, Employee ID: EMP-4821, Departure reason: Better career opportunity, Manager feedback: Management communication could be improved, Company culture rating: 4/5, Role clarity rating: 3/5, Compensation satisfaction: 3/5, Exit interview requested: Yes, Assigned People Business Partner: Jordan Lee

expect.action: BambooHR record for EMP-4821 queried to enrich survey data; AI-generated TLDR summary and action steps from Sarah Mitchell's exit survey sent to #hr-offboarding; notification sent to #people-ops informing Jordan Lee that Sarah Mitchell has requested an exit interview
expect.reason: exit-survey-analysis enriches survey data with BambooHR data, generates AI TLDR and action steps posted to hr-offboarding-channel, and notifies people-ops-channel because exit interview was requested

steps.text: Emoji reaction event in #talent-acquisition: User Marcus Thompson reacted with ✅ to the backfill tracking message for Sarah Mitchell (Senior Software Engineer, EMP-4821), indicating backfill approved

expect.action: Emoji reaction forwarded to offboarding-policy indicating backfill approval for Sarah Mitchell by Marcus Thompson
expect.reason: talent-tracking-channel forwards emoji reaction events to offboarding-policy per its peer declaration and behavior

steps.text: Farewell message form submission received: Employee name: Sarah Mitchell, Employee ID: EMP-4821, Farewell message: 'It has been an incredible journey working with this amazing team for over four years. I am grateful for every opportunity and wish everyone continued success. Thank you for the memories!'

expect.action: Farewell message from Sarah Mitchell forwarded to #hr-offboarding for review and approval before posting to #general
expect.reason: farewell-form forwards submission to offboarding-policy, which routes the farewell message to hr-offboarding-channel for review prior to posting to general-channel

name: Form to Zoho CRM Integration
raw_steps: Set up a comprehensive form to capture contact details and company information.
Configure a table to store submitted form data as a staging area.
Create a Zap that triggers when a new form submission is received.
Pull the data from the table and map it to corresponding Zoho CRM fields.
Automatically create a new entry in the specified CRM module.
Use Canvas to diagram and optimize the entire workflow.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new Typeform form submissions and forwards contact data into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission events including full name, email, phone number, job title, and company name.
objects.behavior: When a new Typeform submission event arrives, forward the full submission payload (full name, email address, phone number, job title, company name) to the lead-intake peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full form submission payload when a new Typeform submission is received

event_sources: webhook: Typeform new submission

objects.role: Business logic object that validates and maps incoming form submission data to Zoho CRM lead fields and orchestrates staging and CRM record creation.
objects.state_description: Tracks current submission being processed including mapped field values: Last Name, Email, Phone, Title, Account Name.
objects.behavior: When a form submission arrives, extract and validate the contact fields: map full name to Last Name, email address to Email, phone number to Phone, job title to Title, and company name to Account Name. If any required field (Last Name, Email) is missing or malformed, discard the submission and log the error. When fields are valid, send the raw submission data to airtable-staging for storage and simultaneously send the mapped CRM fields to zoho-crm-leads for lead record creation.

peers.relationship: Send raw form submission data for staging storage when a valid submission is processed
Send mapped CRM lead fields (Last Name, Email, Phone, Title, Account Name) when a valid submission is processed

objects.role: Write service object representing the Airtable 'Form Submissions' table that stores raw form submission data as a staging area.
objects.state_description: Stores all received form submission records with full name, email, phone, job title, and company name fields; initially empty.
objects.behavior: When a form submission record is received, write it as a new row in the 'Form Submissions' Airtable table, preserving all submitted fields.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zoho CRM Leads module that creates new Lead records from mapped contact data.
objects.state_description: Stores all created Lead records with fields Last Name, Email, Phone, Title, and Account Name; initially empty.
objects.behavior: When mapped CRM lead field data is received, create a new Lead record in the Zoho CRM Leads module with the provided Last Name, Email, Phone, Title, and Account Name values.

steps.text: New Typeform submission received from Sarah Mitchell, Marketing Director at Brightwave Solutions. Submitted fields: full name: Sarah Mitchell, email address: sarah.mitchell@brightwavesolutions.com, phone number: +1-415-555-0192, job title: Marketing Director, company name: Brightwave Solutions.

expect.action: A new Lead record is created in the Zoho CRM Leads module with Last Name: Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@brightwavesolutions.com, Phone: +1-415-555-0192, Title: Marketing Director, Account Name: Brightwave Solutions; and a corresponding row is written to the Airtable 'Form Submissions' table with all submitted fields for Sarah Mitchell.
expect.reason: typeform-submissions forwards the submission payload to lead-intake, which maps the fields and orchestrates writes to both airtable-staging and zoho-crm-leads.

name: Marketing Intake Form Integration
raw_steps: Clients or team members submit the form with project details including objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline.
Form data is automatically integrated with connected tools such as CRM systems, project management software, or email marketing platforms.
Any changes or updates to the form data are instantly reflected across all connected applications to keep stakeholders informed.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives marketing intake form submissions with project details including objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks all submitted and updated form entries with their full field payloads and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, forward the full form payload to intake-routing. When an existing form entry is updated, forward the updated payload and change delta to intake-routing.

peers.relationship: Forward full form payload on new submission or update event

event_sources: webhook: form submitted
webhook: form updated

objects.role: Business logic object that applies intake routing rules, validates form data completeness, and determines which downstream systems must be updated and how.
objects.state_description: Tracks current processing state of each intake submission including validation status, routing decisions, and which integrations have been dispatched.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, validate that all required fields (objectives, target audience, budget, timeline) are present. If any required field is missing, flag the record as incomplete and do not forward. If valid, map the form data to Salesforce opportunity fields, Asana task fields, and Mailchimp contact/campaign fields, then send all three simultaneously. When an update event arrives for an existing submission, revalidate the updated payload, re-map all fields, and send updates to Salesforce, Asana, and Mailchimp simultaneously without waiting for any confirmation.

peers.relationship: Send mapped opportunity/lead record data on new submission or update
Send mapped project task data on new submission or update
Send mapped contact and campaign data on new submission or update

objects.role: Write service object that records intake form data as Salesforce opportunities or leads and maintains an audit trail of all writes.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Salesforce records written, including opportunity/lead IDs, field values, and timestamps of creation and updates.
objects.behavior: When a mapped Salesforce record payload arrives, write or update the corresponding opportunity or lead record. Record the operation in the audit trail with timestamp and payload.

objects.role: Write service object that records intake form data as Asana project tasks and maintains an audit trail of all writes.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Asana tasks written, including task IDs, field values, assignees, and timestamps of creation and updates.
objects.behavior: When a mapped Asana task payload arrives, create or update the corresponding project task. Record the operation in the audit trail with timestamp and payload.

objects.role: Write service object that records intake form data as Mailchimp contacts or campaign entries and maintains an audit trail of all writes.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Mailchimp records written, including contact IDs, list memberships, campaign tags, and timestamps of creation and updates.
objects.behavior: When a mapped Mailchimp contact/campaign payload arrives, create or update the corresponding contact record and apply any relevant campaign tags or audience segments. Record the operation in the audit trail with timestamp and payload.

steps.text: A new marketing intake form submission has arrived with the following project details: Objectives: Launch a Q3 brand awareness campaign targeting millennials in urban markets; Target Audience: Adults aged 25-40 in New York, Los Angeles, and Chicago; Budget: $85,000; Timeline: June 1 – September 30, 2024. Submitted by Elena Vasquez, Senior Marketing Manager at Brightline Creative Agency.

expect.action: Salesforce opportunity record created for Elena Vasquez / Brightline Creative Agency with objectives, target audience ($85,000 budget, June 1–September 30 2024 timeline) written and audit-logged; Asana project task created with the same project details and audit-logged; Mailchimp contact record created for Elena Vasquez with campaign tags for Q3 brand awareness and audience segment for millennials in urban markets, audit-logged.
expect.reason: intake-form forwards the payload to intake-routing, which validates all required fields (objectives, target audience, budget, timeline) as present and routes mapped payloads to salesforce, asana, and mailchimp, each of which writes the record and logs the operation.

steps.text: An existing marketing intake form entry has been updated. The original submission was from Elena Vasquez, Senior Marketing Manager at Brightline Creative Agency. The updated fields are: Budget changed from $85,000 to $110,000; Timeline extended from June 1 – September 30, 2024 to June 1 – December 31, 2024. All other fields remain unchanged: Objectives: Launch a Q3 brand awareness campaign targeting millennials in urban markets; Target Audience: Adults aged 25-40 in New York, Los Angeles, and Chicago.

expect.action: Salesforce opportunity record for Elena Vasquez / Brightline Creative Agency updated with new budget ($110,000) and timeline (June 1–December 31, 2024) and audit-logged; Asana project task updated with revised budget and timeline and audit-logged; Mailchimp contact record for Elena Vasquez updated with revised campaign details and audit-logged.
expect.reason: intake-form detects an updated form entry, forwards the updated payload and change delta to intake-routing, which validates completeness and routes update payloads to salesforce, asana, and mailchimp, each of which updates the existing record and logs the operation.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from salesforce field mapping data. Reference table defining how intake form fields map to Salesforce opportunity and lead field names, types, and required formats.
mock_tools.response_template: {"salesforce_field_mapping_data": {"field_mapping_version": "2.4.1", "last_updated": "2024-05-15", "object_type": "Lead_Opportunity_Hybrid", "records": [{"id": "OPP-20240601-0847", "status": "new", "source": "marketing_intake_form", "assignee": "Elena Vasquez", "company": "Brightline Creative Agency", "contact": {"full_name": "Elena Vasquez", "title": name: Order Request Form Workflow
raw_steps: User fills out the Column Order Request Form with details such as the current column order, desired order, and any additional notes or instructions.
Upon submission, responses are instantly captured and stored in a secure, cloud-based database, organized into a structured table format.
The table updates in real-time, allowing team members to view and manage responses as soon as they are submitted.
Optionally, Zaps can be configured to send automated email or Slack notifications to relevant team members when a new form submission is received.
The form can be integrated with applications such as Google Sheets, Excel, or project management tools to further streamline workflows.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures Column Order Request Form submissions and forwards them into the workflow
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions including current column order, desired column order, and additional notes
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, forward the full submission payload to the order-request-workflow peer.

peers.relationship: Forward complete form submission data when a new submission is received

event_sources: webhook: form submission received

objects.role: Business logic object that processes column order requests, validates submission data, and orchestrates downstream outputs
objects.state_description: Tracks active order requests with their current column order, desired column order, notes, submission timestamp, and processing status
objects.behavior: When a new order request submission arrives, validate that required fields (current column order, desired column order) are present. If fields are missing or malformed, flag the submission as incomplete. If valid, prepare a structured record for storage and prepare a notification summary. Send the structured record to airtable and google-sheets simultaneously, and send the notification summary to slack-notifications.

peers.relationship: Send structured order request record for storage when a valid submission is processed
Send structured order request record for integration when a valid submission is processed
Send notification summary to the #order-requests channel when a valid submission is processed

objects.role: Write service that stores order request form submissions in a structured Airtable table
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all stored order request records including column order details, notes, and submission timestamps
objects.behavior: When a structured order request record is received, store it as a new row in the designated Airtable table, making it immediately visible to Operations team members in real-time.

objects.role: Write service that records order request submissions in Google Sheets to support downstream workflow integration
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all order request records written to Google Sheets
objects.behavior: When a structured order request record is received, append it as a new row in the designated Google Sheets spreadsheet.

objects.role: Write service that sends automated Slack notifications to the #order-requests channel when new form submissions are received
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent including message content and timestamps
objects.behavior: When a notification summary is received, post it as a message to the #order-requests Slack channel.

steps.text: A new Column Order Request Form submission has been received. The submitter is Rachel Nguyen, Operations Analyst. Current column order: ID, Name, Status, Date, Owner, Region, Notes. Desired column order: ID, Name, Owner, Region, Status, Date, Notes. Additional instructions: Please apply this reordering to all active project tracking sheets by end of week.

expect.action: New row created in the designated Airtable table with Rachel Nguyen's submission (current order: ID, Name, Status, Date, Owner, Region, Notes; desired order: ID, Name, Owner, Region, Status, Date, Notes; notes: apply to all active project tracking sheets by end of week); new row appended in the designated Google Sheets spreadsheet with the same record; and a notification message posted to the #order-requests Slack channel summarizing Rachel Nguyen's order request.
expect.reason: order-request-form forwards the submission to order-request-workflow, which validates the required fields (both current and desired column orders are present) and routes the structured record to airtable, google-sheets, and slack-notifications peers for storage and notification.

name: UTM Builder with Link Storage
raw_steps: User submits a form in Zapier Interfaces with UTM parameters including website URL, campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, and content.
Zapier automatically encodes the submitted content using all UTM tags to generate a custom UTM-tagged URL.
Zapier generates a shortened version of the UTM-tagged URL.
Zapier stores both the full UTM-tagged URL and shortened version in a Zapier Table.
The table is displayed directly beneath the form for easy access and reference.
Users can create additional Zaps to automate downstream workflows, such as sending the generated UTM links to Slack or other integrated systems.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interfaces form that captures UTM parameters submitted by users.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted form entries including website URL, campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, and content fields.
objects.behavior: When a user submits the UTM form, forward all submitted field values to the utm-builder peer.

peers.relationship: Forward all UTM form field values (website URL, campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, content) when a form submission arrives.

event_sources: webhook: form submitted via Zapier Interfaces

objects.role: Business logic object that constructs UTM-tagged URLs and coordinates storage of both full and shortened URLs.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current UTM parameter set being processed and the generated full and shortened URLs.
objects.behavior: When UTM parameters arrive from the form, construct the full UTM-tagged URL by appending utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content, and campaign ID as query parameters to the website URL. URL-encode all parameter values to ensure valid URL formation. Once the full URL is assembled, send it to url-shortener to generate a shortened version. When the shortened URL is returned, send both the full UTM-tagged URL and the shortened URL to utm-link-store. If a downstream Slack notification is configured, send both URLs to slack-marketing-channel.

peers.relationship: Send the full UTM-tagged URL to generate a shortened version.
Send both the full UTM-tagged URL and shortened URL for persistent storage.
Send the full and shortened UTM URLs for distribution to the marketing team when downstream Slack automation is active.

objects.role: Read service object representing the URL shortening external system that generates shortened URLs on demand.
objects.state_description: No persistent state; retrieves shortened URLs on demand by calling the external shortening service.
objects.behavior: When a full URL is received, generate and return a shortened version. When answering queries, call the `url_shortener_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table that stores full and shortened UTM-tagged URLs for display and reference.
objects.state_description: Accumulates records of UTM link pairs, each containing the full UTM-tagged URL, the shortened URL, and associated UTM parameter metadata.
objects.behavior: When a full UTM-tagged URL and shortened URL are received, record both as a new row in the Zapier Table along with any available metadata such as campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, and content.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #marketing-campaigns Slack channel that receives UTM link notifications.
objects.state_description: Accumulates outbound Slack messages containing UTM link pairs sent to the marketing channel.
objects.behavior: When UTM link data is received, post a formatted message to the #marketing-campaigns Slack channel containing the full UTM-tagged URL and the shortened URL.

steps.text: User submits the UTM form with the following parameters: website URL: https://www.acmecorp.com/products, campaign ID: CAMP-2024-Q1, source: google, medium: cpc, name: spring_sale, term: running_shoes, content: banner_ad_v2

expect.action: A new row is recorded in the Zapier Table (utm-link-store) containing the full UTM-tagged URL (https://www.acmecorp.com/products?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=spring_sale&utm_term=running_shoes&utm_content=banner_ad_v2&campaign_id=CAMP-2024-Q1) and its shortened URL, along with metadata: campaign ID CAMP-2024-Q1, source google, medium cpc, name spring_sale, term running_shoes, content banner_ad_v2
expect.reason: utm-form forwards submitted fields to utm-builder, which constructs the full UTM-tagged URL and requests a shortened URL from url-shortener, then sends both to utm-link-store which records them as a new row in the Zapier Table

mock_tools.description: Write or notify url-shortener.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to url-shortener.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify utm-link-store.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to utm-link-store.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify slack-marketing-channel.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to slack-marketing-channel.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from url shortener data. When a full URL is received, generate and return a shortened version. When answering queries, call the `url_shortener_data` tool to retrieve data.
mock_tools.response_template: {"url_shortener_data": {"entries": [{"original_url": "https://www.acmecorp.com/products", "utm_parameters": {"utm_source": "google", "utm_medium": "cpc", "utm_campaign": "spring_sale", "utm_term": "running_shoes", "utm_content": "banner_ad_v2", "campaign_id": "CAMP-2024-Q1"}, "full_url_with_utm": "https://www.acmecorp.com/products?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=spring_sale&utm_term=running_shoes&utm_content=banner_ad_v2&campaign_id=CAMP-2024-Q1", "shortened_url": "https://acme.link/s8Kx2Pq", "short_code": "s8Kx2Pq", "domain": "acme.link", "created_at": "2024-03-15T10:22:45Z", "expires_at": null, "click_count": 0, "is_active": true, "tags": ["spring_sale", "cpc", "google", "CAMP-2024-Q1"]}]}}

name: Landing Page Form to CRM Pipeline
raw_steps: A visitor fills out a form on the landing page.
Their information is automatically stored in a Zapier table.
An automated workflow sends a notification to review the new submission.
You can click a button to add the submitter to your CRM directly from the table.
Optionally, a workflow sends an email or grants access to the offering to each form submitter.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures visitor form submissions from the landing page and forwards them into the pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming form submissions with visitor details such as name, email, and any other submitted fields.
objects.behavior: When a visitor submits the landing page form, forward the submission payload to the lead-intake-pipeline business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full form submission data when a new visitor submits the landing page form.

event_sources: webhook: form submitted

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates the lead intake domain — deciding how to store, notify, and confirm each new form submission.
objects.state_description: Tracks each submission being processed, including visitor details, storage status, notification status, and confirmation email status.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, send the visitor data to zapier-table for storage, send a notification to sales-dev-notification simultaneously, and send a confirmation email request to confirmation-email. When a button-click event arrives indicating a user wants to add a submitter to Salesforce, send the relevant record data to salesforce-crm.

peers.relationship: Send visitor form data for storage when a new submission is received.
Send notification with submission details to alert the Sales Development team when a new submission is received.
Send confirmation email request with submitter details when a new submission is received.
Send submitter record data to add to Salesforce when a button-click add-to-salesforce event is triggered.

objects.role: Write service that stores visitor form submission data in a Zapier table for record-keeping and manual action.
objects.state_description: Accumulates stored visitor records with their submitted form fields and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a visitor record is received, store it in the Zapier table. When a button-click event to add a record to Salesforce is initiated from the table UI, forward the event to lead-intake-pipeline.

peers.relationship: Forward add-to-salesforce button-click events when a user clicks the button in the table UI.

event_sources: user action: add-to-salesforce button clicked in Zapier table

objects.role: Write service that sends notifications to the Sales Development team about new form submissions requiring review.
objects.state_description: Logs notifications sent to the Sales Development team including submission details and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a notification request is received, deliver the notification with submission details to the Sales Development team channel or inbox. Record the notification as sent.

objects.role: Write service that sends confirmation emails to form submitters acknowledging their submission.
objects.state_description: Logs confirmation emails sent including recipient address, timestamp, and submission reference.
objects.behavior: When a confirmation email request is received with submitter details, send a confirmation email to the submitter's email address. Record the email as sent.

objects.role: Write service that creates or updates contact and lead records in Salesforce when submitters are added manually.
objects.state_description: Logs records written to Salesforce including submitter details and the timestamp of each write operation.
objects.behavior: When a submitter record is received, create or update the corresponding lead or contact record in Salesforce. Record the operation as completed.

steps.text: A visitor named Sarah Mitchell has submitted the landing page form with the following details: full name 'Sarah Mitchell', email 'sarah.mitchell@example.com', company 'Brightline Solutions', job title 'Marketing Manager', and phone number '+1-555-204-8833'.

expect.action: Confirmation email sent to sarah.mitchell@example.com addressing Sarah Mitchell; notification delivered to the Sales Development team channel with submission details for Sarah Mitchell (Brightline Solutions, Marketing Manager); visitor record for Sarah Mitchell stored in the Zapier table.
expect.reason: lead-intake-pipeline simultaneously forwards the submission to zapier-table for storage, sales-dev-notification for team review, and confirmation-email to acknowledge the submitter

steps.text: A user clicked the 'Add to Salesforce' button in the Zapier table for the record belonging to Sarah Mitchell (email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, company: Brightline Solutions, job title: Marketing Manager, phone: +1-555-204-8833).

expect.action: Lead or contact record created or updated in Salesforce for Sarah Mitchell at Brightline Solutions with email sarah.mitchell@example.com, job title Marketing Manager, and phone +1-555-204-8833; operation recorded as completed.
expect.reason: zapier-table forwards the add-to-salesforce button-click event to lead-intake-pipeline, which sends the submitter record to salesforce-crm to create or update the corresponding lead or contact

name: AI Voice Generator to Google Drive
raw_steps: User fills out a form with text input.
The form sends the text to OpenAI to generate a voice file.
The generated audio file displays in a table for review.
User clicks Save if the audio sounds good.
The workflow uploads the saved file to Google Drive for storage and review.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives form submissions from Typeform containing the user's script text.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions with submitted script text and submission timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a form submission event arrives, forward the submitted script text to the audio-generation-pipeline peer.

peers.relationship: Forward submitted script text when a new Typeform form submission arrives.

event_sources: webhook: Typeform form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates script-to-audio conversion, applying rules about when to generate audio and when to route outputs for review.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress and completed audio generation requests, including script text, generated audio file references, and review status.
objects.behavior: When a script text message arrives from typeform-intake, send the script text to openai-tts to generate an audio file. When the generated audio file is received back, send it to airtable-review for display and user review. When a save-approval event arrives from airtable-review indicating the user approved the audio, send the approved audio file to google-drive-storage for upload.

peers.relationship: Send script text to generate an audio file via text-to-speech when a new script arrives.
Send generated audio file for display and review after audio generation is complete.
Send approved audio file for upload when the user clicks Save in Airtable.

objects.role: Write/read service object that calls the OpenAI text-to-speech API to convert script text into an audio file and returns the result.
objects.state_description: Tracks script-to-audio conversion requests and their resulting audio file references.
objects.behavior: When a script text message arrives, call the OpenAI TTS API to generate an audio file. Reply with the generated audio file to the sender. When answering queries, call the `openai_tts_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Return generated audio file reference after successful TTS conversion.

objects.role: Dual-role service object that displays generated audio files in an Airtable table for user review and forwards Save approval events back to the business logic object.
objects.state_description: Tracks audio files displayed for review, their approval status, and Save actions taken by users.
objects.behavior: When an audio file arrives, write it as a new record in the Airtable table for display and review. When a user clicks Save on a record in Airtable, forward the save-approval event along with the associated audio file to audio-generation-pipeline via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward save-approval event with the approved audio file when the user clicks Save in Airtable.

event_sources: webhook: Airtable Save button click event

objects.role: Write service object that uploads approved audio files to the Audio Recordings folder in Google Drive.
objects.state_description: Tracks uploaded audio files with their Google Drive file references, upload timestamps, and folder locations.
objects.behavior: When an approved audio file message arrives, upload it to the Google Drive folder named Audio Recordings. Record the resulting file reference and upload timestamp in state.

steps.text: Typeform submission received: Script text is 'Welcome to our quarterly product update. This quarter we launched three new features designed to streamline your workflow and boost productivity. Thank you for being a valued customer.'

expect.action: Audio file generated from the submitted script text uploaded as a new record in the Airtable table for display and review.
expect.reason: typeform-intake forwards the script text to audio-generation-pipeline, which sends it to openai-tts for audio generation, then routes the returned audio file to airtable-review, which writes it as a new Airtable record.

steps.text: Airtable Save button clicked on the record containing the audio file generated from the script 'Welcome to our quarterly product update. This quarter we launched three new features designed to streamline your workflow and boost productivity. Thank you for being a valued customer.'

expect.action: Audio file uploaded to the Google Drive folder named Audio Recordings.
expect.reason: airtable-review forwards the save-approval event and associated audio file to audio-generation-pipeline, which routes the approved audio file to google-drive-storage, which uploads it to the Audio Recordings folder.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from openai tts data. When a script text message arrives, call the OpenAI TTS API to generate an audio file. Reply with the generated audio file to the sender. When answering queries, call the `openai_tts_data` tool to ret
mock_tools.response_template: {"openai_tts_data": [{"id": "tts_rec_7Xk2mNpQ94", "script_text": "Welcome to our quarterly product update. This quarter we launched three new features designed to streamline your workflow and boost productivity.
