name: Jira Issue Creation From Form
raw_steps: Design a custom form using Zapier Interfaces to capture issue information such as summary, description, issue type, priority, and assignee.
Store form submissions in a Zapier Table for data manipulation and processing.
Trigger a Zap when a new form submission is received.
Map the form data from the Table to the corresponding fields in Jira Software Cloud.
Create a new issue in Jira Software Cloud with the mapped information.
Optionally notify team members or update other connected apps upon issue creation.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures new Jira issue form submissions from Zapier Interfaces and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming form submissions including summary, description, issue type, priority, and assignee fields.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission event arrives, forward the full submission payload to the issue-mapping peer.

peers.relationship: Forward complete form submission data (summary, description, issue type, priority, assignee) when a new submission is received.

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interfaces form submission

objects.role: Write service that persists form submissions to a Zapier Table for audit and data manipulation purposes.
objects.state_description: Stores all received form submissions as rows with fields: summary, description, issue type, priority, assignee, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a submission record is received, write it to the Zapier Table and retain it in state.

objects.role: Business logic object that maps raw form submission data to Jira-compatible field values and enforces field validation rules.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current submission being processed including mapped Jira fields and validation status.
objects.behavior: When a form submission arrives, simultaneously persist it to the submission store and apply field mapping rules: map issue type values (Bug, Task, Story, Epic) to Jira issue type identifiers; map priority values (Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest) to Jira priority levels; pass summary, description, and assignee through directly. Validate that all required fields are present and non-empty. When mapping is complete and valid, send the mapped Jira payload to jira-cloud. When mapping is complete, also send the mapped payload to slack-notifier.

peers.relationship: Send the raw form submission for persistence immediately upon receipt.
Send the fully mapped Jira issue payload (summary, description, issue type, priority, assignee) once field mapping is complete and validated.
Send the mapped issue details (summary, issue type, priority, assignee) to trigger Engineering team notification once mapping is complete.

objects.role: Write service that creates new issues in Jira Software Cloud using the mapped field data.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all issue creation requests sent to Jira, including summary, issue type, priority, assignee, and creation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a mapped Jira issue payload is received, create a new issue in Jira Software Cloud with the provided summary, description, issue type, priority, and assignee fields. Record the creation event in state.

objects.role: Write service that posts issue creation notifications to the #jira-notifications Slack channel for the Engineering team.
objects.state_description: Log of all notifications sent to #jira-notifications including issue summary, type, priority, assignee, and notification timestamp.
objects.behavior: When issue details are received, post a formatted notification message to the #jira-notifications Slack channel informing the Engineering team of the newly created Jira issue, including summary, issue type, priority, and assignee.

steps.text: New form submission received from Zapier Interfaces with the following details: Summary: 'Login page throws 500 error on invalid credentials', Description: 'When a user enters incorrect credentials on the login page, the server returns a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a 401 Unauthorized response. Steps to reproduce: 1) Navigate to /login, 2) Enter invalid username/password, 3) Click Submit.', Issue Type: Bug, Priority: High, Assignee: Marcus Chen, Software Engineer.

expect.action: A new Jira issue is created in Jira Software Cloud with summary 'Login page throws 500 error on invalid credentials', issue type Bug, priority High, and assignee Marcus Chen; and a formatted notification is posted to the #jira-notifications Slack channel informing the Engineering team of the newly created issue including the summary, issue type, and priority.
expect.reason: form-intake forwards the submission to issue-mapping, which simultaneously persists it to submission-store and maps the fields, then triggers jira-cloud to create the issue and slack-notifier to post to #jira-notifications.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify submission-store.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to submission-store.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify jira-cloud.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to jira-cloud.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify slack-notifier.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to slack-notifier.

name: Document Approval Workflow
raw_steps: Requesters submit documents through an upload form.
The submitted document is stored in an approval table.
A Zap sends an email notification to the approver when a new document arrives.
The approver clicks an approve or reject button in the email.
A notification email is sent to the requester with the approval decision.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Google Forms upload form that receives submitted documents from requesters.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted documents with requester identity, document content, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new document is submitted via the Google Forms upload form, forward the document details and requester information to the approval-policy object.

peers.relationship: Forward submitted document details and requester information when a new document arrives

event_sources: webhook: Google Forms submission with document upload

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces the document approval workflow, routing documents for review and processing approval decisions.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight approval requests with document details, requester identity, approver assignment, current status (pending/approved/rejected), and decision timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new document submission arrives, record it as a pending approval and send the document record to the approval-table and an email notification to the approver-notifier simultaneously. When an approval decision (approve or reject) arrives from the approval-action-receiver, update the status of the corresponding document record and send the decision notification to the requester-notifier.

peers.relationship: Send document details and requester information to store as a new approval record when a submission arrives
Send document details and approval request when a new document needs review
Send the approval decision and requester contact details when a decision has been made
Receives approve/reject decisions forwarded from approval-action-receiver

objects.role: Write service object representing the Airtable approval table that stores submitted document records and their approval status.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all document approval records including document content, requester, submission time, approval status, and decision timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a document record is received, store it in the Airtable approval table. When a status update is received, update the corresponding record with the approval decision and timestamp.

objects.role: Write service object representing the email notification system that sends approval request emails to the approver with approve/reject action buttons.
objects.state_description: Log of approval request emails sent, including document reference, approver address, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an approval request is received, send an email to the designated approver containing the document details and embedded approve/reject action buttons linking back to the workflow.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the endpoint that captures the approver's approve or reject button click from the email and forwards the decision into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Log of received approval decisions including document reference, decision made, and timestamp of action.
objects.behavior: When an approve or reject action event arrives from the approver's email button click, forward the decision along with the document reference to the approval-policy object via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward the approver's decision (approve or reject) and document reference when an action button is clicked

event_sources: webhook: approver clicks approve or reject button in email

objects.role: Write service object representing the email notification system that sends the approval decision to the original document requester.
objects.state_description: Log of decision notification emails sent, including requester address, document reference, decision, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a decision notification is received, send an email to the requester informing them whether their document was approved or rejected.

steps.text: A new document has been submitted via the Google Forms upload form. Requester: Maria Chen, Senior Analyst. Document title: Q3 Budget Report. File URL: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTuV. Submission timestamp: 2024-11-14T09:32:00Z.

expect.action: Email sent to the designated approver containing document details for 'Q3 Budget Report' submitted by Maria Chen, with approve/reject action links embedded in the message. Record for 'Q3 Budget Report' stored in the Airtable approval table with status 'pending'.
expect.reason: approval-policy routes new submissions simultaneously to approval-table (to store the record) and approver-notifier (to send the approval request email) upon receiving document details from document-intake.

steps.text: The approver, David Okafor, has clicked the Approve button in the notification email for the document 'Q3 Budget Report' submitted by Maria Chen. Document reference ID: DOC-2024-1114-001. Decision: approved. Action timestamp: 2024-11-14T10:05:00Z.

expect.action: Email sent to Maria Chen informing her that her document 'Q3 Budget Report' has been approved. Record for 'Q3 Budget Report' in the Airtable approval table updated with status 'approved' and timestamp 2024-11-14T10:05:00Z.
expect.reason: approval-action-receiver forwards the decision to approval-policy, which sends a decision notification to requester-notifier (email to requester) and a status update to approval-table (record update).

name: Lead Capture and Email Notification
raw_steps: Create a customizable Contact Us form page using Zapier Interfaces with configurable fields such as text, date pickers, and file uploads.
Add custom branding to the Contact Us page to match your website.
Share the form URL or embed the form code on your website.
When a user submits the form, automatically store the lead information in a Zapier table.
Automatically redirect the user to a customizable Thank You page after form submission.
Automatically send an email notification to you containing the new lead's contact information and a link to the Zapier table.
Enable the ability to send emails directly to new leads by clicking a button within the Zapier table.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interfaces Contact Us form that captures lead submissions and triggers the automation.
objects.state_description: Tracks form configuration including fields (Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, Company Name, Message, Preferred Contact Date, Resume Upload), branding settings (logo, primary color #2D6BE4, font), and the Thank You page redirect message.
objects.behavior: When a user submits the Contact Us form, forward the full lead payload (all submitted field values including any uploaded resume reference) to the lead-capture-policy business logic object. The form is configured with custom branding and redirects the user to a Thank You page displaying 'Thank you for reaching out! Our team will contact you within 1 business day.' immediately after submission.

peers.relationship: Forward complete lead submission payload when a user submits the Contact Us form

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interfaces form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces lead capture rules, coordinates storage of new leads, and triggers notifications upon form submission.
objects.state_description: Holds the current lead submission being processed including all contact fields and resume reference.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from the contact-form, validate that required fields (Full Name, Email Address) are present. Then simultaneously send the lead data to the leads-table write service for storage and send a notification request to the sales-notification write service containing the lead's contact information and a link to the Leads Zapier table. No sequential confirmation is required from either service.

peers.relationship: Send full lead data for storage when a new valid lead submission is received
Send lead contact details and Leads table link for email notification to Sales Manager when a new lead is received

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table named Leads that persistently stores captured lead records and supports direct email sending to leads.
objects.state_description: Stores all captured lead records with fields: Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, Company Name, Message, Preferred Contact Date, Resume Upload reference, and submission timestamp. Also tracks the Send Email button configuration per record.
objects.behavior: When a lead record message is received, append it as a new row in the Leads Zapier table. Each record includes a Send Email button that allows users to initiate an email directly to the lead from within the table interface.

objects.role: Write service object representing the email notification system that sends new lead alerts to the Sales Manager.
objects.state_description: Logs all outbound email notifications sent to the Sales Manager, including lead contact details and table links included in each notification.
objects.behavior: When a notification request is received, send an email to the Sales Manager containing the new lead's Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, Company Name, Message, Preferred Contact Date, and a direct link to the Leads Zapier table.

steps.text: A user submits the Contact Us form with the following information: Full Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email Address: sarah.mitchell@techcorp.com, Phone Number: +1-415-555-0192, Company Name: TechCorp Solutions, Message: 'We are interested in learning more about your enterprise pricing and integration options.', Preferred Contact Date: 2024-02-15, Resume Upload: sarah_mitchell_resume.pdf

expect.action: New lead record for Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@techcorp.com, +1-415-555-0192, TechCorp Solutions) written to the Leads Zapier table, and an email notification sent to the Sales Manager containing Sarah Mitchell's Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, Company Name, Message, Preferred Contact Date, and a direct link to the Leads Zapier table.
expect.reason: lead-capture-policy validates required fields (Full Name and Email Address are present), then simultaneously sends lead data to leads-table (which appends the record) and to sales-notification (which emails the Sales Manager with lead details and a link to the Leads table).

name: Telegram Form Message Automation
raw_steps: Create a customizable form using Zapier Interfaces to collect Telegram message details including chat ID, message text, and advanced options like scheduled sending and inline keyboard interactions.
Set up a Zap trigger that activates when a new form submission is received from the Interfaces form.
Configure the Telegram Send Message action and map form fields to the corresponding Telegram message parameters such as chat ID and message text.
Optionally configure advanced options including message scheduling, inline keyboard interactions, and custom metadata for tracking.
Test and activate the workflow to automatically send customized Telegram messages whenever the form is submitted.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new form submissions from Zapier Interfaces containing Telegram message details
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions including chat ID, message text, scheduled sending options, inline keyboard config, and campaign_id metadata
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives via Zapier Interfaces, forward all submission fields to the telegram-message-policy object. Record the submission in state as an audit trail.

peers.relationship: Forward all form submission fields (chat ID, message text, schedule, inline keyboard config, campaign_id) when a new submission is received

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interfaces form submission received

objects.role: Business logic object that applies message composition rules, maps form fields to Telegram parameters, and enforces scheduling and keyboard configuration policies
objects.state_description: Tracks current message composition context including mapped chat ID, message text, scheduled delivery time, inline keyboard button definitions, and campaign_id for each processed submission
objects.behavior: When a form submission arrives, map the chat_id field to the Telegram chat ID parameter and the message_text field to the Telegram message text parameter. Apply scheduling policy: set delivery time to the fixed configured delivery time from the submission's scheduled sending option. Apply inline keyboard policy: construct an inline keyboard with two buttons labeled Confirm and Cancel using the keyboard interaction fields from the submission. Apply metadata policy: attach the campaign_id field value as a custom metadata field named campaign_id for tracking. Validate that all required fields (chat ID, message text) are present before forwarding. Send the fully composed Telegram message payload to telegram-sender.

peers.relationship: Send fully composed Telegram message payload including chat ID, message text, scheduled delivery time, inline keyboard buttons, and campaign_id metadata when a valid submission has been processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers outbound Telegram messages via the Telegram Send Message action
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Telegram messages sent, including chat ID, message text, scheduled time, inline keyboard config, campaign_id, and delivery status
objects.behavior: When a composed Telegram message payload is received, send the message to the specified chat ID with the provided message text, scheduled delivery time, inline keyboard configuration, and campaign_id metadata using the Telegram Send Message action. Record the delivery attempt and result in state.

steps.text: A new form submission has been received from the Zapier Interfaces form with the following details: chat_id: 987654321, message_text: 'Hello from the campaign! Please confirm your participation.', scheduled_delivery_time: 2024-06-15T14:00:00Z, inline_keyboard_buttons: ['Confirm', 'Cancel'], campaign_id: 'camp_spring_2024'.

expect.action: Telegram message sent to chat ID 987654321 with text 'Hello from the campaign! Please confirm your participation.', scheduled for delivery at 2024-06-15T14:00:00Z, inline keyboard buttons ['Confirm', 'Cancel'] attached, and campaign_id 'camp_spring_2024' included as metadata.
expect.reason: form-submissions forwards submission fields to telegram-message-policy, which maps chat_id and message_text to Telegram parameters and applies scheduling and keyboard policies, then telegram-sender delivers the composed message payload via the Telegram Send Message action.

name: Email Campaign Drip Sequence Manager
raw_steps: Add four emails and their content to the Email Campaign Portal Template table, specifying the sequence number for each email.
Navigate to the connected Workflows (Zaps) and configure each step of the automation.
Test each workflow to ensure proper functionality.
Publish each workflow.
Add a prospect to the form to trigger the email campaign sequence.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures new prospect submissions from the intake form and triggers the email drip campaign.
objects.state_description: Records each prospect submission received, including prospect name, email address, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new prospect submission arrives via the intake form, forward the prospect details to the campaign-sequence peer. Record the submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward prospect details (name, email, submission timestamp) when a new intake form submission is received.

event_sources: external system: intake form prospect submission

objects.role: Read service that retrieves email campaign template records from the Airtable Email Campaign Portal Template table, including content and sequence numbers.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for email template records by sequence number or full list. When answering queries, call the `airtable_templates_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces the drip sequence rules, determining which email template to send at each step based on sequence number and prospect state.
objects.state_description: Tracks each active prospect's current sequence position (1–4), scheduled send times, and completion status.
objects.behavior: When a new prospect is received from intake-form, look up all four email templates from airtable-templates by sequence numbers 1 through 4. Schedule email sends in drip order: send sequence 1 immediately, then sequence 2, 3, and 4 at configured intervals. For each scheduled send, forward the prospect email address and the corresponding template content to gmail-sender. When all four emails have been dispatched for a prospect, mark the prospect's sequence as complete in state.

peers.relationship: Query for email templates by sequence number (1–4) when a new prospect is received.
Send prospect email address and template content for each sequence step at the scheduled time.
Send workflow execution status and sequence progress for each prospect after each email dispatch.

objects.role: Write service that sends outbound emails to prospects via Gmail using the provided template content.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all emails sent, including recipient address, sequence number, template content used, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an email send request is received containing a recipient address and template content, deliver the email via Gmail and record the send event in state.

objects.role: Write service that records Zapier workflow execution events, sequence progress, and campaign status for auditing and monitoring.
objects.state_description: Log of all workflow execution events, including prospect ID, sequence step, execution result, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a workflow execution status message is received, append the event details to the audit log in state.

steps.text: New prospect submission received from the intake form. Prospect details: Sarah Mitchell, Marketing Director at Brightline Solutions, email: sarah.mitchell@brightlinesolutions.com, phone: 555-204-8811, company size: 150 employees, interested in: enterprise plan.

expect.action: Prospect record for Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@brightlinesolutions.com) recorded in intake-form state and details forwarded to campaign-sequence peer for drip sequence initiation.
expect.reason: intake-form behavior specifies that on new prospect submission it records the submission in state and forwards prospect details to the campaign-sequence peer.

name: Product Feedback Collection and Response
raw_steps: Customer submits feedback through the Feedback Form in the Interface.
The submitted feedback is automatically added as a new row in the Product Feedback Table.
The 'Send Email Notification' Zap triggers when a new row is added to the table.
An email notification is sent to the team with all feedback information.
Team member reviews the feedback and fills out the email subject, body, and from name fields in the table.
Team member clicks the send email button to send a reply to the customer.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the customer-facing feedback form that captures and forwards submitted feedback.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted feedback entries with customer details and feedback content.
objects.behavior: When a customer submits feedback through the form, forward the complete feedback payload to the feedback-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward all submitted feedback data when a new submission is received

event_sources: external system: customer submits feedback form

objects.role: Business logic object that governs the feedback lifecycle — routing new submissions for storage and notification, and handling manager-composed replies to customers.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current feedback item under review, including customer contact info, feedback content, and any reply fields (subject, body, from name) filled in by the Product Manager.
objects.behavior: When a new feedback submission arrives from the feedback form, write it to the product-feedback-table and send an email notification to the product team via email-notifier simultaneously. When a Product Manager submits a reply (email subject, body, from name populated and send button clicked), send the composed reply email to the customer via customer-email-sender.

peers.relationship: Write new feedback row when a submission arrives
Send notification with all feedback details to the Product Team when a new submission arrives
Send the composed reply email to the customer when the Product Manager triggers the send action

objects.role: Write service representing the Product Feedback Table that persists feedback rows and manager-composed reply fields.
objects.state_description: Stores all feedback rows, each containing customer info, feedback content, and optional reply fields (email subject, body, from name) added by the Product Manager.
objects.behavior: Record every feedback entry and reply-field update received. When a new row is added, emit a row-added event. Maintain the full audit trail of all feedback and associated manager responses.

peers.relationship: Forward row-added event to feedback-processor when a new feedback row is created

event_sources: external system: Product Manager fills reply fields and clicks send email button in the table

objects.role: Write service that sends email notifications to the Product Team when new feedback is received.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all notification emails sent, including timestamp, recipient, and feedback summary.
objects.behavior: When a notification request is received, send an email to the Product Team containing all provided feedback information. Record the sent notification in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that sends reply emails from the Product Team to customers using the manager-composed subject, body, and from name.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all reply emails sent to customers, including recipient, subject, body, from name, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a reply email request is received containing subject, body, from name, and customer email address, send the email to the customer and record it in the audit log.

steps.text: Customer Sarah Mitchell submits feedback through the feedback form with the following details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, Product: Mobile App v2.3, Rating: 3/5, Feedback: 'The new navigation menu is confusing and hard to use. I often get lost trying to find the settings page. Please consider reverting to the previous layout or providing a clearer guide.'

expect.action: Email notification sent to the Product Team containing Sarah Mitchell's feedback details (name: Sarah Mitchell, email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, product: Mobile App v2.3, rating: 3/5, feedback message about confusing navigation menu), and a new row recorded in the Product Feedback Table with the same feedback information.
expect.reason: feedback-processor receives the submission from feedback-form and simultaneously writes the row to product-feedback-table and sends a notification via email-notifier to the Product Team

steps.text: Product Manager Alex Rivera fills in the reply fields for Sarah Mitchell's feedback row in the Product Feedback Table: Email Subject: 'Re: Your Feedback on Mobile App v2.3 Navigation', From Name: 'Alex Rivera, Product Team', Email Body: 'Dear Sarah, thank you for your feedback on the Mobile App v2.3 navigation. We hear your concerns and are actively working on improvements. In the meantime, we have added a quick-access guide in the Help section. We appreciate your patience and support.' Alex Rivera then clicks the send email button.
steps.source: Product Manager via Product Feedback Table

expect.action: Reply email sent to sarah.mitchell@example.com with subject 'Re: Your Feedback on Mobile App v2.3 Navigation', from name 'Alex Rivera, Product Team', and the composed body message thanking Sarah Mitchell and describing the navigation improvements and help guide.
expect.reason: product-feedback-table emits a send-email event upon manager action, feedback-processor routes the reply fields to customer-email-sender, which sends the email to the customer

mock_tools.description: Write or notify product-feedback-table.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to product-feedback-table.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify email-notifier.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to email-notifier.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify customer-email-sender.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to customer-email-sender.

name: Applicant Tracker Notification System
raw_steps: Create a job posting page with a customizable form for candidates to submit information.
Send an email notification to the hiring manager when a new application is received.
Record an updated date in the applicant tracker when a candidate's status changes.
Share the job posting URL or embed it on a website to receive applications.
Manage applicants, materials, and statuses in the centralized Applicant Tracker interface.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives candidate job applications submitted via the job posting form
objects.state_description: Logs all received application submissions including name, email, resume, cover letter, and submission timestamp
objects.behavior: When a candidate submits the application form with their name, email, resume, and cover letter, forward the complete application payload to the application-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full application payload (name, email, resume, cover letter) when a new submission is received

event_sources: webhook: form submission event

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives applicant status change events from the Applicant Tracker interface
objects.state_description: Logs all status change events including applicant ID, previous status, new status, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a status change event arrives indicating a candidate's status has changed to Applied, Interviewing, Offer Extended, or Rejected, forward the event details to the application-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward status change details (applicant ID, new status, timestamp) when a candidate status update is received

event_sources: webhook: applicant status change event

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing and processing rules for new applications and status changes
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight application events and status transitions being processed, including which notifications and records have been dispatched
objects.behavior: When a new application payload arrives, simultaneously send a hiring manager notification request to email-notifications and record the new applicant entry with status Applied to applicant-tracker. When a status change event arrives with a new status of Applied, Interviewing, Offer Extended, or Rejected, send an updated-date record request to applicant-tracker with the applicant ID, new status, and current timestamp. No other statuses trigger a record update.

peers.relationship: Send hiring manager notification request with applicant name and email when a new application is received
Send new applicant record or status update with applicant details, new status, and timestamp when triggered by a new application or status change

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to the Hiring Manager
objects.state_description: Audit log of all email notifications sent, including recipient, subject, body, and send timestamp
objects.behavior: When a notification request is received, send an email to the Hiring Manager with the provided applicant details and record the send event in the audit log.

objects.role: Dual-role write service and read service that stores applicant records, materials, and statuses and serves as the centralized interface for managing applicants
objects.state_description: Stores all applicant records including name, email, resume, cover letter, current status, and updated date; tracks status history per applicant
objects.behavior: When a new applicant record is received, store it with the provided details and set the initial status and updated date. When a status update is received, update the applicant's status and record the updated date. When a status change event originates from the tracker interface, forward it to status-change-events via peer message. When answering queries, call the applicant_tracker_data tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward status change event details when a hiring team member updates an applicant's status via the tracker interface

steps.text: Candidate Maya Patel submits a job application for the Senior Software Engineer position with name: Maya Patel, email: maya.patel@email.com, resume: 5 years of experience in full-stack development with expertise in Python and React, cover letter: I am excited to apply for this role as my background aligns closely with your team's needs.

expect.action: Email notification sent to the Hiring Manager with applicant details for Maya Patel (maya.patel@email.com, Senior Software Engineer); applicant record created in applicant-tracker with status Applied and updated date recorded.
expect.reason: application-routing simultaneously sends a notification request to email-notifications and records the new applicant entry with status Applied to applicant-tracker upon receiving a new application payload

steps.text: Applicant status change event received for Maya Patel (email: maya.patel@email.com) for the Senior Software Engineer position: status updated to Interviewing.

expect.action: Applicant record for Maya Patel updated in applicant-tracker with status Interviewing and a new updated date recorded.
expect.reason: application-routing forwards status change events to applicant-tracker, which updates the applicant's status and records the updated date

name: Lead Router Alert System
raw_steps: Prospects submit information through a customizable lead capture form embedded on the website.
The system routes leads to qualified sales reps based on criteria such as region and company size.
Qualified sales representatives are alerted when a new lead matches their criteria.
An alerted sales rep claims the lead through the agent claim page.
The system updates lead details when a rep claims it.
The system tracks sales team events (meetings booked and closed won deals) to calculate agent ranking scores.
Weights for metrics (Meetings booked and Closed Won) are adjusted via the weighted control to refine agent ranking.
Agents and sales reps are added to the agent roster table with region, company size, and active status.
The CRM is connected to record agent events (meetings booked and closed won deals) into the agent events table.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives prospect submissions from the website lead capture form and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming lead submissions with prospect details including name, company, company size, region, and contact information.
objects.behavior: When a prospect submits the lead capture form, forward the full lead payload to the lead-routing object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead submission payload when a new prospect submits the form

event_sources: webhook: lead form submitted

objects.role: Read service that retrieves qualified sales representative records from the Airtable agent roster table including region, company size, and active status.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about available sales reps filtered by region, company size, and active status. When answering queries, call the `agent_roster_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to match incoming leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing status, matched rep assignments, and claim state.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, query the agent-roster read service for active sales reps matching the lead's region and company size. When matching reps are found, notify all matched reps simultaneously via the slack-alerts write service. When a rep claims a lead via the claim event, update the lead's claim state and send the updated lead details to salesforce-leads. When no matching reps are found, log the unmatched lead in its state for review.

peers.relationship: Query for active sales reps matching the lead's region and company size when a new lead arrives
Send lead details and matched rep list to post in #new-leads channel
Send claimed lead details including rep assignment when a rep claims the lead

objects.role: Write service that posts lead alert notifications to the Slack #new-leads channel for qualified sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent including lead details, matched reps, and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a lead alert message arrives, post it to the #new-leads channel in Slack. Record each notification in state as an audit entry.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the internal sales portal claim page where reps claim leads; receives claim actions and forwards them to lead-routing.
objects.state_description: Tracks claim events including which rep claimed which lead and when.
objects.behavior: When a sales rep submits a claim action on the agent claim page, forward the claim event including lead ID and rep ID to lead-routing via peer message. Record each claim event in state.

peers.relationship: Forward lead claim events including lead ID and rep ID when a rep claims a lead

event_sources: external system: sales rep submits lead claim via portal

objects.role: Write service that records and updates lead details in Salesforce when a rep claims a lead.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all lead records written and updated in Salesforce including claim assignments and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When lead claim details arrive, write or update the lead record in Salesforce with the assigned rep and claim status. Record each operation in state.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives sales team events from Salesforce including meetings booked and closed won deals and forwards them for ranking calculation.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Salesforce events including event type, rep ID, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce agent event arrives such as a meeting booked or closed won deal, forward the event payload to agent-ranking.

peers.relationship: Forward agent event details including event type and rep ID when a Salesforce event arrives

event_sources: webhook: Salesforce agent event recorded (meeting booked, closed won)

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the current metric weights for meetings booked and closed won from the weighted control panel configuration.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about current weight values for ranking metrics including meetings booked and closed won. When answering queries, call the `ranking_weights_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that calculates and maintains agent ranking scores based on Salesforce events and configurable metric weights.
objects.state_description: Tracks each agent's cumulative event counts and current ranking scores indexed by rep ID.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce agent event arrives, query ranking-weights for the current metric weights. Apply the weights to the agent's event counts to recalculate their ranking score. Update the agent's score in state. Write the updated event and score to the airtable-agent-events write service.

peers.relationship: Query for current metric weights when recalculating agent ranking scores
Send agent event details and updated ranking score to be recorded in Airtable

objects.role: Write service that records agent events including meetings booked and closed won deals into the agent events table in Airtable.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all agent events written to Airtable including event type, rep ID, score, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an agent event record arrives, write it to the agent events table in Airtable. Record each write operation in state.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the weighted control panel where admins adjust metric weights for agent ranking.
objects.state_description: Tracks weight update events including which metrics were adjusted and their new values.
objects.behavior: When an admin submits updated weights via the control panel, forward the new weight configuration to agent-ranking.

peers.relationship: Forward updated metric weight configuration when an admin adjusts weights via the control panel

event_sources: external system: admin updates metric weights via weighted control panel

steps.text: Prospect Maria Gonzalez submits the lead capture form on the website with the following details: name: Maria Gonzalez, email: maria.gonzalez@techcorp.com, company: TechCorp, company size: Enterprise, region: West Coast, phone: 415-555-0192.

expect.action: Slack message posted to #new-leads channel notifying matched sales reps of new lead from Maria Gonzalez at TechCorp (Enterprise, West Coast).
expect.reason: lead-capture-form forwards the lead payload to lead-routing, which queries agent-roster for matching active reps by region and company size, then instructs slack-alerts to post a notification to #new-leads.

steps.text: Sales rep Jordan Kim (rep ID: rep-204) submits a claim on lead ID lead-8821 (Maria Gonzalez, TechCorp) via the agent claim page in the internal sales portal.

expect.action: Lead record for Maria Gonzalez (lead-8821) written/updated in Salesforce with assigned rep Jordan Kim and claim status set to claimed.
expect.reason: lead-claim-portal forwards the claim event to lead-routing, which sends claim details to salesforce-leads to update the lead record in Salesforce.

steps.text: Salesforce records a 'meeting booked' event for sales rep Jordan Kim (rep ID: rep-204), associated with lead TechCorp / Maria Gonzalez, event timestamp: 2024-06-10T14:00:00Z.

expect.action: Agent event record for Jordan Kim (meeting booked, TechCorp/Maria Gonzalez) written to the agent events table in Airtable, and Jordan Kim's ranking score recalculated and updated.
expect.reason: salesforce-events forwards the event to agent-ranking, which queries ranking-weights, recalculates the score, and instructs airtable-agent-events to write the event record.

steps.text: Admin Taylor Reyes submits updated metric weights via the weighted control panel: meetings booked weight = 0.4, closed won weight = 0.6.

expect.action: Agent ranking scores recalculated for all affected agents using updated weights (meetings booked: 0.4, closed won: 0.6) and scores updated accordingly.
expect.reason: weight-control-panel forwards the new weight configuration to agent-ranking, which applies the updated weights to recalculate agent ranking scores.

name: Round Robin Lead Assignment
raw_steps: Capture lead information through a customizable form on a lead capture page.
Maintain a sales reps table with current representatives and their assigned positions.
Automatically update positions in the sales reps table when a new rep is added or an existing rep is removed.
Retrieve the sales rep currently in position 1 when a new lead is submitted.
Assign the new lead to the sales rep in position 1.
Move the assigned sales rep to the back of the line to rotate positions.
Send a notification alert to the assigned sales rep via the chosen app.
Accept leads from third-party sources such as Facebook Lead Ads or CRM systems.
Repeat the assignment and rotation process for each new lead received.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures lead information submitted through the company website lead capture form.
objects.state_description: Logs each submitted lead payload including contact details and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a lead form submission arrives, forward the lead data to the lead-assignment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward lead data when a new lead form submission is received

event_sources: webhook: lead form submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from Facebook Lead Ads.
objects.state_description: Logs each Facebook lead event including lead details and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Facebook Lead Ad submission arrives, forward the lead data to the lead-assignment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward lead data when a new Facebook Lead Ad submission is received

event_sources: external system: Facebook Lead Ad submitted

objects.role: Read/write service object representing the Airtable sales reps table that stores representatives and their ordered positions.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current list of sales representatives with their assigned queue positions, updated on each assignment rotation and when reps are added or removed.
objects.behavior: When receiving a write request to update positions or rotate a rep to the back of the line, record the changes. When receiving a write request to add or remove a rep, update the table and recalculate sequential positions for all remaining reps. When answering queries, call the sales_reps_table_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that implements the round-robin lead assignment rules, determining which sales rep receives each new lead and rotating the queue.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current round-robin state including the last assigned rep and pending assignment in progress.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from any source, query the sales-reps-table to retrieve the rep currently in position 1. Assign the lead to that rep. Then send a write request to sales-reps-table to move the assigned rep to the back of the queue, incrementing all other reps up by one position. Send the lead assignment details and rep contact info to slack-notifications. Send the lead record to lead-record-store.

peers.relationship: Query for the rep in position 1 when a new lead arrives; send rotation update to move assigned rep to the back of the queue
Send assignment alert with lead details and assigned rep info after each lead is assigned
Send new lead record with assigned rep details for storage

objects.role: Write service object that sends Slack notification alerts to assigned sales reps when a new lead is assigned to them.
objects.state_description: Logs each notification sent including recipient rep, lead details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send the alert message to the specified sales rep via Slack and record the notification in the log.

objects.role: Write service object that persists new lead records along with their assigned sales rep information.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with assignment details, source, and submission timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a lead record arrives, store it with the assigned rep, lead source, and all submitted lead fields.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted through the company website lead capture form. Lead details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, Phone: 555-234-5678, Company: Bright Horizons LLC, Interest: Enterprise Software Package.

expect.action: Lead assigned to the sales rep currently in position 1 in the Airtable sales reps table; that rep is rotated to the back of the queue; a Slack notification is sent to the assigned rep mentioning Sarah Mitchell and her lead details; the lead record for Sarah Mitchell is stored with the assigned rep and lead source.
expect.reason: lead-form forwards the lead to lead-assignment, which queries sales-reps-table for position 1, assigns the lead, rotates the rep to the back, triggers slack-notifications to alert the rep, and stores the record in lead-record-store.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via Facebook Lead Ads. Lead details: Name: Carlos Rivera, Email: carlos.rivera@example.com, Phone: 555-876-5432, Company: Sunrise Digital Media, Interest: Professional Services Plan.

expect.action: Lead assigned to the sales rep now in position 1 (after the prior rotation from the first step); that rep is rotated to the back of the queue; a Slack notification is sent to the newly assigned rep mentioning Carlos Rivera and his lead details; the lead record for Carlos Rivera is stored with the assigned rep and lead source.
expect.reason: facebook-lead-ads forwards the lead to lead-assignment, which queries sales-reps-table for the current position 1 rep, assigns the lead, rotates the rep to the back, triggers slack-notifications, and stores the record in lead-record-store.

name: HubSpot Closed Won Deal Slack Alerts
raw_steps: A deal is marked as Closed Won in HubSpot.
AI creates a summary of key details from the deal.
The summary and extended version are shared in Slack.
If the deal amount exceeds the specified threshold, the update is also shared to an executive-specific channel.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives deal stage change events from HubSpot and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming deal stage change events received from HubSpot, including deal name, amount, close date, and associated contact.
objects.behavior: When a deal stage change event arrives from HubSpot, forward the full deal payload to the deal-summary peer for processing.

peers.relationship: Forward full deal details when a deal is marked as Closed Won in HubSpot

event_sources: webhook: HubSpot deal stage changed to Closed Won

objects.role: Business logic object that generates concise and extended summaries of Closed Won deals and routes them to the appropriate Slack channels based on deal amount.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current deal being processed, including generated short and extended summaries, deal amount, and routing decisions.
objects.behavior: When a Closed Won deal payload arrives, extract deal name, amount, close date, and associated contact. Generate a concise summary and an extended summary of the deal. Send both summaries to the deals-won-channel. If the deal amount exceeds $50,000, also send the summaries to the executive-updates-channel.

peers.relationship: Send short and extended deal summaries for every Closed Won deal
Send short and extended deal summaries when deal amount exceeds $50,000

objects.role: Write service object representing the #deals-won Slack channel that receives and posts deal summary messages.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all deal summary messages posted to the #deals-won Slack channel, including timestamps and deal identifiers.
objects.behavior: When a deal summary message is received, post it to the #deals-won Slack channel. Record the message in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object representing the #executive-updates Slack channel that receives high-value deal summary messages.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all high-value deal summary messages posted to the #executive-updates Slack channel, including timestamps and deal identifiers.
objects.behavior: When a deal summary message is received, post it to the #executive-updates Slack channel. Record the message in the audit trail.

steps.text: HubSpot deal stage changed to Closed Won. Deal name: Acme Corp Enterprise License, amount: $120,000, close date: 2024-06-15, associated contact: Sarah Mitchell, VP of Procurement at Acme Corp.

expect.action: A concise summary and extended summary of the Acme Corp Enterprise License deal ($120,000, closed 2024-06-15, contact: Sarah Mitchell) are posted to the #deals-won Slack channel, and both summaries are also posted to the #executive-updates Slack channel because the deal amount exceeds $50,000.
expect.reason: deal-summary generates summaries and routes them to deals-won-channel for all Closed Won deals, and additionally to executive-updates-channel when deal amount exceeds $50,000.

name: Unified Lead Capture Pipeline
raw_steps: Trigger a capture Zap when a new lead arrives from an advertising platform, contact form, or other source.
Send the lead data from the capture Zap to a centralized catchall Sub-Zap.
Standardize the lead data in the catchall Sub-Zap to ensure consistency.
Add the standardized leads to your CRM.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead events from Facebook Lead Ads and forwards them into the pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming lead events received from Facebook Lead Ads.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from Facebook Lead Ads, forward the raw lead data to the lead-standardization object.

peers.relationship: Forward raw lead data when a new lead event is received from Facebook Lead Ads

event_sources: webhook: Facebook Lead Ads new lead

objects.role: Business logic object that standardizes and normalizes raw lead data to ensure consistency before downstream processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks leads currently being standardized including their raw input and normalized output fields.
objects.behavior: When raw lead data is received, apply standardization rules: normalize field names to a canonical schema (first name, last name, email, phone, source, timestamp), trim whitespace, format phone numbers to E.164, lowercase email addresses, and fill in default values for missing optional fields. Once standardized, send the normalized lead record to salesforce.

peers.relationship: Send standardized lead record after normalization is complete

objects.role: Write service object that receives standardized lead records and adds them as new leads in Salesforce.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all lead records submitted to Salesforce, including submission timestamp and lead fields.
objects.behavior: When a standardized lead record is received, record it in the audit trail and write it to Salesforce as a new lead entry.

steps.text: New lead submitted via Facebook Lead Ads. Lead details: first name: Maria, last name: Gonzalez, email: maria.gonzalez@example.com, phone: 5551234567, source: Facebook Lead Ads, timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:15:00Z.
steps.source: webhook: Facebook Lead Ads new lead

expect.action: Standardized lead record for Maria Gonzalez (email: maria.gonzalez@example.com, phone: +1-555-123-4567, source: Facebook Lead Ads, timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:15:00Z) written to Salesforce as a new lead entry.
expect.reason: facebook-lead-ads forwards raw lead data to lead-standardization, which normalizes the fields and forwards the canonical record to salesforce, which writes it as a new lead in Salesforce.

name: Leave Request Approval Workflow
raw_steps: Admins configure leave types and assign balances per employee in Zapier Interfaces.
Employees log in to view their leave balances and submit leave requests through Zapier Interfaces.
Leave requests are stored in Zapier Tables for manager review.
Managers approve or reject leave requests in Zapier Tables.
Automated notifications are sent upon submission, approval, or rejection of leave requests.
Leave balances are automatically updated based on request status.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new leave request submissions from Zapier Interfaces and forwards them into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted leave requests with employee ID, leave type, dates, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new leave request submission event arrives from Zapier Interfaces, forward the full request payload to the leave-approval-policy object. Record the submission in local state.

peers.relationship: Forward full leave request payload when a new submission event arrives

event_sources: webhook: leave request submitted via Zapier Interfaces

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives manager approval or rejection decisions from Zapier Tables and forwards them into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks manager decisions with request ID, manager ID, decision (approved/rejected), and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a manager approval or rejection event arrives from Zapier Tables, forward the decision payload to the leave-approval-policy object. Record the decision in local state.

peers.relationship: Forward manager decision payload when an approval or rejection event arrives

event_sources: webhook: manager decision recorded in Zapier Tables

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces leave approval rules, validates balances, determines notification targets, and orchestrates downstream updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leave requests under review, including request ID, employee ID, leave type, requested days, current status, and any validation outcomes.
objects.behavior: When a new leave request arrives: validate that the leave type is one of Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Unpaid Leave, or Maternity Leave; query leave-balance-store for the employee's current balance for the requested leave type; if balance is insufficient for types that require it (Annual Leave), flag the request as invalid and notify the employee; otherwise mark the request as pending and write it to leave-request-store; simultaneously send submission notification instructions to email-notifications targeting both the employee and the relevant manager. When a manager decision arrives: retrieve the original request from leave-request-store; if approved, calculate the number of days consumed and send a balance deduction update to leave-balance-store; update the request status in leave-request-store; simultaneously send approval or rejection notification instructions to email-notifications targeting both the employee and the manager.

peers.relationship: Query current leave balance for an employee and leave type before approving; send balance deduction update when a request is approved
Write new pending leave requests on submission; update request status on manager decision
Send notification instructions for submission, approval, and rejection events targeting employee and manager

objects.role: Dual-role read/write service object that stores employee leave balances per leave type and answers balance lookup queries.
objects.state_description: Stores per-employee leave balances keyed by employee ID and leave type (Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Unpaid Leave, Maternity Leave), including configured initial balances and current remaining balances.
objects.behavior: When a balance lookup query arrives, call the leave_balance_store_data tool to retrieve the current balance for the specified employee and leave type, then reply with the result. When a balance deduction update arrives, record the updated remaining balance for the specified employee and leave type. When admin configuration events arrive specifying initial balances, store them against the appropriate employee and leave type. When answering queries, call the `leave_balance_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that persists leave requests and their status changes for manager review and audit purposes.
objects.state_description: Stores all leave requests with fields: request ID, employee ID, leave type, start date, end date, days requested, submission timestamp, status (pending/approved/rejected), and manager decision timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new pending leave request record arrives, store it with status set to pending. When a status update arrives for an existing request, update the status and record the decision timestamp. Maintain the full history of all requests as an audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to employees and managers upon leave request submission, approval, or rejection.
objects.state_description: Tracks sent notification records with recipient, event type, request ID, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When notification instructions arrive specifying recipients, event type (submitted, approved, rejected), and request details, send the appropriate email to each recipient and record the notification in local state.

steps.text: A new leave request has been submitted via Zapier Interfaces. Employee: Sarah Mitchell, Marketing Coordinator. Leave type: Annual Leave. Start date: 2024-02-12. End date: 2024-02-16. Duration: 5 days. Reason: Family vacation. Manager: David Chen, Marketing Manager. Employee email: sarah.mitchell@company.com. Manager email: david.chen@company.com.

expect.action: Leave request stored as pending in leave-request-store for Sarah Mitchell (Annual Leave, 2024-02-12 to 2024-02-16). Submission notification email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com and david.chen@company.com confirming the request has been received and is awaiting manager review.
expect.reason: leave-approval-policy validates the leave type (Annual Leave is valid), queries leave-balance-store for Sarah Mitchell's balance, stores the pending request via leave-request-store, and triggers email-notifications to send submission emails to the employee and manager.

steps.text: Manager David Chen has recorded a decision in Zapier Tables for Sarah Mitchell's leave request (Annual Leave, 2024-02-12 to 2024-02-16). Decision: Approved. Manager email: david.chen@company.com. Employee email: sarah.mitchell@company.com. Request ID: LR-2024-0112.

expect.action: Leave balance for Sarah Mitchell deducted by 5 days for Annual Leave in leave-balance-store. Leave request LR-2024-0112 status updated to approved in leave-request-store. Approval notification email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com and david.chen@company.com confirming the leave request has been approved.
expect.reason: leave-approval-policy processes the approval decision, instructs leave-balance-store to deduct the 5-day balance, updates the request status via leave-request-store, and triggers email-notifications to send approval emails to the employee and manager.

name: Form to Pipedrive Deal Creation
raw_steps: User submits a custom form with deal information including title, value, currency, stage, status, person, organization, close date, and owner.
Form submission data is stored in Zapier Tables for review and modification.
The form submission triggers a Zap that automatically creates a new deal in Pipedrive with the captured information.
Team members can visualize and manage the entire workflow using Canvas.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives custom form submissions containing deal information and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions with their raw payload including title, value, currency, stage, status, person, organization, close date, and owner.
objects.behavior: When a form submission event arrives, forward the complete submission payload to the deal-processing peer. Record the submission in internal state as an audit trail.

peers.relationship: Forward complete form submission payload when a new form is submitted
Send form submission data for storage and review by Revenue Operations team

event_sources: webhook: form submitted

objects.role: Business logic object that validates and maps form submission data to Pipedrive deal fields and enforces deal creation rules.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight deal creation requests with their mapped field values, validation status, and processing outcome.
objects.behavior: When a form submission payload arrives, validate that required fields are present: title, value, currency, stage (must be one of: Qualified, Proposal Sent, Negotiation, Closed Won, Closed Lost), and status (must be one of: Open, Won, Lost). Map the form fields to Pipedrive deal schema: title to deal name, value and currency to deal value, stage to pipeline stage, status to deal status, associated person to contact link, organization to org link, close date to expected close date, and owner to deal owner. If validation passes, send the mapped deal record to pipedrive-deals for creation. If validation fails, log the failure in state and do not forward.

peers.relationship: Send mapped and validated deal record for creation in Pipedrive

objects.role: Write service object representing Zapier Tables that stores form submission data for review and modification by the Revenue Operations team.
objects.state_description: Stores all received form submission records indexed by submission timestamp and form fields for Revenue Operations review.
objects.behavior: When a form submission record is received, store it as a new row in the Zapier Tables dataset. Each record includes all submitted fields. Records are available for the Revenue Operations team to visualize, review, and modify via Zapier Canvas.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Pipedrive CRM that receives mapped deal data and creates new deal records.
objects.state_description: Tracks all deal creation requests received, including deal name, value, currency, stage, status, person, organization, close date, and owner for each created deal.
objects.behavior: When a mapped deal record is received, create a new deal in Pipedrive using the provided fields: name, value, currency, pipeline stage, status, associated person, organization, expected close date, and owner. Record the creation event in internal state as an audit trail.

steps.text: A form submission arrives with the following deal information: Title: 'Enterprise Software License - Acme Corp', Value: 45000, Currency: USD, Stage: Proposal Sent, Status: Open, Associated Person: Sarah Mitchell (VP of Technology), Organization: Acme Corp, Close Date: 2024-03-31, Owner: Daniel Reyes (Account Executive).

expect.action: A new deal record is created in Pipedrive with name 'Enterprise Software License - Acme Corp', value 45000 USD, stage Proposal Sent, status Open, associated person Sarah Mitchell, organization Acme Corp, expected close date 2024-03-31, and owner Daniel Reyes; and the submission is stored as a new row in Zapier Tables containing all submitted fields.
expect.reason: form-intake forwards the submission to deal-processing and zapier-tables; deal-processing validates and maps the fields then sends the mapped deal to pipedrive-deals, which creates the deal record; zapier-tables stores the submission row for Revenue Operations review.

name: AI Agent Marketing Campaign Tracker
raw_steps: Pull ad performance data from Facebook Ads.
Pull ad performance data from LinkedIn Ads.
Structure and store the collected data in Zapier Tables.
Have the AI Agent review weekly performance trends from the stored data.
Generate key insights and recommendations based on the trend analysis.
Send the summary report with recommendations via email or Slack.

objects.role: Entry-point read service representing the Facebook Ads external system, providing ad performance data on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to requests for ad performance data by retrieving current campaign metrics. When answering queries, call the `facebook_ads_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward ad performance data when requested by the campaign performance object

event_sources: scheduled trigger: weekly ad performance data pull

objects.role: Entry-point read service representing the LinkedIn Ads external system, providing ad performance data on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to requests for ad performance data by retrieving current campaign metrics. When answering queries, call the `linkedin_ads_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward ad performance data when requested by the campaign performance object

event_sources: scheduled trigger: weekly ad performance data pull

objects.role: Core business logic object that orchestrates collection of ad data, analyzes weekly performance trends, and generates insights and recommendations.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current weekly analysis cycle, collected ad performance data from all platforms, identified trends, generated insights, and recommendations status.
objects.behavior: When a weekly trigger fires, query facebook-ads and linkedin-ads for their latest ad performance data. Once both data sets are received, structure the combined data and send it to zapier-tables for storage. Then query zapier-tables for the stored weekly trend data. Analyze trends across platforms: compare week-over-week metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, spend, ROAS), identify top-performing and underperforming campaigns, and detect significant changes. Generate key insights summarizing what changed and why it matters. Produce actionable recommendations (e.g., reallocate budget, pause underperforming ads, scale winning creatives). Format a concise summary report including trend highlights, insights, and recommendations. Send the formatted report to slack-marketing.

peers.relationship: Request weekly ad performance data at the start of each analysis cycle
Request weekly ad performance data at the start of each analysis cycle
Send structured combined ad performance data for storage, and query stored data for trend analysis
Send the final summary report with insights and recommendations

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing Zapier Tables, storing structured ad performance data and answering queries about historical trend data.
objects.state_description: Stores structured weekly ad performance records from Facebook Ads and LinkedIn Ads, indexed by platform, campaign, and week.
objects.behavior: Accepts incoming structured ad performance data and records it persistently. When answering queries about stored performance trends, call the `zapier_tables_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Return queried trend data when requested for weekly analysis

objects.role: Write service object representing the Slack #marketing-performance channel, receiving and posting summary reports.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all summary reports posted to the #marketing-performance channel, including timestamps and content.
objects.behavior: When a summary report message is received, post it to the #marketing-performance Slack channel. Record the message content and timestamp in the audit trail.

steps.text: Weekly ad performance data pull scheduled trigger fires for the week of 2024-06-10 to 2024-06-16.
Weekly ad performance data pull scheduled trigger fires for the week of 2024-06-10 to 2024-06-16.
