name: Employee Onboarding Manager
raw_steps: Click a button to trigger the onboarding workflow for a new employee.
Find and add the employee to an onboarding event in Google Calendar.
Add the employee to your Zapier account.
Send a team message notification in Slack.
Send a checklist email to the new hire.
Transform date fields into month-day format for recurring reminders.
Send a reminder email on the employee's work anniversary.
Send a reminder email on the employee's birthday.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures the button-click event initiating the onboarding workflow for a new employee.
objects.state_description: Records each trigger event with employee details and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a button-click onboarding trigger event arrives, forward the employee payload to the onboarding-rules peer.

peers.relationship: Forward new employee details when a button-click trigger event is received

event_sources: webhook: button click onboarding trigger

objects.role: Business logic object that encodes all onboarding domain rules, orchestrates downstream actions, and formats date fields for anniversary and birthday reminders.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current onboarding workflow state per employee including calendar status, Zapier status, Slack notification status, checklist email status, and formatted anniversary/birthday dates.
objects.behavior: When a new employee payload arrives: validate required fields (name, email, start date, birthday); transform start date and birthday into month-day format (MM-DD) for recurring reminders; then simultaneously send calendar onboarding event request to google-calendar, add employee to zapier-account, send Slack notification request to slack-general, send checklist email request to email-service, send anniversary reminder request to email-service, and send birthday reminder request to email-service. Use the formatted month-day dates for the anniversary and birthday reminder emails.

peers.relationship: Send employee details and request to find and add them to the onboarding calendar event
Send employee details to add them to the Zapier account
Send team notification message about the new employee joining
Send checklist email to new hire, anniversary reminder email, and birthday reminder email using formatted month-day dates

objects.role: Write service object representing Google Calendar that records requests to add employees to onboarding events.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all calendar onboarding event additions with employee name, email, event details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a request to add an employee to an onboarding calendar event arrives, record the employee details and event association in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier account that records new employee additions.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all employees added to the Zapier account with employee details and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a request to add a new employee arrives, record the employee details in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Slack #general channel that records team notification messages about new hires.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all team notification messages posted with message content and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a team notification message about a new employee arrives, record the message content in state.

objects.role: Write service object representing the email delivery system that records and sends checklist emails, anniversary reminders, and birthday reminders to employees.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all emails sent including recipient, email type (checklist, anniversary, birthday), scheduled send date, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an email request arrives (checklist, anniversary reminder, or birthday reminder), record the recipient, type, content, and scheduled date in state.

steps.text: Button click received: initiate onboarding workflow for new employee Sarah Mitchell, Junior Software Engineer. Employee details: name=Sarah Mitchell, title=Junior Software Engineer, email=sarah.mitchell@company.com, start date=2024-03-15, birthday=1995-07-22.

expect.action: Google Calendar updated with Sarah Mitchell added to onboarding event; Zapier account updated with Sarah Mitchell's employee record; Slack #general channel notified with message about Sarah Mitchell joining as Junior Software Engineer; checklist email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com; anniversary reminder email scheduled for 03-15 to sarah.mitchell@company.com; birthday reminder email scheduled for 07-22 to sarah.mitchell@company.com.
expect.reason: onboarding-trigger forwards the employee payload to onboarding-rules, which validates fields, transforms start date 2024-03-15 to 03-15 and birthday 1995-07-22 to 07-22, then simultaneously dispatches requests to google-calendar, zapier-account, slack-general, and email-service for all required onboarding actions.

name: Slack to GitHub Issue Creator
raw_steps: A team member pushes a Slack message to the Zap.
The Zap captures the message and all information shared in the thread.
AI analyzes the content and transforms it into a structured GitHub issue format.
A GitHub issue is created with a proper title, description, and relevant labels.
Confirmation notifications are sent to the original Slack thread.
Team members can immediately start working on the newly created issue.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures incoming Slack messages and thread information pushed by the Backend Engineer.
objects.state_description: Records each received Slack message payload including message text, thread context, channel ID, user ID, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Slack message event arrives via the Zap, capture the full message content and all thread information. Forward the complete payload to the issue-formatter peer. When a confirmation message is received from a peer, post it back to the originating Slack thread.

peers.relationship: Forward full Slack message and thread content when a new message event is received
Forward structured issue payload for creation

event_sources: webhook: Slack message pushed via Zap

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes Slack message content and transforms it into a structured GitHub issue format with title, description, and labels.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress formatting jobs with original message content, derived title, description, and assigned labels.
objects.behavior: When a Slack message payload arrives, analyze the content to extract intent, context, and technical details. Generate a concise issue title from the core problem or request. Compose a detailed description preserving all relevant context from the thread. Classify the issue by assigning one or more labels: use 'bug' when the message describes broken or unexpected behavior, 'enhancement' when it describes a new feature or improvement request, and 'documentation' when it describes missing or incorrect docs. When multiple labels apply, include all relevant ones. After formatting is complete, send the structured issue data to github-issues and simultaneously send a confirmation summary to slack-notifications.

objects.role: Write service object that creates GitHub issues from structured issue data received from the issue-formatter.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all GitHub issues created, including title, description, labels, issue URL, and creation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a structured issue payload arrives, create a new GitHub issue with the provided title, description, and labels. Record the created issue details including the resulting issue URL in state.

peers.relationship: Send confirmation message after issue is created

objects.role: Write service object that posts confirmation notifications back to the original Slack thread after a GitHub issue is created.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all confirmation messages sent, including target channel, thread timestamp, message content, and delivery timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a confirmation message payload arrives, post the confirmation notification to the specified Slack thread. Record the delivery details in state.

steps.text: Backend Engineer Marcus Chen pushes a Slack message to the Zap from the #backend-team channel. The message reads: 'We're seeing repeated 500 errors on the /api/orders endpoint when order quantity exceeds 100 items. Stack trace shows a timeout in the database query layer. This is affecting roughly 15% of high-volume orders. We need to investigate and fix the query optimization.' Thread ID: 1701234567.890123, Channel ID: C04XYZABC12.

expect.action: A GitHub issue is created titled 'Fix 500 errors on /api/orders endpoint for high-volume orders' with a description detailing the timeout in the database query layer affecting 15% of high-volume orders, labeled 'bug', and a confirmation notification is posted to Slack thread 1701234567.890123 in channel C04XYZABC12 with the new GitHub issue URL.
expect.reason: slack-trigger forwards the message payload to issue-formatter, which generates a structured issue and passes it to github-issues for creation, then slack-notifications posts the confirmation back to the original thread.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify issue-formatter.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to issue-formatter.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify github-issues.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to github-issues.

name: Two-Sided Marketplace Email Connection
raw_steps: Store service and vendor information in a Table when a new service is created through a form.
Display the submitted services in an Interface with a table layout for clients to browse.
When a potential client selects a service from a dropdown form, automatically send an email to the client while BCC'ing the vendor.
Keep the vendor's email hidden from the client unless the vendor replies to establish direct contact.

objects.role: Entry-point write service that captures new service submissions from vendors via a Zapier Form and forwards them for storage and display.
objects.state_description: Tracks all submitted service form entries including service name, description, vendor name, and vendor email.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, forward the service details to the service-store and marketplace-connection peers.

peers.relationship: Send new service submission data for persistent storage
Forward new service details so the marketplace display can be updated

event_sources: external system: Zapier Form submission for new service creation

objects.role: Write service that persistently stores service and vendor information in a Zapier Table.
objects.state_description: Stores all service records including service name, description, vendor name, vendor email, and creation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new service record arrives, write it to the Zapier Table. Maintain the complete list of available services and vendor details. Vendor email must be stored but treated as confidential and not exposed in outbound client communications.

objects.role: Dual-role service object that displays available services in a Zapier Interface table for clients to browse and captures client service-selection form submissions.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current list of displayed services and incoming client selection events including selected service and client email.
objects.behavior: When updated service data arrives, refresh the table display in the Zapier Interface. When a client submits a service-selection form (choosing a service from a dropdown and providing their email), forward the selection event to the marketplace-connection peer.

peers.relationship: Forward client service-selection events including selected service name and client email

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interface client service-selection form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces the two-sided marketplace connection rules, determining how clients and vendors are introduced while keeping vendor email confidential.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending and completed connection requests including client email, selected service, vendor email, and connection status.
objects.behavior: When a new service is submitted, ensure the marketplace display is kept current with the latest service offerings. When a client selects a service, look up the corresponding vendor details from the service-store. Apply connection policy: the client must receive an introduction email that describes the selected service and provides contact instructions, but the vendor email must never appear in the To or body fields visible to the client — the vendor is BCC'd so they can see the client inquiry and choose to reply directly to establish contact. Compose the outbound email with the client in the To field and the vendor in BCC. The vendor's email remains hidden from the client unless the vendor replies directly.

peers.relationship: Query for vendor details matching the selected service when a client selection event arrives
Send composed connection email with client in To and vendor in BCC

objects.role: Write service that sends outbound emails via Gmail, supporting To and BCC addressing for marketplace connection introductions.
objects.state_description: Tracks all sent emails including recipient, BCC addresses, subject, body, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an email send request arrives containing recipient address, BCC address, subject, and body, dispatch the email via Gmail with the specified To and BCC fields. Record the sent email in state. Never expose BCC recipients in the visible email headers.

steps.text: Vendor Marcus Webb submits a new service listing via the Zapier Form with the following details: Service Name: 'Professional Logo Design', Category: 'Graphic Design', Description: 'Custom logo design with 3 revision rounds, delivered in all standard formats', Price: '$350', Turnaround: '5 business days', Vendor Name: Marcus Webb, Vendor Email: marcus.webb@designstudio.com

expect.action: Service record for 'Professional Logo Design' by Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@designstudio.com) written to the Zapier Table via service-store, and marketplace-display refreshed with the updated service listing.
expect.reason: service-form forwards new submission to service-store (which writes to Zapier Table) and marketplace-connection (which ensures marketplace-display is updated with the latest service offerings).

steps.text: Client Sophie Harrington submits the service-selection form on the Zapier Interface, selecting 'Professional Logo Design' from the dropdown and providing her email: sophie.harrington@clientco.com

expect.action: Gmail email sent To: sophie.harrington@clientco.com and BCC: marcus.webb@designstudio.com with subject and body introducing Sophie Harrington to the 'Professional Logo Design' service, without revealing marcus.webb@designstudio.com to Sophie.
expect.reason: marketplace-display forwards the client selection to marketplace-connection, which looks up vendor details from service-store and instructs gmail-sender to send an introduction email To the client and BCC the vendor, keeping the vendor email confidential.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from service-store data. Stores all service records including service name, description, vendor name, vendor email, and creation timestamp.
mock_tools.response_template: {"service_store_data": [{"id": "SVC-10047", "status": "active", "created_at": "2024-11-14T09:22:35Z", "service_name": "Professional Logo Design", "category": "Graphic Design", "description": "Custom logo design with 3 revision rounds, delivered in all standard formats", "price": "$350", "turnaround": "5 business days", "vendor_name": "Marcus Webb", "vendor_email": "marcus.webb@designstudio.com", "submission_source": "Zapier Form", "listing_status": "published"}, {"id": "SEL-20183", "status": "submitted", "created_at": "2024-11-14T11:47:52Z", "service_name": "Professional Logo Design", "service_id_ref": "SVC-10047", "client_name": "Sophie Harrington", "client_email": "sophie.harrington@clientco.com", "submission_source": "Zapier Interface", "vendor_name": "Marcus Webb", "vendor_email": "marcus.webb@designstudio.com", "selection_status": "pending_confirmation"}]}

name: Gong Call to AI Follow-up Automation
raw_steps: Trigger automation when a Gong call transcript is processed.
Use AI by Zapier to analyze the call summary and transcript and generate a personalized email subject line and HTML body.
Create a draft email in Gmail addressed to the first external participant with other attendees CC'd and the sales rep as sender.
Create a new task in HubSpot containing the call summary, Gmail draft link, and a 24-hour deadline.
Send a Slack direct message to the assigned sales rep with the email preview and direct links to the Gmail draft and HubSpot task.
Sales rep reviews the AI-generated content and sends the email when ready.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives processed Gong call transcripts and summaries and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stores received Gong call payloads including transcript, summary, and participant metadata.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call transcript is processed, forward the full payload (transcript, summary, participant list including external participants and sales rep) to the call-follow-up-engine peer.

peers.relationship: Forward Gong call payload (transcript, summary, participants) when a new processed call transcript arrives

event_sources: webhook: Gong call transcript processed

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates AI content generation and coordinates downstream actions for post-call follow-up workflows.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight follow-up jobs keyed by call ID, including generated subject line, HTML email body, identified primary external recipient, CC list, sales rep identity, and status of downstream actions.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call payload arrives, extract the first external participant as the primary recipient, all other external attendees as CC recipients, and the sales rep as sender. Send the transcript and summary to ai-content-generator to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML body. Once AI content is ready, simultaneously send draft email instructions to gmail-drafts and task creation instructions to hubspot-tasks. Once the Gmail draft link is available (retrieved via gmail_drafts_data tool) and the HubSpot task link is available (retrieved via hubspot_tasks_data tool), send a Slack notification to slack-notifications containing the email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link addressed to the sales rep.

peers.relationship: Send call transcript and summary to generate personalized email subject line and HTML body
Send draft creation instructions including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, and HTML body once AI content is generated
Send task creation instructions including call summary, Gmail draft link, and 24-hour deadline once AI content is generated
Send Slack DM to sales rep with email preview and direct links to Gmail draft and HubSpot task once both are created

objects.role: Read service that uses AI by Zapier to analyze call summaries and transcripts and generate personalized email subject lines and HTML bodies.
objects.state_description: Logs generation requests and returned AI-generated content keyed by call ID.
objects.behavior: When a generation request arrives containing a call transcript and summary, invoke AI by Zapier to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML email body. When answering queries, call the `ai_content_generator_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates draft emails in Gmail with specified recipients, CC, sender, subject, and HTML body.
objects.state_description: Audit log of draft creation requests including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, body, and returned Gmail draft link.
objects.behavior: When a draft creation instruction arrives, create a Gmail draft addressed to the primary external recipient with other attendees CC'd, the sales rep as sender, and the provided subject and HTML body. Record the resulting Gmail draft link in state.

objects.role: Write service that creates tasks in HubSpot containing call summaries, Gmail draft links, and deadlines.
objects.state_description: Audit log of task creation requests including call summary, Gmail draft link, deadline, and returned HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a task creation instruction arrives, create a new HubSpot task containing the call summary, the Gmail draft link, and a 24-hour deadline from the time of creation. Record the resulting HubSpot task link in state.

objects.role: Write service that sends Slack direct messages to sales reps with email previews and action links.
objects.state_description: Log of Slack DMs sent including recipient sales rep, email preview content, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a notification instruction arrives, send a Slack direct message to the specified sales rep containing the AI-generated email preview, a direct link to the Gmail draft, and a direct link to the HubSpot task. Record the sent message in state.

steps.text: Gong call transcript processed for a call between Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive, sales rep) and two external participants: David Okafor (VP of Procurement, Acme Corp, primary contact) and Linda Tran (Director of Operations, Acme Corp). Call summary: Discussed Q3 renewal terms, pricing adjustments, and a potential upsell to the enterprise tier. David raised concerns about onboarding timelines. Next steps include sending a follow-up proposal. Full transcript: 'Sarah: Thanks for joining today. David: Happy to be here. We wanted to revisit the renewal terms... [full transcript continues]'. Participant emails: david.okafor@acmecorp.com (primary), linda.tran@acmecorp.com (CC), sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com (sender/sales rep). Sales rep Slack user ID: @sarah.mitchell.

expect.action: Slack direct message sent to @sarah.mitchell containing the AI-generated email preview with personalized subject line and HTML body, a direct link to the Gmail draft addressed to david.okafor@acmecorp.com, and a direct link to the newly created HubSpot task with a 24-hour deadline summarizing the Acme Corp Q3 renewal call.
expect.reason: gong-calls forwards the payload to call-follow-up-engine, which orchestrates AI content generation via ai-content-generator, creates a Gmail draft via gmail-drafts, creates a HubSpot task via hubspot-tasks, and triggers slack-notifications to send the DM to the sales rep.

name: Employee Onboarding AI Chatbot
raw_steps: Configure data sources (files, tables, or web pages) as the chatbot's knowledge base for accurate responses.
Set up the OpenAI-powered chatbot to respond to employee questions using the configured data sources.
Add actions to chatbot buttons that allow employees to copy responses or trigger Zaps when clicked.
Deploy the chatbot as a standalone experience or embed it in another location via a premium plan.
Share the HR chatbot with employees for onboarding support.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the company intranet chatbot interface that receives employee questions and button-click actions
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming employee questions, generated responses, and button-click events (copy or log-to-sheet)
objects.behavior: When an employee submits a question via the chatbot interface, forward it to the onboarding-knowledge object for processing. When an employee clicks the 'copy' button, handle the copy action locally. When an employee clicks the 'log question' button, forward the question and response details to the question-logger object.

peers.relationship: Forward employee question when submitted via chatbot
Forward question and response details when the log-question button is clicked

event_sources: user action: employee submits question via chatbot
user action: employee clicks log-question button

objects.role: Business logic object that applies onboarding domain knowledge to answer employee questions using the configured knowledge base sources
objects.state_description: Tracks current conversation context, pending queries, and assembled responses for active employee sessions
objects.behavior: When an employee question arrives, determine which knowledge sources are relevant (handbook, benefits, policies). Query the employee-handbook, benefits-overview, and company-policies read services as needed. Synthesize a coherent, accurate answer using the retrieved content. When the question relates to HR policies, prioritize company-policies data. When the question relates to benefits, prioritize benefits-overview data. When the question relates to general onboarding procedures, prioritize employee-handbook data. Send the composed response back to the intranet-chatbot for display to the employee.

peers.relationship: Query for onboarding procedures and general HR information when employee question is relevant
Query for benefits details when employee question is relevant
Query for policy details when employee question is relevant
Send composed answer response for display to the employee

objects.role: Read service representing the Employee Handbook PDF knowledge base used to answer onboarding and HR procedure questions
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about onboarding procedures, HR policies, and general employment information sourced from the Employee Handbook PDF. When answering queries, call the `employee_handbook_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service representing the Benefits Overview spreadsheet used to answer questions about employee benefits
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about employee benefits, coverage details, enrollment procedures, and compensation packages sourced from the Benefits Overview spreadsheet. When answering queries, call the `benefits_overview_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service representing the Company Policies web page used to answer questions about workplace rules and compliance
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about company rules, compliance requirements, code of conduct, and workplace policies sourced from the Company Policies web page. When answering queries, call the `company_policies_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that records employee questions and chatbot responses to a Google Sheets tracking sheet for HR analytics
objects.state_description: Audit trail of logged questions including employee identifier, question text, chatbot response, timestamp, and session metadata
objects.behavior: When a log-question event arrives containing question text, response text, and session metadata, record the entry to the Google Sheets tracking sheet. Append a new row with the employee identifier, question, response, and timestamp.

steps.text: New employee Sarah Chen submits the question 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?' via the company intranet chatbot during onboarding.

expect.action: Response delivered to Sarah Chen via the intranet chatbot stating the number of vacation days in the first year, sourced from the Employee Handbook PDF and/or Benefits Overview spreadsheet knowledge base.
expect.reason: intranet-chatbot forwards the question to onboarding-knowledge, which queries employee-handbook and benefits-overview, then returns the answer back to intranet-chatbot for display to the employee.

steps.text: New employee Sarah Chen clicks the 'log question' button in the intranet chatbot after receiving the answer to 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?', with response text 'Full-time employees receive 10 vacation days in their first year of employment.' and session ID 'session-20240315-sc001'.

expect.action: A new row appended to the Google Sheets tracking sheet recording employee Sarah Chen, question 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?', response 'Full-time employees receive 10 vacation days in their first year of employment.', and session ID 'session-20240315-sc001'.
expect.reason: intranet-chatbot forwards the log-question event to question-logger, which appends the entry to the Google Sheets tracking sheet.

name: Jira Epic to GitLab Release Notes
raw_steps: Monitor Jira workspace for epic status changes.
Filter to ensure only epic-level issues trigger the workflow.
Analyze epic details using AI to generate professional Markdown release notes.
Create a new Git branch for the release note update.
Fetch the current release notes file from the repository.
Merge new content at the top of existing release notes.
Commit changes with a descriptive message linking back to the epic.
Open a merge request automatically for team review and approval.
Look up the epic assignee in Slack workspace.
Send a notification to the responsible team member requesting review.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Jira workspace for epic status changes to Done and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Jira issue status-change webhook payloads received since startup.
objects.behavior: When a Jira issue status-change event arrives, forward the full event payload to the release-notes-generator peer. Only forward events where the issue type is Epic and the new status is Done.

peers.relationship: Forward epic-completed event payload when an epic issue transitions to Done status

event_sources: webhook: Jira issue status changed

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes completed Jira epics and orchestrates generation, publication, and notification of release notes.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current epic being processed including epic key, title, description, assignee, generated release notes content, branch name, merge request URL, and processing stage.
objects.behavior: When an epic-completed event arrives, verify the issue is of type Epic and status is Done. Use the generate-markdown-release-notes skill to call OpenAI and produce professional Markdown release notes from the epic details. Construct the branch name as release-notes/epic-update. Send the generated content and branch name to gitlab-repo to create the branch, fetch CHANGELOG.md, merge new content at the top, commit with a message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Simultaneously look up the epic assignee's Slack profile via the slack-directory read service. Once the merge request URL is available and the assignee Slack user ID is resolved, send a direct Slack message via slack-notifications to the assignee requesting review of the merge request.

peers.relationship: Send branch name, generated release notes content, epic key, and epic title to create branch, update CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request
Look up the Slack profile of the epic assignee by name or email to resolve their Slack user ID
Send a direct Slack message to the epic assignee with the merge request URL requesting review

objects.role: Write service object representing the GitLab repository that creates branches, updates CHANGELOG.md, commits changes, and opens merge requests.
objects.state_description: Records of branch creation events, CHANGELOG.md fetch-and-update operations, commits made, and merge requests opened, keyed by epic key.
objects.behavior: When a request arrives with branch name, release notes content, epic key, and epic title: create the specified branch from main, fetch the current CHANGELOG.md from main, prepend the new release notes content to the existing CHANGELOG.md, commit the updated file with a descriptive message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Record each operation in state.

objects.role: Read service object that looks up Slack user profiles by name or email to resolve Slack user IDs.
objects.behavior: When answering queries, call the slack_directory_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that sends direct Slack messages to specified users.
objects.state_description: Log of direct messages sent including recipient user ID, message content, timestamp, and associated epic key.
objects.behavior: When a send-direct-message request arrives with a Slack user ID and message content, deliver the direct message to the specified user via Slack. Record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration' assigned to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Mobile Payment Gateway Integration. Epic key: PROJ-142.

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Sofia Reyes requesting review of the release notes merge request for PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration, including a link to the GitLab merge request targeting the main branch.
expect.reason: jira-events forwards the epic-completed event to release-notes-generator, which generates release notes via OpenAI, instructs gitlab-repo to create the release-notes/epic-update branch, prepend CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request, then looks up Sofia Reyes via slack-directory and sends her a direct message via slack-notifications.

name: Granola to Asana Task Tracker
raw_steps: Trigger the workflow when a Granola meeting ends.
Use AI to extract actionable tasks from the meeting notes.
Create tasks in Asana with context, priorities, and assignments based on the extracted action items.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives webhook events when a Granola meeting ends and forwards meeting notes to the task extraction logic.
objects.state_description: Tracks received meeting-end events including meeting ID, title, timestamp, and raw notes content.
objects.behavior: When a Granola meeting-end event arrives, forward the meeting ID, title, timestamp, and full meeting notes to the task-extraction peer.

peers.relationship: Forward meeting ID, title, timestamp, and raw meeting notes when a meeting-end event is received

event_sources: webhook: granola meeting ended

objects.role: Business logic object that uses AI to parse meeting notes and extract actionable tasks with priorities and assignees.
objects.state_description: Tracks extracted task lists per meeting, including task descriptions, inferred priorities (High/Medium/Low), context snippets, and assigned team member names.
objects.behavior: When meeting notes arrive, analyze the content to identify all actionable items. For each action item, extract: a clear task description, relevant context from the meeting, a priority level (High for urgent/blocking items, Medium for important but non-blocking items, Low for nice-to-have items), and the name of the team member responsible based on explicit mentions or contextual inference. Once all tasks are extracted and enriched, look up each assignee in the team directory to resolve their Asana user ID by querying the team-directory peer. After receiving resolved assignee IDs, send all tasks with full context, priority, and assignee IDs to the asana-tasks peer.

peers.relationship: Query for Asana user IDs for each extracted assignee name after tasks are identified
Send all extracted tasks with description, context, priority, and resolved assignee IDs for creation in Asana

objects.role: Read service that resolves team member names to their Asana user IDs and profile information.
objects.behavior: Respond to queries for team member Asana user IDs and profile details by name or email. When answering queries, call the `team_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates tasks in Asana with the provided description, context, priority, and assignee.
objects.state_description: Tracks all tasks created in Asana including task name, context, priority, assignee, Asana task ID, and creation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a task creation request arrives, record the task details including description, context, priority level, and assignee ID, then create the corresponding task in Asana via the Asana API.

steps.text: Granola meeting ended. Meeting ID: mtg_20240315_001. Title: Q2 Product Roadmap Planning. Timestamp: 2024-03-15T14:30:00Z. Meeting notes: Discussed Q2 product priorities and resource allocation. Action items identified: 1) Sarah Chen (Product Manager) to finalize the feature prioritization document for the engineering team by March 22nd — this is critical for sprint planning so mark as High priority. 2) Marcus Rivera (Engineering Lead) to review and estimate the new payment integration module and provide a timeline by March 20th — High priority as it blocks other work. 3) Priya Patel (Designer) to create mockups for the redesigned onboarding flow by March 29th — Medium priority. 4) James Okafor (QA Lead) to update the regression test suite to cover the new authentication changes by April 1st — Medium priority. 5) Linda Zhao (Marketing Manager) to draft the go-to-market messaging for the Q2 feature releases by April 5th — Low priority for now.

expect.action: Tasks created in Asana: (1) 'Finalize feature prioritization document' assigned to Sarah Chen with High priority and context about sprint planning deadline of March 22nd; (2) 'Review and estimate payment integration module' assigned to Marcus Rivera with High priority and context about blocking dependency, due March 20th; (3) 'Create mockups for redesigned onboarding flow' assigned to Priya Patel with Medium priority, due March 29th; (4) 'Update regression test suite for authentication changes' assigned to James Okafor with Medium priority, due April 1st; (5) 'Draft go-to-market messaging for Q2 feature releases' assigned to Linda Zhao with Low priority, due April 5th.
expect.reason: granola-meetings forwards meeting notes to task-extraction, which uses AI to extract action items and resolves assignees via team-directory, then asana-tasks creates each task in Asana with description, context, priority, and assignee ID.

name: Clapperboard Data Capture Automation
raw_steps: Upload a video file to the designated folder.
Trigger Zapier to process the video with Google Gemini 1.5 Flash.
Extract clapperboard details from the video using Gemini.
Automatically populate the extracted data into the Zapier table.

objects.role: Entry-point write service representing the Google Drive Production Footage folder that detects new video file uploads and triggers the automation.
objects.state_description: Tracks uploaded video files with their file names, upload timestamps, and Drive file IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new video file is uploaded to the Production Footage folder, forward the file reference and metadata to the clapperboard-extraction peer. Record each upload event in state.

peers.relationship: Forward video file reference and metadata when a new video is uploaded to the folder

event_sources: external system: Google Drive file uploaded to Production Footage folder

objects.role: Business logic object that applies Google Gemini 1.5 Flash to extract structured clapperboard data from uploaded video files.
objects.state_description: Tracks extraction jobs with input video file reference, extraction status, and extracted fields: scene number, take number, director name, and date.
objects.behavior: When a video file reference is received, invoke the Gemini 1.5 Flash model to analyze the video and extract clapperboard details including scene number, take number, director name, and date. If extraction succeeds, compile the structured data record and send it to the zapier-table write service. If extraction fails or required fields are missing, log the failure in state and do not forward incomplete records.

peers.relationship: Send extracted clapperboard data record (scene number, take number, director name, date, and source video reference) after successful extraction

objects.role: Write service representing the Zapier table that receives and persistently stores extracted clapperboard data records.
objects.state_description: Stores populated clapperboard data rows with scene number, take number, director name, date, source video reference, and row creation timestamp. Initially empty.
objects.behavior: When a clapperboard data record is received, append it as a new row in the Zapier table. Record each insertion in state as the audit trail.

steps.text: A new video file 'Scene_04_Take_02_DirectorJanePark_2024-11-15.mp4' has been uploaded to the Google Drive Production Footage folder by cinematographer Marcus Webb.

expect.action: Extracted clapperboard data record — scene number: 4, take number: 2, director name: Jane Park, date: 2024-11-15 — appended as a new row in the Zapier table.
expect.reason: production-footage forwards the file reference to clapperboard-extraction, which invokes Gemini 1.5 Flash to extract clapperboard details, then forwards the structured record to zapier-table, which appends it as a new row.

name: Team Call Prep Document Generator
raw_steps: When a team member clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page in the Team Dashboard Interface, trigger the automation.
Retrieve relevant customer information from the Zapier Customers Table.
Use AI by Zapier to analyze the customer data and generate call preparation insights.
Create a new document containing the call prep information.
Send a notification or email to the team member with a link to the generated document.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the Team Dashboard Interface that captures the Account Executive's 'Generate call prep' button click event
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming button-click events including the Account Executive identity and associated customer context
objects.behavior: When a 'Generate call prep' button click event arrives from the Team Dashboard Interface, forward the event payload (Account Executive identity, customer identifier) to the call-prep-workflow business logic object via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward the Account Executive identity and customer identifier when a 'Generate call prep' button click is received

event_sources: webhook: generate_call_prep button click event from Team Dashboard Interface

objects.role: Read service representing the Zapier Customers Table that retrieves customer records on demand
objects.behavior: Serves as the authoritative source for customer data including contact details, history, and account information. When answering queries, call the `customers_table_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates call preparation by retrieving customer data, generating insights, and coordinating document creation and notification
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress call prep requests including Account Executive identity, customer identifier, retrieved customer data, and generated insights
objects.behavior: When a call prep request arrives with an Account Executive identity and customer identifier, query the customers-table read service for the full customer record. Once customer data is available, apply AI analysis to generate call preparation insights including key talking points, recent activity, account health, and recommended next steps. Then send the customer data and generated insights to google-docs for document creation, and send the Account Executive email address and the call prep content summary to email-sender simultaneously.

peers.relationship: Query for full customer record using the customer identifier from the trigger event
Send generated call prep insights and customer context to create a new Google Doc
Send Account Executive email address and Google Doc link request to deliver the final notification

objects.role: Write service representing Google Docs that creates new documents containing call prep information and returns document links
objects.state_description: Tracks created Google Docs with document IDs, titles, content, and creation timestamps
objects.behavior: When call prep content is received, create a new Google Doc with the provided insights and customer information. Record the document ID and shareable link. Reply with the generated document URL so callers can include it in downstream notifications.

peers.relationship: Send the generated Google Doc URL so it can be included in the Account Executive notification email

objects.role: Write service representing an email delivery system that sends the Account Executive a link to the generated Google Doc
objects.state_description: Tracks sent emails with recipient, subject, body, Google Doc link, and send timestamp
objects.behavior: When an email request arrives containing an Account Executive email address and a Google Doc URL, compose and send an email to the Account Executive with the link to the generated call prep Google Doc. Record the sent email details.

steps.text: Account Executive Sarah Mitchell clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Pinnacle Solutions (customer ID: CUST-8821) in the Team Dashboard Interface. Sarah Mitchell's email is sarah.mitchell@company.com.

expect.action: Email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com containing a link to the newly created Google Doc with call prep insights for Pinnacle Solutions.
expect.reason: team-dashboard forwards the event to call-prep-workflow, which retrieves customer data from customers-table, generates insights, creates a Google Doc via google-docs, and triggers email-sender to deliver the doc link to the Account Executive.

name: AI Email Assistant Categorizer
raw_steps: When a new email arrives in Gmail, trigger the automation.
Use ChatGPT to categorize the email into one of: App Notifications, Action Required, Sales Emails, or Newsletters.
Use ChatGPT to generate a concise summary of the email content.
Store the email record, category, and summary in Zapier Tables.
Connect Gmail and ChatGPT accounts to the Zap.
Test and publish the Zap.
Optionally configure navigation and access controls for managed users.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for new incoming emails and triggers the automation workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks the most recently received email events including sender, subject, body, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Gmail, forward the full email payload (sender, subject, body, metadata) to the email-categorizer business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full email payload when a new email arrives in Gmail

event_sources: gmail: new email received

objects.role: Business logic object that applies categorization rules and generates summaries for incoming emails using ChatGPT.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current email being processed, its assigned category, and generated summary.
objects.behavior: When an email payload is received, determine its category by analyzing sender, subject, and body content — assign exactly one of: App Notifications (automated system messages), Action Required (emails demanding a response or task), Sales Emails (promotional or commercial offers), or Newsletters (subscription-based informational content). Then generate a concise 2-3 sentence summary capturing the key points of the email. Send the original email record, assigned category, and generated summary to email-record-store.

peers.relationship: Send email record, assigned category, and generated summary after processing is complete

objects.role: Write service object that persists email records, categories, and summaries into Zapier Tables.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all stored email records including original email data, category label, and summary text with timestamps.
objects.behavior: When an email record with category and summary is received, store all fields as a new row in Zapier Tables. Record the email sender, subject, body, assigned category, summary, and processing timestamp.

steps.text: A new email arrives in Gmail from Marcus Hale (marcus.hale@techcorp.io) with subject 'Q3 Contract Renewal – Action Needed by Friday' and body: 'Hi team, please review and sign the attached Q3 contract renewal before end of day Friday. Let me know if you have any questions. Thanks, Marcus.' The email was received at 2024-10-15T09:32:00Z.

expect.action: A new row is written to Zapier Tables containing: sender 'marcus.hale@techcorp.io (Marcus Hale)', subject 'Q3 Contract Renewal – Action Needed by Friday', body 'Hi team, please review and sign the attached Q3 contract renewal before end of day Friday. Let me know if you have any questions. Thanks, Marcus.', category 'Action Required', a concise ChatGPT-generated summary of the email content, and processing timestamp '2024-10-15T09:32:00Z'.
expect.reason: gmail-inbox forwards the email payload to email-categorizer, which assigns a category and generates a summary via ChatGPT, then passes the full record to email-record-store, which writes all fields as a new row in Zapier Tables.

name: Table Task Slack Update Automation
raw_steps: Store project tasks in a Zapier Table with details including category, owner, start date, end date, and notes.
Click a button within the Table to trigger the Zap.
The Zap automatically sends a Slack message with all task record details to the team.
Configure the Zap by authenticating your Slack account or selecting an alternative app from 6,000+ integrations.
Publish the Zap and use the Table to manage your project plan.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Table that stores project tasks and triggers the Zap when a button is clicked
objects.state_description: Tracks button-click trigger events received from the Zapier Table, including the task record details (category, owner, start date, end date, notes) that accompanied each trigger
objects.behavior: When a button-click trigger event arrives from the Zapier Table, forward the full task record payload to the task-update-policy peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full task record details (category, owner, start date, end date, notes) when a button-click trigger is received

event_sources: external system: Zapier Table button click with task record payload

objects.role: Business logic object that applies project task update rules, formats the Slack message content from the task record details, and routes the formatted message to the Slack channel
objects.state_description: Tracks the most recently processed task record details and the formatted Slack message generated for each trigger event
objects.behavior: When a task record payload arrives, extract all task fields: category, owner, start date, end date, and notes. Format a clear, readable Slack message summarizing all task details. Send the formatted message to the slack-channel peer.

peers.relationship: Send formatted Slack message with all task record details when a task trigger has been processed

objects.role: Write service object representing the Slack #project-updates channel that receives and posts task update messages
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all task update messages posted to the #project-updates channel, including timestamp and message content
objects.behavior: When a formatted task update message is received, post it to the #project-updates Slack channel. Record the message content and timestamp in the audit trail.

steps.text: Button clicked in Zapier Table for task record: Category: Product Launch, Owner: Maria Chen, Start Date: 2024-02-01, End Date: 2024-03-15, Notes: Coordinate with design and engineering teams for Q1 release milestone.

expect.action: Slack message posted to #project-updates channel summarizing task details: Category: Product Launch, Owner: Maria Chen, Start Date: 2024-02-01, End Date: 2024-03-15, Notes: Coordinate with design and engineering teams for Q1 release milestone.
expect.reason: zapier-table forwards the task record to task-update-policy, which formats the message and routes it to slack-channel, which posts it to #project-updates

name: Keyword-to-Blog Automated Publishing Pipeline
raw_steps: Input target keywords into the system to trigger the automated workflow.
Claude AI (Claude 3.5 Sonnet) generates SEO-optimized blog content including titles, meta descriptions, and expert-level written content based on the keywords.
DALL-E generates relevant featured images and supporting visuals to complement the written content.
Publish the completed blog post (content and images) to a Supabase database, which connects to your Lovable site or other compatible CMS.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives keyword submissions from Zapier and triggers the content generation pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted keyword payloads with timestamps and pipeline trigger status.
objects.behavior: When a keyword submission event arrives from Zapier, forward the keyword payload to the content-generation peer.

peers.relationship: Forward keyword payload when a new keyword submission event arrives

event_sources: webhook: Zapier keyword submission

objects.role: Business logic object that applies SEO content strategy rules to generate optimized blog titles, meta descriptions, and full article bodies from submitted keywords.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress content generation requests including keywords received, generated title, meta description, article body, and completion status.
objects.behavior: When keywords arrive, apply SEO optimization rules: generate a compelling, keyword-rich blog title, a meta description under 160 characters that includes the primary keyword, and a full article body with proper heading structure (H1, H2, H3), keyword density between 1-2%, introductory hook, supporting sections, and a conclusion with a call-to-action. Ensure content is original, informative, and structured for search engine indexing. When content is fully generated, send the keyword set and content brief to the image-generation peer simultaneously with sending the completed article content to the blog-publisher peer.

peers.relationship: Send keyword context and article content when blog content generation is complete
Send generated title, meta description, and article body when content generation is complete

objects.role: Business logic object that applies visual content rules to generate a featured hero image and two supporting inline images via DALL-E based on article content and keywords.
objects.state_description: Tracks image generation requests including article context received, hero image URL, inline image 1 URL, inline image 2 URL, and generation status.
objects.behavior: When article content and keywords arrive, derive three distinct image prompts: one hero image prompt that captures the overarching theme of the article with a wide-format composition suitable for a blog header, one inline image prompt for a mid-article supporting visual illustrating a key concept, and one inline image prompt for a secondary supporting visual near the article conclusion. Each prompt must be descriptive, photorealistic or illustrative as appropriate to the domain, and avoid text overlays. Generate all three images via DALL-E. When all three images are ready, send the hero image URL and two inline image URLs along with their positional metadata to the blog-publisher peer.

peers.relationship: Send hero image URL, inline image 1 URL, and inline image 2 URL with positional metadata when all three images are generated

objects.role: Business logic object that assembles the complete blog post from content and image components and routes the final post to the CMS write service.
objects.state_description: Tracks assembly state per post including received article content, received image assets, assembly completeness flag, and publish dispatch status.
objects.behavior: When article content arrives from content-generation and image assets arrive from image-generation, wait until both sets of data are present for the same pipeline run. Once both are available, assemble the final blog post: embed the hero image at the top of the post, insert inline image 1 after the second major section, insert inline image 2 before the conclusion, attach the meta description as post metadata, and set the title. Send the fully assembled blog post record to the supabase-cms peer.

peers.relationship: Send fully assembled blog post record including title, meta description, article body with embedded images when assembly is complete

objects.role: Write service object that records completed blog posts to the Supabase database connected to the Lovable site CMS.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all published blog posts including post title, meta description, article body, hero image URL, inline image URLs, publish timestamp, and post ID.
objects.behavior: When a fully assembled blog post record arrives, write it to the Supabase database as a new CMS entry with all fields including title, meta description, body content, hero image, inline images, and publication timestamp. Record the operation in the audit trail.

steps.text: Zapier submits a keyword payload with the target keyword 'sustainable home decor trends' to trigger the content generation pipeline.

expect.action: A fully assembled blog post record — including a keyword-rich title, a meta description under 160 characters containing 'sustainable home decor trends', a full article body, a hero image, and two supporting inline images — is written to the Supabase database as a new CMS entry on the Lovable site.
expect.reason: keyword-input forwards the keyword to content-generation, which generates SEO-optimized content and passes it to image-generation and blog-publisher; image-generation produces three DALL-E visuals and sends them to blog-publisher; blog-publisher assembles the complete post and routes it to supabase-cms, which writes the final record to the database.

name: LinkedIn Lead to Store Conversion Tracking
raw_steps: When a new lead is generated through a LinkedIn lead ad campaign, trigger a Zap.
Capture the lead information and log it in Zapier Tables as a central database.
Set up an interface to provide a user-friendly view of the lead database.
When a lead makes a purchase in the physical store, update their status in Zapier Tables through the interface.
When a purchase is recorded, trigger another Zap to send the conversion event back to LinkedIn.
LinkedIn receives the conversion data and refines future ad campaigns for better targeting.
Connect your LinkedIn account to Zapier.
Select your lead ad campaign in the Zap setup.
Customize the database and interface to match your business needs.
Train staff on updating purchase information in the interface.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead events from LinkedIn lead ad campaigns and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming LinkedIn lead event with timestamp, lead details, and campaign metadata.
objects.behavior: When a new lead is generated from a LinkedIn lead ad campaign, capture all lead fields (name, email, phone, campaign ID, etc.) and forward to the lead-conversion-tracking business logic object. Record the raw event payload as an audit entry.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new LinkedIn lead ad event arrives

event_sources: webhook: LinkedIn lead ad form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the lifecycle of a lead from initial capture through in-store purchase conversion, applying status rules and routing data to appropriate services.
objects.state_description: Tracks each lead's current status (new, contacted, purchased), associated campaign, and conversion timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from linkedin-leads, validate and enrich the lead record then send it to zapier-tables for storage. When a purchase update arrives from zapier-interface, update the lead status to 'purchased' in zapier-tables and simultaneously trigger a conversion event to linkedin-conversions. Apply the rule: only leads with a confirmed purchase event should be forwarded to linkedin-conversions. Never forward duplicate conversion events for the same lead.

peers.relationship: Send new lead records for storage when a lead is first captured; send status updates when a purchase is recorded
Send conversion event payload when a lead's purchase is confirmed
Notify interface of new lead records so staff can view and update them

objects.role: Write service that acts as the central persistent database for all lead records and their purchase statuses.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with fields: lead ID, name, contact info, campaign ID, status (new/contacted/purchased), capture timestamp, and purchase timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead record or status update is received, write it to the Zapier Tables database. Record every write operation as an audit trail entry. Accept both insert (new lead) and update (status change) operations keyed by lead ID.

objects.role: Dual-role service object that renders the lead database for store staff and receives in-store purchase update actions from staff, forwarding them to the business logic object.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current view state of the lead database interface and logs all staff purchase update actions.
objects.behavior: Display lead records sourced from zapier-tables to store staff in a user-friendly view. When a staff member records an in-store purchase for a lead through the interface, forward the purchase update event (lead ID, purchase details, timestamp) to lead-conversion-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward purchase update events when store staff records an in-store purchase for a lead

event_sources: user action: store staff submits purchase update via Zapier Interface

objects.role: Write service that receives conversion events and sends them back to LinkedIn to refine future ad campaign targeting.
objects.state_description: Logs all conversion events sent to LinkedIn including lead ID, campaign ID, conversion type, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a conversion event is received, transmit it to the LinkedIn Conversions API to register the offline purchase conversion against the originating lead ad campaign. Record each submission as an audit log entry with response status.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via LinkedIn lead ad campaign. Lead details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@email.com, Phone: 555-203-4871, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241, Campaign Name: Spring Retail Awareness 2024, Job Title: Marketing Manager, Company: Brightfield Co.

expect.action: Lead record for Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@email.com, 555-203-4871, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241) written to Zapier Tables as a new insert entry with status 'lead captured', and an audit trail entry recorded for the insert operation.
expect.reason: linkedin-leads forwards the lead to lead-conversion-tracking, which validates and enriches the record then sends it to zapier-tables for storage; zapier-tables records the insert and audit trail.

steps.text: Store staff member records an in-store purchase for lead Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@email.com) via the Zapier Interface. Purchase details: Order ID: ORD-88821, Product: Brightfield Spring Bundle, Amount: $149.99, Purchase Date: 2024-05-10, Store Location: Downtown Retail - Main St.
steps.source: user action: store staff submits purchase update via Zapier Interface

expect.action: Conversion event for Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@email.com, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241, Order ID: ORD-88821, Amount: $149.99) transmitted to LinkedIn Conversions API, and an audit trail entry recorded for the submission; lead status updated to 'purchased' in Zapier Tables with an audit trail entry for the update.
expect.reason: zapier-interface forwards the purchase update to lead-conversion-tracking, which updates the lead status in zapier-tables and triggers linkedin-conversions to send the conversion event to the LinkedIn Conversions API.

name: Facebook Content Calendar Automation
raw_steps: Add post details (content, images, date, time) to a user interface.
Store all submitted post information in Zapier Tables as a centralized database.
Display the content calendar in another interface for viewing and editing posts.
Check the database daily for posts scheduled to go live.
Pull scheduled post content from the database and publish it to Facebook.
Update the corresponding database record to mark the post as published.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interface form where users submit post details including content, images, date, and time.
objects.state_description: Records of all submitted form entries including content, images, scheduled date and time.
objects.behavior: When a new post submission arrives via the form, forward the full post details to the content-calendar-scheduler peer.

peers.relationship: Forward submitted post details (content, images, date, time) when a new form submission arrives.

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interface form submission

objects.role: Entry-point service object that fires a daily trigger to initiate checking for posts scheduled to go live.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last triggered timestamp for daily schedule checks.
objects.behavior: When the daily scheduled trigger fires, notify the content-calendar-scheduler to check for posts due to be published.

peers.relationship: Send daily check signal to trigger lookup of posts scheduled for today.

event_sources: scheduled trigger: daily interval

objects.role: Dual-role read/write service object representing Zapier Tables, storing all post records and answering queries about scheduled posts.
objects.state_description: All stored post records with fields: content, images, scheduled date, scheduled time, published status, record ID.
objects.behavior: Store new post records when write requests arrive. When queried for posts scheduled for a given date or for a specific record, call the post_database_data tool to retrieve matching records. When answering queries, call the `post_database_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Return queried post records (scheduled posts for today or specific record lookups) in response to read requests.

objects.role: Write service object representing the second Zapier Interface used to display and edit the content calendar.
objects.state_description: Current state of displayed posts including all edits made via the calendar interface.
objects.behavior: When updated post data is received, record and display it in the content calendar view. Accept edit events from users and store updated post details.

objects.role: Write service object representing the connected Facebook Page, responsible for publishing posts.
objects.state_description: Log of all posts published to the Facebook Page including content, images, and publish timestamp.
objects.behavior: When post content and images are received, publish them to the connected Facebook Page and record the publish event in the log.

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the content calendar lifecycle: storing new submissions, surfacing them in the calendar view, detecting posts due for publishing, triggering publication, and marking posts as published.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight post scheduling operations including pending publishes and records awaiting status update.
objects.behavior: When new post details arrive from the submission form, send the post record to post-database for storage and send the updated calendar data to content-calendar-view for display. When a daily check signal arrives, query post-database for all posts scheduled for today that are not yet published. For each retrieved post, send the post content and images to facebook-publisher and send an update request to post-database to mark the corresponding record as published. Send updates to content-calendar-view to reflect the newly published status.

peers.relationship: Send new post records for storage on form submission; send update requests to mark posts as published after publication; query for posts scheduled for today on daily trigger.
Send updated calendar data when new posts are added or when posts are marked as published.
Send post content and images for each post due to be published today.

steps.text: A new post submission has arrived from the Zapier Interface form. Post details: Author: Maria Gonzalez, Content: 'Exciting news — our summer sale starts Friday! Up to 40% off all items. Don't miss out! 🎉', Image: summer_sale_banner.jpg, Scheduled Date: 2024-07-12, Scheduled Time: 10:00 AM EST.

expect.action: Post record for Maria Gonzalez stored in post-database with content 'Exciting news — our summer sale starts Friday! Up to 40% off all items. Don't miss out! 🎉', image summer_sale_banner.jpg, scheduled for 2024-07-12 at 10:00 AM EST; content-calendar-view updated to display the new post entry.
expect.reason: content-calendar-scheduler receives post details from post-submission-form, writes the record to post-database, and sends updated calendar data to content-calendar-view

steps.text: Daily scheduled trigger fires to check for posts due to be published today.
