name: Facebook Lead Tracker and Email
raw_steps: Capture a new lead from Facebook Lead Ads.
Create a new row in a Zapier Table with the lead information.
Wait for a user to click a button in the Zapier Table.
Send an email using Email by Zapier.
Update the record in the Zapier Table with the sent date.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures new leads from Facebook Lead Ads and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks received lead events including name, email, phone number, and submission date.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from Facebook Lead Ads, forward the lead payload (name, email, phone number, submission date) to the lead-tracker business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward new lead data (name, email, phone number, submission date) when a new Facebook lead is captured.

event_sources: webhook: Facebook Lead Ads new lead submission

objects.role: Business logic object that manages lead lifecycle, decides when to create records, and coordinates follow-up email actions based on sales rep triggers.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their name, email, phone number, submission date, follow-up status, and whether a follow-up email has been sent.
objects.behavior: When a new lead payload arrives from facebook-leads, send the lead information to zapier-tables to create a new row. When a button-click follow-up event arrives from zapier-tables indicating a sales rep has triggered follow-up for a specific lead, send a follow-up email request for that lead to email-sender. Simultaneously, send an update to zapier-tables with the follow-up email sent date for that lead record.

peers.relationship: Send new lead data to create a row when a lead is first captured; send follow-up sent date to update the record after email is dispatched.
Send follow-up email request with lead contact details when a sales rep triggers follow-up via the Zapier Table button.

objects.role: Dual-role service object that stores lead records in a Zapier Table and forwards sales rep button-click events to the business logic object.
objects.state_description: Stores lead rows with name, email, phone number, submission date, and follow-up email sent date; tracks button-click trigger events from sales reps.
objects.behavior: When a new lead record write request arrives, store it as a new row in the Zapier Table. When an update request arrives with a follow-up sent date, update the matching lead record. When a sales representative clicks the follow-up button in the Zapier Table, forward the button-click event (including the associated lead record details) to lead-tracker via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward button-click follow-up trigger events from sales reps along with the associated lead record data.

event_sources: user action: sales rep clicks follow-up button in Zapier Table

objects.role: Write service object that sends follow-up emails to leads using Email by Zapier on behalf of the sales team.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all follow-up emails sent, including recipient name, email address, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a follow-up email request arrives with lead contact details, send a follow-up email to the lead's email address using Email by Zapier with a sales team follow-up message.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via Facebook Lead Ads. Lead details: Name: Maria Gonzalez, Email: maria.gonzalez@email.com, Phone: 555-234-5678, Submission Date: 2024-06-10.

expect.action: A new row is created in the Zapier Table with the following lead information: Name: Maria Gonzalez, Email: maria.gonzalez@email.com, Phone: 555-234-5678, Submission Date: 2024-06-10.
expect.reason: facebook-leads forwards the lead payload to lead-tracker, which sends the lead information to zapier-tables to create a new row.

steps.text: Sales representative David Kim has clicked the follow-up button for the lead record belonging to Maria Gonzalez (email: maria.gonzalez@email.com) in the Zapier Table.

expect.action: A follow-up email is sent via Email by Zapier to maria.gonzalez@email.com with a sales team follow-up message, and the Zapier Table record for Maria Gonzalez is updated with the follow-up sent date.
expect.reason: zapier-tables forwards the button-click event to lead-tracker, which instructs email-sender to send the follow-up email and then instructs zapier-tables to update the record with the sent date.

name: Out-of-Office Task Tracking System
raw_steps: Set your OOO status using the custom interface to trigger a Zap that notifies your team and activates the task-tracking system.
Monitor Slack for Reacjis (emoji reactions) on messages during your absence.
Connect Reacjis to Tables to create a database of tasks and requests submitted while you are away.
Access the interface upon your return to view the accumulated tasks stored in the database.
Update task status directly in the interface as you work through them.
Trigger notifications to original requesters via Slack when you update task status.
View a visual representation of the entire workflow in Canvas to see how each component works together.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the Zapier Interfaces OOO status form that captures the user's out-of-office activation trigger.
objects.state_description: Records submitted OOO form entries including submitter identity and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO form submission arrives, forward the event to the ooo-status-policy business logic object. Record each submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward OOO activation event with submitter details when a form is submitted.

event_sources: webhook: Zapier Interfaces form submission

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the #general Slack channel, both receiving outbound notifications and forwarding inbound Reacji (emoji reaction) events.
objects.state_description: Tracks inbound Reacji events (message ID, emoji, reactor, timestamp) and outbound notifications sent to the channel.
objects.behavior: When a Reacji (emoji reaction) event arrives on a message in #general, forward it to the task-tracker business logic object via peer message. When a notification message is received from a peer, post it to the #general Slack channel.

peers.relationship: Forward Reacji event details (message ID, emoji, reactor, original message content, timestamp) when an emoji reaction is detected on a message.

event_sources: external system: Slack Reacji (emoji reaction) on #general message

objects.role: Write service representing the Engineering Team Slack channel that receives OOO status notifications.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of OOO notifications sent to the Engineering Team, including message content and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification message is received, post it to the Engineering Team Slack channel and record it in state.

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces OOO activation rules, notifies the Engineering Team, and activates the task-tracking system.
objects.state_description: Tracks current OOO status (active/inactive), the active user's identity, and activation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO activation event arrives from the form: set OOO status to active for the submitting user, compose an Engineering Team notification message announcing the user's absence, and activate task tracking mode. Send the notification to engineering-team-slack and the activation signal to task-tracker simultaneously.

peers.relationship: Send OOO notification message to the Engineering Team when OOO is activated.
Send OOO activation signal with user identity when OOO status becomes active.

objects.role: Business logic object that manages task creation, status transitions, and requester notifications for tasks submitted during OOO absence.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether OOO mode is active, pending task queue with task ID, original message, requester, emoji trigger, status (Pending/In Progress/Complete), and last updated timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO activation signal arrives, enable task-capture mode. When a Reacji event arrives while OOO mode is active, create a new task record with status Pending, capturing the original message, requester identity, emoji, and timestamp, then write it to task-store. When a task status update arrives from the ooo-dashboard (Pending, In Progress, or Complete), update the task record in task-store and compose a status notification to the original requester, then send the notification to requester-notifier. When OOO mode is deactivated, disable task-capture mode.

peers.relationship: Write new task records when Reacji events are captured and update existing task records when status changes.
Send requester notification with updated task status when a task status change is processed.

objects.role: Dual-role write and read service representing the Zapier Tables database that stores tasks and supports dashboard queries.
objects.state_description: Database of task records with fields: task ID, original message, requester, emoji trigger, status (Pending/In Progress/Complete), created timestamp, updated timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new task record is received, insert it into the Zapier Tables database and record it in state. When a task update is received, update the matching record in the database. When answering queries, call the task_store_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Interfaces dashboard that displays accumulated tasks and captures status update actions from the returning user.
objects.state_description: Tracks dashboard view sessions and status update actions submitted by the user (task ID, new status, timestamp).
objects.behavior: When a status update action is submitted by the user on the dashboard (setting a task to Pending, In Progress, or Complete), forward the update event to task-tracker. When a dashboard view request arrives, retrieve current tasks from task-store and render them for the user.

peers.relationship: Forward task status update events (task ID, new status) when the user updates a task on the dashboard.
Query task records to populate the dashboard view when a user accesses the dashboard.

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interfaces dashboard status update action

objects.role: Write service representing the Slack notification system that sends direct messages or channel messages to original task requesters about status updates.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of requester notifications sent, including recipient, task ID, status, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a requester notification is received, send a Slack message to the original requester with the task status update and record it in state.

steps.text: Sarah Chen, Software Engineer, submits the OOO status form indicating she will be out of office starting Monday June 9 through Friday June 13, 2025.

expect.action: Notification posted to Engineering Team Slack channel announcing Sarah Chen's absence from June 9 to June 13, 2025; task-capture mode activated in task-tracker.
expect.reason: ooo-form forwards submission to ooo-status-policy, which notifies engineering-team-slack and activates task-tracker via peer messages.

steps.text: User @marcus_lee reacts with a 📋 emoji (Reacji) to a message in #general from @priya_patel (message text: 'Can someone review the API rate-limiting PR #482 when they get a chance?', message timestamp: 2025-06-10T14:23:00Z, channel: #general).

expect.action: New task record inserted into Zapier Tables database with status Pending, capturing original message 'Can someone review the API rate-limiting PR #482 when they get a chance?' from @priya_patel, requester @priya_patel, Reacji by @marcus_lee, and timestamp 2025-06-10T14:23:00Z.
expect.reason: slack-general-channel forwards Reacji event to task-tracker, which creates a new task record and writes it to task-store via peer message.

steps.text: Sarah Chen returns from OOO and updates task for 'API rate-limiting PR #482 review' (task ID: TASK-001) to status 'In Progress' via the Zapier Interfaces dashboard.
steps.source: Zapier Interfaces dashboard status update action

expect.action: Slack message sent to @priya_patel notifying that task 'Can someone review the API rate-limiting PR #482 when they get a chance?' has been updated to status 'In Progress' by Sarah Chen; task record TASK-001 updated in Zapier Tables database.
expect.reason: ooo-dashboard forwards status update to task-tracker, which updates task-store and triggers requester-notifier to send a Slack message to the original requester @priya_patel.

name: AI-Powered Form Data Extraction
raw_steps: Users submit data through a Zapier Interface form.
The form submission triggers a Zap that sends the combined fields to ChatGPT for processing.
ChatGPT extracts and structures the data into twelve separate fields such as First Name, Last Name, and Company Name.
The extracted and formatted data is stored in a Zapier Table for access and management.
The form URL is shared or embedded on a website to receive ongoing submissions.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interface form that collects contact and company information from users.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming form submissions with their raw combined field payloads and submission timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a form submission event arrives, forward the combined form payload to the data-extraction peer for processing.

peers.relationship: Forward the raw combined form submission payload when a new form entry is received.

event_sources: webhook: Zapier Interface form submission

objects.role: Business logic object responsible for parsing and structuring raw form submissions into twelve standardized contact and company fields using ChatGPT.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight extraction requests with their raw input and the resulting structured twelve-field output per submission.
objects.behavior: When a raw form submission payload arrives, invoke ChatGPT to extract and structure the data into exactly twelve fields: First Name, Last Name, Company Name, Job Title, Email Address, Phone Number, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Country, and Industry. Validate that all twelve fields are present in the response; if any are missing, set them to an empty string. Once extraction is complete, send the structured twelve-field record to the contact-store for persistence.

peers.relationship: Send the fully structured twelve-field contact record after successful extraction and formatting.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table that persists structured contact and company records for ongoing access and management.
objects.state_description: Accumulates all stored contact records, each containing the twelve structured fields: First Name, Last Name, Company Name, Job Title, Email Address, Phone Number, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Country, and Industry.
objects.behavior: When a structured twelve-field contact record is received, record it as a new row in the Zapier Table. Each received record is appended to the persistent store without modification.

name: AI-Powered Brand Press Monitoring
raw_steps: Run the Zap on a weekly schedule.
Use OpenAI to search for the 25 most recent press mentions of your brand.
Use News API and PhantomJsCloud to gather comprehensive media data.
Use OpenAI to summarize each article and extract key information and sentiment.
Store all press mentions and AI-generated summaries in Zapier Tables.
Display the stored data in a custom Zapier interface for viewing and analysis.
Send an email notification to relevant team members when new press mentions are discovered.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that fires a weekly trigger to initiate the press monitoring workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last scheduled run time and next scheduled execution time.
objects.behavior: When the weekly schedule fires, forward the trigger event to press-monitoring-pipeline.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly trigger event to start the press monitoring workflow.

event_sources: schedule: weekly timer

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates press mention discovery, summarization, and routing of results to storage and notifications.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current batch of discovered press mentions, their summaries, sentiment scores, and processing status for the active run.
objects.behavior: When a weekly trigger arrives, instruct openai-search to find the 25 most recent press mentions of Acme Corporation. When search results arrive, instruct news-api and phantomjscloud to gather comprehensive media data for each mention simultaneously. When media data is collected, instruct openai-summarizer to summarize each article and extract key information and sentiment. When summaries are ready, send all press mentions and AI-generated summaries to press-data-store and send a new-mentions notification to marketing-notifications simultaneously. Never wait for confirmation from press-data-store or marketing-notifications before completing the run.

peers.relationship: Request the 25 most recent press mentions of Acme Corporation when the weekly trigger fires.
Request comprehensive media data for discovered press mentions.
Request additional media data and page renders for discovered press mentions.
Send collected article content for summarization and sentiment extraction.
Send all press mentions and AI-generated summaries for storage.
Send notification with new press mention details when new mentions are discovered.

objects.role: Read service that uses OpenAI to search for the most recent press mentions of Acme Corporation.
objects.behavior: Accepts search requests for press mentions of Acme Corporation and returns the 25 most recent results. When answering queries, call the `openai_search_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service that queries the News API to gather comprehensive media data for press mentions.
objects.behavior: Accepts requests for media data about specific articles or topics and returns comprehensive news data including source, publication date, and article content. When answering queries, call the `news_api_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service that uses PhantomJsCloud to render and extract data from web pages for press mentions.
objects.behavior: Accepts URLs or article references and returns fully rendered page content and extracted media data. When answering queries, call the `phantomjscloud_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service that uses OpenAI to summarize articles and extract key information and sentiment.
objects.behavior: Accepts article content and returns a structured summary including key points, extracted entities, and sentiment score. When answering queries, call the `openai_summarizer_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that stores all press mentions and AI-generated summaries in Zapier Tables for persistent record-keeping.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all stored press mention records including article metadata, summaries, sentiment scores, and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When press mention records with summaries arrive, store each record in Zapier Tables. Records are made available for display in the Zapier interface.

objects.role: Write service that sends email notifications to the Marketing team when new press mentions are discovered.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all email notifications sent, including recipient, timestamp, and mention summary.
objects.behavior: When a new-mentions notification arrives, send an email to the Marketing team listing the newly discovered press mentions and their summaries.

steps.text: Weekly schedule timer fires to initiate the Acme Corporation press monitoring workflow.

name: Facebook Lead to In-Store Conversion Tracking
raw_steps: Trigger when a Facebook ad campaign generates a new lead.
Capture the lead data and store it in Zapier Tables.
Display the lead information in a custom interface for team management.
Record a conversion event in the interface when a lead makes an in-store purchase.
Trigger the second Zap to send conversion data back to Facebook.
Format the conversion data using Zapier's Formatter tool to meet Facebook's requirements.
Send the formatted conversion data to Facebook to update ad campaign metrics and improve targeting.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead events from Facebook ad campaigns via webhook.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming lead events with lead ID, campaign ID, ad set ID, contact details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead event arrives from a Facebook ad campaign, forward the lead data to the lead-conversion-tracker business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new Facebook lead event is received

event_sources: webhook: Facebook Lead Ads new lead event

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives conversion trigger events from the dashboard when a lead makes an in-store purchase.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion trigger events with lead ID, purchase details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a conversion event is triggered from the Zapier Interfaces dashboard indicating an in-store purchase, forward the conversion data to the lead-conversion-tracker business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward conversion event data when an in-store purchase is recorded in the dashboard

event_sources: webhook: Zapier Interfaces dashboard conversion event triggered

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the full lifecycle of Facebook leads from capture through in-store conversion and Facebook reporting.
objects.state_description: Tracks each lead's current state (new, active, converted), associated campaign metadata, conversion details, and formatting status for Facebook Conversions API submission.
objects.behavior: When a new lead payload arrives, store it to zapier-tables and send it to the sales-dashboard for display. When a conversion event arrives for a known lead, retrieve the lead record from zapier-tables, enrich it with the conversion details, send the combined conversion data to conversion-formatter for formatting, and simultaneously update the sales-dashboard with the conversion status. When formatted conversion data is returned from conversion-formatter, send it to facebook-conversions-api.

peers.relationship: Send new lead data for storage when a lead is received; send updated conversion data when a conversion event is processed
Send lead information for display when a new lead arrives; send conversion update when an in-store purchase is recorded
Send raw conversion data for formatting to meet Facebook Conversions API requirements when a conversion event is processed
Send formatted conversion data to update Facebook ad campaign metrics when formatting is complete

objects.role: Write service that persistently stores lead records and conversion updates in Zapier Tables.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with fields: lead ID, campaign ID, contact details, capture timestamp, conversion status, and conversion timestamp.
objects.behavior: When lead data or conversion update data is received, record it in the Zapier Tables store. Maintain an audit trail of all lead and conversion records.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Interfaces dashboard used by the Retail Sales team to view leads and record in-store conversions.
objects.state_description: Displays current lead records with status, contact details, and conversion flags visible to the Retail Sales team.
objects.behavior: When lead data is received, display it in the dashboard for the Retail Sales team. When a conversion update is received, update the displayed lead record with conversion status. When a sales team member records an in-store purchase conversion event in the dashboard, forward the conversion event data to lead-conversion-tracker via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward in-store purchase conversion event to the entry-point when a sales team member records a conversion in the dashboard

objects.role: Write service and formatting utility that transforms raw conversion data into the structure required by Facebook's Conversions API.
objects.state_description: Logs all formatting requests and outputs with input payload, formatted output, and transformation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When raw conversion data is received, apply Facebook Conversions API formatting rules: normalize event names, hash required PII fields (email, phone) using SHA-256, structure the payload with event_name, event_time, user_data, and custom_data fields, and return the formatted payload to the caller.

objects.role: Write service that sends formatted conversion events to Facebook's Conversions API to update ad campaign metrics and improve targeting.
objects.state_description: Logs all conversion submissions sent to Facebook with payload, response status, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When formatted conversion data is received, submit it to the Facebook Conversions API endpoint and record the submission and response status in the audit log.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via a Facebook Lead Ad campaign. Lead details: Name: Maria Gonzalez, Email: maria.gonzalez@email.com, Phone: +1-555-847-2931, Ad Campaign: Summer Sale 2024, Ad Set: Retargeting - Local Shoppers, Lead ID: fb_lead_78423, Submitted At: 2024-07-15T10:23:00Z.

expect.action: Lead record for Maria Gonzalez (fb_lead_78423) stored in Zapier Tables and lead information displayed in the sales-dashboard for the Retail Sales team, including name, email, phone, ad campaign Summer Sale 2024, and submission timestamp.
expect.reason: facebook-leads forwards the lead payload to lead-conversion-tracker, which stores it in zapier-tables and sends it to sales-dashboard per its behavior.

steps.text: A conversion event has been triggered from the Zapier Interfaces dashboard indicating that lead Maria Gonzalez (fb_lead_78423) made an in-store purchase. Purchase details: Store: Downtown Flagship, Purchase Amount: $142.50, Purchase Date: 2024-07-16T14:05:00Z, Salesperson: James Rivera.

expect.action: Formatted conversion event for Maria Gonzalez (fb_lead_78423) submitted to the Facebook Conversions API with hashed PII fields (email: maria.gonzalez@email.com, phone: +1-555-847-2931), event name 'Purchase', purchase value $142.50, and store Downtown Flagship; response status recorded in the audit log.
expect.reason: facebook-conversion-trigger forwards the conversion data to lead-conversion-tracker, which retrieves the lead record from zapier-tables, sends raw conversion data to conversion-formatter for SHA-256 hashing and payload structuring, then forwards the formatted payload to facebook-conversions-api for submission.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify zapier-tables.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to zapier-tables.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify sales-dashboard.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to sales-dashboard.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify conversion-formatter.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to conversion-formatter.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify facebook-conversions-api.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to facebook-conversions-api.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from user data. When raw conversion data is received, apply Facebook Conversions API formatting rules: normalize event names, hash required PII fields (email, phone) using SHA-256, structure the payload with event_name, event_time, user_data, and custom_data fields, and return the formatted payload to the caller.

name: Subscription Renewal Reminder Tracker
raw_steps: Store subscription details in a Zapier Table with columns for Subscription Name, Start Date, Renewal Date, Cost, Status, and Contact Email.
Check the table seven days before each renewal date.
Send a reminder email with subscription details to the configured recipient when the renewal date approaches.
Allow customization of the reminder timeframe and recipient email address.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Table that stores subscription records and triggers daily schedule checks.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all subscription records received and forwarded, including Subscription Name, Start Date, Renewal Date, Cost, Status, and Contact Email.
objects.behavior: When a scheduled daily check event arrives, forward all subscription records to the renewal-policy object for evaluation. Record each event received as part of the audit trail.

peers.relationship: Forward all subscription records on each scheduled daily check for renewal evaluation

event_sources: scheduled trigger: daily check event from Zapier Table

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates subscription renewal dates and determines which subscriptions require a reminder notification.
objects.state_description: Tracks the configured reminder timeframe (default 7 days) and the configured recipient email address (default billing@company.com), along with the list of subscriptions currently under evaluation.
objects.behavior: When subscription records arrive, compare each record's Renewal Date against today's date. If the Renewal Date is within the configured reminder timeframe (default 7 days), mark that subscription as requiring a reminder. Collect all subscriptions requiring reminders and send their details to the email-notifications object. If no subscriptions are within the reminder window, take no action. Apply the configured recipient email address (default billing@company.com) to all outgoing reminders. Allow the reminder timeframe and recipient email to be updated via configuration messages.

peers.relationship: Send reminder email when renewal is within configured timeframe

objects.role: Write service object that sends reminder emails to the configured billing manager with subscription renewal details.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all reminder emails sent, including recipient address, subscription name, renewal date, cost, status, and timestamp of send.
objects.behavior: When reminder details arrive, send a reminder email to the specified recipient address containing the Subscription Name, Start Date, Renewal Date, Cost, Status, and Contact Email for each subscription flagged for renewal. Record each email sent in the audit trail.

steps.text: Daily scheduled check event fires for the Zapier Table containing subscription records.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from renewal-policy data. Tracks the configured reminder timeframe (default 7 days) and the configured recipient email address (default billing@company.com), along with the list of subscriptions currently under evaluation.

name: Expense Tracking and Team Notification System
raw_steps: Add expense details (Date, Description, Amount, Category) manually as a new row in the Zapier Tables expense tracker
Click the reimbursement button on the expense row to trigger a Zap that sends an email to your finance team notifying them of the new expense
A second Zap automatically updates the expense status to 'pending' after the button is clicked

objects.role: Entry-point write service representing the Zapier Tables expense tracker that receives new expense rows and button-click events
objects.state_description: Tracks all expense rows with fields: Date, Description, Amount, Category, Status, and reimbursement button state
objects.behavior: Record incoming expense row additions and button-click events. When a reimbursement button-click event arrives on an expense row, forward the expense details to expense-processing via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward expense row details (Date, Description, Amount, Category) when the reimbursement button is clicked

event_sources: external system: new expense row added to Zapier Tables
external system: reimbursement button clicked on expense row

objects.role: Business logic object that applies expense notification and status update rules when a reimbursement is triggered
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight expense events being processed, including expense details and current processing step
objects.behavior: When an expense reimbursement button-click event arrives with expense details (Date, Description, Amount, Category), apply the following rules: format a notification email for the Finance Operations team summarizing the expense details, and simultaneously trigger a status update to set the expense row status to 'pending'. Send the formatted notification to finance-notifications and send the status update instruction to expense-tracker-updater at the same time.

peers.relationship: Send formatted expense notification email with Date, Description, Amount, and Category when a reimbursement button is clicked
Send expense row identifier and status value 'pending' to update the record when a reimbursement button is clicked

objects.role: Write service representing the email notification system that delivers expense alerts to the Finance Operations team
objects.state_description: Audit log of all notification emails sent, including recipient, subject, body, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record each incoming notification request and deliver the email to the Finance Operations team. Log the sent notification with timestamp and expense details.

objects.role: Write service that updates the status field of expense rows in Zapier Tables to reflect processing state
objects.state_description: Audit log of all status updates applied to expense rows, including row identifier, old status, new status, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Record each incoming status update request and apply the new status value to the specified expense row in Zapier Tables. Log the update with timestamp and row details.

steps.text: A new expense row has been added to the Zapier Tables expense tracker with the following details: Date: 2024-06-15, Description: Client dinner with Acme Corp stakeholders, Amount: $347.50, Category: Meals & Entertainment. The row is assigned ID EXP-2024-0892 and belongs to employee Sarah Mitchell, Senior Account Manager.

expect.action: Expense row EXP-2024-0892 recorded in the expense tracker for Sarah Mitchell with Date: 2024-06-15, Description: Client dinner with Acme Corp stakeholders, Amount: $347.50, Category: Meals & Entertainment.
expect.reason: expense-tracker records incoming expense row additions as defined by its event_sources

steps.text: The reimbursement button has been clicked on expense row EXP-2024-0892 (Date: 2024-06-15, Description: Client dinner with Acme Corp stakeholders, Amount: $347.50, Category: Meals & Entertainment, Employee: Sarah Mitchell, Senior Account Manager).

expect.action: Notification email sent to the Finance Operations team detailing expense EXP-2024-0892 for Sarah Mitchell (Date: 2024-06-15, Description: Client dinner with Acme Corp stakeholders, Amount: $347.50, Category: Meals & Entertainment), and expense row EXP-2024-0892 status updated to 'pending' in Zapier Tables.
expect.reason: expense-tracker forwards button-click event to expense-processing, which routes a formatted notification to finance-notifications for email delivery to the Finance Operations team and sends a status update request to expense-status-updater to set the row status to 'pending'

name: Contact List Email Automation
raw_steps: Store contact information including name, email, business details, and communication history in a Zapier Table.
Click the Send Email button to trigger a Zap for a selected contact.
Automatically send an email to the contact's email address using the subject and body fields from the table.
Automatically update the Last Email Sent column with the current date.
Optionally import contact details from a CSV file or Google Sheets into the table.
Optionally add custom fields to the table for additional contact information like emergency numbers.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Table named Business Contacts; stores contact records and fires button-click trigger events.
objects.state_description: Stores contact records including name, email, business details, communication history, Last Email Sent date, and Emergency Phone Number field.
objects.behavior: When a Send Email button-click event arrives for a contact, forward the contact's details (name, email, subject, body) to contact-email-workflow via peer message. When a CSV import event arrives, forward the imported contact rows to contact-email-workflow via peer message. When a record update is received, persist the updated field values to the contact record.

peers.relationship: Forward contact details and trigger type when a Send Email button is clicked or a CSV import event arrives
Not directly — updates are received back from contact-email-workflow

event_sources: zapier-table: Send Email button clicked
zapier-table: CSV file import triggered

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates contact email sending and record maintenance based on contact data and trigger type.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight email jobs: contact id, trigger type (button-click or CSV import), email address, subject, body, and processing status.
objects.behavior: When a button-click trigger arrives with contact details, extract the email address, subject, and body fields from the contact record and send an email instruction to email-sender. Simultaneously send a Last Email Sent date-update instruction to contact-table with the current date and the contact's record id. When a CSV import trigger arrives with contact rows, validate each row has at minimum a name and email, then upsert each contact record to contact-table. When a request to add a custom field arrives, forward the field definition to contact-table.

peers.relationship: Send email address, subject, and body when a contact email must be dispatched
Send record-update instruction with current date for Last Email Sent column after email dispatch; send upsert instructions for CSV-imported contacts

objects.role: Write service object that sends outbound emails via the configured email provider.
objects.state_description: Audit log of sent emails: recipient address, subject, body, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an email instruction is received containing a recipient address, subject, and body, send the email immediately and record the delivery in the audit log.

steps.text: Send Email button clicked for contact Margaret Chen, Senior Procurement Manager, email: margaret.chen@acmesupply.com, subject: 'Q3 Partnership Proposal', body: 'Dear Margaret, we wanted to follow up on our recent discussion about expanding our partnership in Q3. Please let us know your availability for a call this week. Best regards, The Sales Team'

expect.action: Email sent to margaret.chen@acmesupply.com with subject 'Q3 Partnership Proposal' and body 'Dear Margaret, we wanted to follow up on our recent discussion about expanding our partnership in Q3. Please let us know your availability for a call this week. Best regards, The Sales Team'; Last Email Sent column updated with today's date for Margaret Chen's record in Business Contacts
expect.reason: contact-table forwards button-click event to contact-email-workflow, which instructs email-sender to send the email and instructs contact-table to update the Last Email Sent field

steps.text: CSV file import triggered for Business Contacts table containing the following contacts: David Okafor (david.okafor@brightlogistics.com, Bright Logistics, Operations Director), Priya Nair (priya.nair@novafin.io, Nova Financial, CFO), and Carlos Mendez (carlos.mendez@urbanretail.co, Urban Retail Group, Head of Procurement)

expect.action: Contact records for David Okafor, Priya Nair, and Carlos Mendez created in the Business Contacts table with their respective name, email, business, and role details
expect.reason: contact-table receives the CSV import event and forwards the contact data to contact-email-workflow for processing and storage into the Business Contacts table

name: Inventory Reorder Automation
raw_steps: Enter product data into the inventory table with details including cost and quantity needed.
Check the Need More? box when a product's quantity reaches the reorder level.
Click the Send Reorder Request button to trigger the automation.
The first Zap sends an email to a designated email address with inventory information from the table.
The second Zap automatically updates the reorder quantity and last reorder date fields in the table.
Optionally configure the Zap to send emails to different addresses by adding an Email to field in the table for product-specific reorder recipients.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the inventory table that captures product data and triggers reorder workflows when the Need More? checkbox is checked and the Send Reorder Request button is clicked.
objects.state_description: Tracks all product records including product name, cost, quantity needed, reorder level, Need More? flag, email-to field, reorder quantity, and last reorder date.
objects.behavior: When a Send Reorder Request button click event arrives (with Need More? checked for a product), forward the full product record including product name, cost, quantity needed, reorder level, and optional Email to field to the reorder-policy object. When updated reorder quantity and last reorder date values are received, record them against the relevant product row.

peers.relationship: Forward product record details when Send Reorder Request is triggered with Need More? checked
Send reorder email to the determined recipient

event_sources: user action: Send Reorder Request button clicked with Need More? checked

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces reorder rules, determines the correct recipient email address, and coordinates outbound notifications and table updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight reorder requests with product details, resolved recipient addresses, and computed reorder quantities.
objects.behavior: When a product reorder request arrives, determine the recipient email address: if the product record includes an Email to field, use that address; otherwise use purchasing@company.com. Compute the reorder quantity based on the quantity needed. Set the last reorder date to today's date. Send the reorder email notification to email-sender with the resolved recipient, product name, cost, and quantity details. Send the updated reorder quantity and last reorder date back to inventory-table.

objects.role: Write service object that sends reorder request emails to the resolved purchasing recipient with inventory details.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all reorder emails sent, including recipient address, product details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a send-email request arrives, dispatch the email to the specified recipient address with the provided subject and body content. Record the sent email details in the audit log.

steps.text: User checks the Need More? box for product 'Thermal Label Rolls' (cost: $18.50, quantity needed: 200, reorder level: 50, no custom Email to field) and clicks the Send Reorder Request button in the inventory table.

expect.action: Email sent to purchasing@company.com with subject 'Reorder Request: Thermal Label Rolls' and body containing product name 'Thermal Label Rolls', cost $18.50, quantity needed 200, and reorder level 50; inventory table updated with new reorder quantity 200 and last reorder date set to today's date for the Thermal Label Rolls record.
expect.reason: inventory-table forwards the product record to reorder-policy, which resolves the recipient as purchasing@company.com (no Email to field present), instructs email-sender to dispatch the reorder email, and writes the updated reorder quantity and last reorder date back to the inventory table.

name: Employee Directory Anniversary Reminder
raw_steps: Create an employee directory table in Zapier Tables with fields for employee name, phone number, job title, office location, email address, employment status, and custom fields as needed.
Add employee records to the directory table with their personal and contact information.
Set up an automated workflow that transforms the work anniversary date field into a month-day format to enable yearly recurring reminders.
Set up an automated workflow that transforms the birthday date field into a month-day format to enable yearly recurring reminders.
Configure the workflows to send a reminder email when an employee's work anniversary date matches the current month and day.
Configure the workflows to send a reminder email when an employee's birthday matches the current month and day.
Enable automatic record processing in the workflow settings to ensure reminders are sent without manual intervention.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that fires a daily event for each employee record when the work anniversary date (in month-day format) matches the current month and day.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-processed date to avoid duplicate triggers; logs each anniversary match event.
objects.behavior: Each day, for every employee record whose work anniversary month-day matches today's month-day, emit a trigger event containing the employee's full record. Forward the event to the anniversary-reminder-rules object.

peers.relationship: Forward employee anniversary match event with full employee record when the work anniversary date matches today's month-day

event_sources: scheduled: daily date match check on work anniversary field in Zapier Tables

objects.role: Entry-point service object that fires a daily event for each employee record when the birthday date (in month-day format) matches the current month and day.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-processed date to avoid duplicate triggers; logs each birthday match event.
objects.behavior: Each day, for every employee record whose birthday month-day matches today's month-day, emit a trigger event containing the employee's full record. Forward the event to the birthday-reminder-rules object.

peers.relationship: Forward employee birthday match event with full employee record when the birthday date matches today's month-day

event_sources: scheduled: daily date match check on birthday field in Zapier Tables

objects.role: Business logic object that applies work anniversary reminder policy, determining when and how to notify HR about employee work anniversaries.
objects.state_description: Tracks anniversary reminder events processed, including employee name, anniversary date, and notification status.
objects.behavior: When an anniversary match event arrives, extract the employee name, job title, department, office location, and work anniversary date from the event payload. Compose an HR reminder email indicating the employee's work anniversary details. Send the formatted reminder to the hr-email-notifications object.

peers.relationship: Send formatted work anniversary reminder email to HR with employee name, job title, department, office location, and anniversary date

objects.role: Business logic object that applies birthday reminder policy, determining when and how to notify HR about employee birthdays.
objects.state_description: Tracks birthday reminder events processed, including employee name, birthday date, and notification status.
objects.behavior: When a birthday match event arrives, extract the employee name, job title, department, office location, and birthday from the event payload. Compose an HR reminder email indicating the employee's birthday details. Send the formatted reminder to the hr-email-notifications object.

peers.relationship: Send formatted birthday reminder email to HR with employee name, job title, department, office location, and birthday

objects.role: Write service that sends reminder emails to the HR team and logs each notification dispatched.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all reminder emails sent, including recipient, subject, employee details, event type (anniversary or birthday), and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a reminder email message arrives, send the email to the HR team address with the provided subject and body. Record the notification in the audit log with timestamp, event type, and employee details.

Employee record found: Maria Gonzalez, Senior HR Coordinator, Human Resources department, Austin office, work anniversary date June 15.

name: Email Attachment Saver
raw_steps: Monitor Gmail for incoming emails with attachments.
Extract attachments from the received email.
Upload the extracted attachments to a folder in Google Drive.
Save the attachment link, date, sender, and inbox details as a record in Zapier Tables for easy reference.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for incoming emails with attachments and forwards them to the attachment-processor.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last processed email timestamp and message IDs to avoid duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Gmail with one or more attachments, extract the email metadata (sender, date, inbox/label, subject) and the raw attachment data, then forward all of this to the attachment-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward email metadata and attachment data when a new email with attachments is detected

event_sources: external system: Gmail new email with attachment

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates extraction of attachments from incoming emails and coordinates saving them to Drive and recording metadata.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight processing jobs with email ID, sender, date, inbox, attachment names, and processing status.
objects.behavior: When an email with attachments arrives, extract each attachment file from the email payload. For each attachment, send the file to google-drive-storage for upload. Simultaneously send the attachment metadata record (sender, date, inbox, subject, attachment name) to zapier-tables-store. After obtaining the Drive link from google-drive-storage, include it in the record sent to zapier-tables-store.

peers.relationship: Send each extracted attachment file for upload to the Email Attachments folder
Send attachment link, date, sender, and inbox details as a new record for storage

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment files and uploads them to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive, returning the shareable link.
objects.state_description: Tracks uploaded files with their Drive file IDs, names, upload timestamps, and shareable links.
objects.behavior: When an attachment file is received, upload it to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive. Return the shareable Drive link for the uploaded file to the sender so it can be included in downstream records.

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment metadata records and saves them as rows in Zapier Tables for easy reference.
objects.state_description: Tracks saved records with columns for attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name.
objects.behavior: When a metadata record is received containing attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name, write it as a new row in the designated Zapier Tables table.

steps.text: New email received in Gmail from Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@techcorp.com) with subject 'Q3 Budget Report - Final Files', sent on 2024-07-15, in the Primary inbox, containing two attachments: 'Q3_Budget_Report.pdf' (2.4 MB) and 'Budget_Breakdown.xlsx' (1.1 MB).

expect.action: Two rows written to Zapier Tables: one for 'Q3_Budget_Report.pdf' with its Google Drive shareable link, date 2024-07-15, sender sarah.mitchell@techcorp.com, and inbox Primary; one for 'Budget_Breakdown.xlsx' with its Google Drive shareable link, date 2024-07-15, sender sarah.mitchell@techcorp.com, and inbox Primary. Both files uploaded to the 'Email Attachments' folder in Google Drive.
expect.reason: gmail-monitor forwards email metadata and attachments to attachment-processor, which sends each file to google-drive-storage for upload and the resulting metadata (link, date, sender, inbox, attachment name) to zapier-tables-store for recording.

name: Form to Google Contacts Integration
raw_steps: Create a custom form with fields for name, email, phone number, and address.
Send form submission data to a table for verification and storage.
Connect the verified data from the table to Google Contacts.
Optionally use Canvas to visualize and customize the workflow.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives form submission events from Typeform containing name, email, phone number, and address fields.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submissions with timestamp and raw field values.
objects.behavior: When a form submission event arrives, forward the submission payload to the contact-validation-rules object via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward raw form submission data (name, email, phone, address) when a new submission is received.

event_sources: webhook: Typeform form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that validates and normalizes form submission data before routing it to storage and contact creation.
objects.state_description: Tracks validation outcomes per submission including field-level errors, normalization results, and routing decisions.
objects.behavior: When a form submission arrives, validate that name is non-empty, email matches a valid format, phone number contains only digits and allowed characters, and address is non-empty. Normalize phone numbers to E.164 format and trim whitespace from all fields. If validation passes, send the verified record to google-sheets-store and google-contacts-store simultaneously. If validation fails, flag the record with error details and send it to google-sheets-store as a failed record for manual review.

peers.relationship: Send verified or failed-validation records with status flag for storage and audit trail.
Send verified contact records for creation in Google Contacts when validation passes.
Send workflow event metadata (submission ID, validation status, routing actions taken) for visualization.

objects.role: Write service object that records all form submission data including validation status into a Google Sheets table for verification and storage.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all received records with fields: name, email, phone, address, validation_status, timestamp, submission_id.
objects.behavior: When a record arrives, append it as a new row to the designated Google Sheets table, including all contact fields and the validation status flag.

objects.role: Write service object that creates or updates contact entries in Google Contacts using verified submission data.
objects.state_description: Log of contact creation and update operations including contact fields and operation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a verified contact record arrives, create a new Google Contact entry with the provided name, email, phone number, and address fields. If a contact with the same email already exists, update the existing record with the new field values.

objects.role: Write service object that records workflow execution metadata to Zapier Canvas for visualization and customization of the automation workflow.
objects.state_description: Log of workflow events including submission IDs, validation outcomes, and routing actions for canvas display.
objects.behavior: When a workflow event message arrives, record the event metadata to the Zapier Canvas workflow visualization layer, capturing each step taken in the automation for monitoring and customization purposes.

steps.text: New Typeform submission received: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, Phone: +1-555-867-5309, Address: 142 Oakwood Drive, Austin, TX 78701

expect.action: A new row appended to the designated Google Sheets table with Sarah Mitchell's contact fields (name: Sarah Mitchell, email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, phone: +1-555-867-5309, address: 142 Oakwood Drive, Austin, TX 78701) and a validation status of valid; a new Google Contact entry created for Sarah Mitchell with the same fields; and a workflow event metadata record written to Zapier Canvas capturing each step of the automation.
expect.reason: contact-validation-rules validates and normalizes the submission, then forwards the verified record to google-sheets-store (which appends a row), google-contacts-store (which creates the contact), and zapier-canvas-log (which records the workflow event metadata).

name: Slack Messages to Notion Tasks
raw_steps: Monitor Slack messages for task mentions
Extract task information and context from Slack conversations
Create a new task entry in Notion database
Populate Notion task with extracted details and metadata

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the #project-tasks Slack channel and forwards incoming messages to the task-extraction business logic object.
objects.state_description: Tracks messages received from the #project-tasks channel, including message content, sender, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new message arrives in the #project-tasks channel, forward the full message content, sender identity, message timestamp, and message permalink to the task-extraction peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full message content, sender, timestamp, and permalink when a new message arrives in #project-tasks

event_sources: external system: Slack message posted in #project-tasks channel

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes Slack messages to identify task mentions and extracts structured task data including title, description, assignee, and due date.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress extraction attempts with the raw message, extracted fields, and extraction confidence for each processed message.
objects.behavior: When a Slack message is received, analyze it to determine whether it contains a task mention. If no task is identified, discard the message. If a task is identified, extract the task title, description, assignee (name or handle), and due date from the message text using natural language understanding. If a field is ambiguous or missing, apply best-effort inference or leave it blank. Once extraction is complete, send the structured task data along with the source message permalink to the notion-tasks-db write service.

peers.relationship: Send extracted task title, description, assignee, due date, and source message permalink when a task is successfully identified and extracted

objects.role: Write service object that creates new task entries in the Engineering Team Notion database with the provided task details.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all task entries created, including task title, description, assignee, due date, source message link, and creation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When task data is received, create a new entry in the Engineering Team Notion database populating the title, description, assignee, due date, and source message link fields. Record the created entry details in the audit trail.

steps.text: New message posted in #project-tasks by Sarah Chen (Senior Engineer): 'Hey team, we need to add OAuth2 support to the API gateway. @Marcus Rivera please handle this — it should cover token refresh and scope validation. Due date is next Friday, December 13th. This is blocking the mobile team so high priority.' Message timestamp: 2024-12-06T14:32:00Z, permalink: https://workspace.slack.com/archives/C04PROJECTTASKS/p1733492120000100

expect.action: New task entry created in the Engineering Team Notion database with title 'Add OAuth2 support to the API gateway', description covering token refresh and scope validation, assignee Marcus Rivera, due date December 13 2024, and source message link https://workspace.slack.com/archives/C04PROJECTTASKS/p1733492120000100
expect.reason: slack-tasks-channel forwards the message to task-extraction, which identifies and extracts the task details, then passes structured data to notion-tasks-db, which creates the Notion entry

mock_tools.description: Write or notify notion-tasks-db.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to notion-tasks-db.

name: GMB Review Triage With AI
raw_steps: Monitor Google My Business for new customer reviews.
Analyze each review's content and star rating for risk factors.
Assess for indicators such as 1-star ratings, legal language, threats, or safety concerns.
Route normal reviews (positive feedback or constructive criticism) to the standard response path.
Route high-risk reviews (negative ratings with concerning content) to the approval path.
Create a draft response for high-risk reviews.
Send an email notification to your team with the review details and suggested response draft.
Post automated professional responses directly to Google My Business for standard reviews.
Hold drafted responses for high-risk reviews pending human approval.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Google My Business for new customer reviews and forwards them for triage.
objects.state_description: Records all incoming reviews with timestamp, star rating, reviewer name, and review text.
objects.behavior: When a new review arrives on Google My Business, record it and forward it to the review-triage peer. When a response is received from a peer to post to GMB, call the GMB API to publish the response.

peers.relationship: Forward full review payload (star rating, review text, reviewer name, timestamp) when a new review is detected
Receive outbound response content and post it to Google My Business

event_sources: external system: Google My Business new review webhook

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes review content and star ratings to classify reviews as standard or high-risk and routes them accordingly.
objects.state_description: Tracks each review's classification decision, risk factors identified, and routing outcome.
objects.behavior: When a review arrives, analyze the star rating and text content for risk indicators. Risk indicators include: 1-star or 2-star ratings combined with legal language (e.g., 'lawyer', 'sue', 'lawsuit', 'legal action'), threats, safety concerns, or highly inflammatory content. If risk indicators are present, classify as high-risk: send the review to draft-composer to generate a response draft, and send a notification to cx-notification with the review details and the pending draft. If no risk indicators are present, classify as standard: send the review to response-composer to generate an automated professional response for direct posting. Constructive criticism and positive feedback are standard. Any review with legal language, threats, or safety concerns regardless of star rating is high-risk.

peers.relationship: Send high-risk review details requesting a draft response for human review
Send standard review details requesting an automated professional response
Send high-risk review details and classification rationale so the CX team can be notified

objects.role: Business logic object that generates professional automated responses for standard reviews.
objects.state_description: Tracks composed responses and the reviews they correspond to.
objects.behavior: When a standard review is received, compose a professional, brand-appropriate response tailored to the review content and sentiment. Positive reviews should receive a warm thank-you response. Constructive criticism reviews should receive an empathetic acknowledgment and a commitment to improvement. Send the composed response to gmb-poster for direct publication.

peers.relationship: Send the composed automated response along with the review identifier for direct posting to GMB

objects.role: Business logic object that generates draft responses for high-risk reviews to be reviewed by the Customer Experience team before posting.
objects.state_description: Tracks draft responses keyed by review identifier, including draft content and approval status (pending).
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review is received, compose a careful, de-escalating, professional draft response that addresses the concerns raised without admitting liability or escalating tension. Store the draft as pending approval. Send the draft to cx-notification so it can be included in the human approval email. Do not send the draft to GMB directly — it must await human approval.

peers.relationship: Send the composed draft response alongside the high-risk review details for inclusion in the approval email
Store draft and await approval decision; forward approved responses to gmb-poster

objects.role: Write service object that holds high-risk draft responses pending human approval and routes approved responses to posting.
objects.state_description: Holds pending draft responses with review ID, draft content, submission timestamp, and approval status (pending/approved/rejected).
objects.behavior: When a draft response is received, record it as pending. When a human approval decision arrives, update the record status. If approved, forward the response to gmb-poster for publication. If rejected, record the rejection and notify cx-notification.

peers.relationship: Forward approved draft response with review identifier when human approval is received
Notify CX team if a draft response is rejected

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to the Customer Experience team for high-risk reviews requiring human approval.
objects.state_description: Logs all notifications sent including review details, draft response content, timestamp, and recipient.
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review notification and draft response are received, compose and send an email to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text, star rating, reviewer name, risk factors identified, and the suggested draft response. Record the notification as sent.

objects.role: Write service object that publishes approved or automated responses to Google My Business.
objects.state_description: Logs all responses posted to GMB including review ID, response text, posting timestamp, and response type (automated or approved-draft).
objects.behavior: When a response is received, call the GMB API to post the response to the corresponding review. Record the post with timestamp and type.

steps.text: A new Google My Business review has been submitted by Marcus Delgado, 1-star rating, review text: 'This place is an absolute disaster. I slipped and fell due to their negligence and I have already spoken to my lawyer about filing a lawsuit. I have photos and witnesses. This is dangerous and I will make sure everyone knows about it. Completely unacceptable safety hazard.' Reviewer: Marcus Delgado, posted on listing: Downtown Flagship Store.

expect.action: Email notification sent to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text ('This place is an absolute disaster...'), 1-star rating, reviewer name Marcus Delgado, listing Downtown Flagship Store, identified risk factors (1-star rating, legal language: 'lawyer', 'lawsuit', safety concern), and a draft de-escalating response composed for the review, with the draft held in response-approval as pending human approval.
expect.reason: review-triage classifies the review as high-risk due to 1-star rating and legal/safety language, routes it to draft-composer which composes a draft and forwards it to cx-notification and response-approval; cx-notification sends the email to the Customer Experience team; response-approval records the draft as pending.

name: Meeting Signup Calendar Sync
raw_steps: Team members opt into recurring meetings through a self-service interface.
Responses are automatically captured and tracked.
Attendee information syncs to Google Calendar.
Calendar invites are automatically updated with new attendees.
Team members receive calendar notifications with updated attendee information.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Google Form that captures team member opt-in responses for weekly all-hands meetings.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission events including respondent email, name, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission event arrives, forward the respondent details to the attendee-sync peer.

peers.relationship: Forward respondent details (name, email, timestamp) when a new form submission is received.

event_sources: external system: Google Form submission

objects.role: Write service object representing the Google Sheet that persistently records all form responses as an audit trail.
objects.state_description: Accumulates all form submission records including respondent name, email, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new attendee record is received, append it as a new row to the sheet.

objects.role: Business logic object that processes new signup responses, deduplicates attendees, and coordinates updates to the sheet and calendar.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current list of confirmed attendees for the recurring all-hands meeting, including their names and emails, to detect new additions and prevent duplicates.
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, check if the respondent is already in the attendee list. If the respondent is new, add them to the attendee list, then send the record to response-sheet and the updated attendee list to calendar-events. If the respondent is already tracked, discard the duplicate.

peers.relationship: Send new attendee record for persistent logging when a new unique respondent is identified.
Send updated attendee list when a new unique attendee is added, triggering calendar invite update.

objects.role: Write service object representing Google Calendar that maintains and updates the recurring all-hands meeting invite with current attendee information.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current state of the recurring all-hands calendar event including event ID, attendee list, and last updated timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an updated attendee list is received, update the recurring all-hands meeting event to include all attendees and trigger calendar notifications to all attendees including the newly added one.

steps.text: New Google Form submission received: Jordan Mitchell, Senior Engineer, has opted into the recurring weekly all-hands meeting. Submission includes name: Jordan Mitchell, role: Senior Engineer, email: jordan.mitchell@company.com.

expect.action: Google Calendar recurring all-hands meeting event updated to include Jordan Mitchell (jordan.mitchell@company.com); calendar notification sent to Jordan Mitchell and all existing attendees with updated attendee list.
expect.reason: signup-form forwards respondent details to attendee-sync, which deduplicates and finds Jordan Mitchell is new, sends the record to response-sheet to append a new row, and sends the updated attendee list to calendar-events, which updates the recurring meeting invite and triggers calendar notifications to all attendees.

name: Automatic Call Prep Workflow
raw_steps: Pull meeting data from scheduling tool.
Retrieve customer information from CRM.
Enrich data using enrichment service.
Generate comprehensive meeting brief using AI.
Deliver brief to Slack the day before meeting.

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new meeting events from Calendly via webhook
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming meeting event payloads including meeting title, time, attendees, and organizer details
objects.behavior: When a new meeting scheduled event arrives via webhook, forward the meeting data to the meeting-brief-builder peer.

peers.relationship: Forward meeting data payload when a new meeting is scheduled

event_sources: webhook: meeting.scheduled

objects.role: Read service that retrieves customer and account information from Salesforce on demand
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for customer and account records by contact email or account name. When answering queries, call the `salesforce_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service that retrieves enriched company and contact data from Clearbit on demand
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for enriched firmographic and contact data by email or domain. When answering queries, call the `clearbit_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that delivers messages to the #sales-prep Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all messages delivered to #sales-prep, including timestamp, recipient channel, and message content
objects.behavior: When a meeting brief message is received, post it to the #sales-prep Slack channel.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates data retrieval, enrichment, and brief generation for upcoming meetings
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress brief jobs including meeting metadata, retrieved Salesforce records, Clearbit enrichment data, and generated brief content
objects.behavior: When meeting data arrives from Calendly, query salesforce for customer and account records matching the meeting attendees. Query clearbit for enriched firmographic data on those contacts and companies. Once both data sets are available, compile all gathered information and use the generate-brief skill to produce a comprehensive meeting brief via GPT-4. Schedule delivery so that the brief is sent to slack-sales-prep the day before the scheduled meeting. If the meeting is less than 24 hours away, send the brief immediately.

peers.relationship: Query for customer and account records by attendee email when a new meeting arrives
Query for enriched contact and company data by attendee email or domain after Salesforce data is retrieved
Send the completed meeting brief the day before the scheduled meeting

steps.text: New meeting scheduled event from Calendly: Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive at Acme Corp, sarah.mitchell@acmecorp.com) has booked a 30-minute discovery call with Jordan Lee (Sales Rep, jordan.lee@ourcompany.com) scheduled for tomorrow at 2:00 PM EST. Meeting title: 'Acme Corp Discovery Call'. Attendee details: Sarah Mitchell, VP of Operations, sarah.mitchell@acmecorp.com, Acme Corp (acmecorp.com).

name: Slack Reactions to Google Tasks
raw_steps: User reacts to a message in Slack with a chosen emoji.
The automation detects the emoji reaction on the Slack message.
The automation retrieves the original message content and context from Slack.
The automation creates a new task in Google Tasks with the message content.
The task is formatted and stored in Google Tasks for later reference.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that listens for Slack emoji reaction events and forwards them to the task-formatter business logic object.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Slack reaction events including the emoji used, the message ID, channel ID, and the reacting user.
objects.behavior: When a reaction_added event arrives with the 📌 emoji, forward the event details (message ID, channel ID, timestamp, reacting user) to task-formatter. Ignore all other emoji reactions.

peers.relationship: Forward 📌 reaction event details (message ID, channel ID, timestamp, reacting user) when a 📌 reaction is detected on a Slack message.

event_sources: webhook: Slack reaction_added event

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the original Slack message content and context given a message ID and channel ID.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for Slack message content by fetching the message text, author, timestamp, and channel context. When answering queries, call the `slack_messages_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that processes Slack message content and formats it into a well-structured Google Task.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending task creation requests including the source message content, author, channel, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a 📌 reaction event arrives, query slack-messages for the original message content and context using the message ID and channel ID. Once the message content is retrieved, format a Google Task with: title derived from the first line or a truncated summary of the message, notes containing the full message text, the Slack channel name, the message author, and the original timestamp as a link reference. Then send the formatted task to google-tasks for creation.

peers.relationship: Query for original message content and context using the message ID and channel ID from the reaction event.
Send the fully formatted task (title, notes, metadata) for creation in Google Tasks.

objects.role: Write service that receives formatted task data and creates new tasks in Google Tasks.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all tasks created, including title, notes, creation timestamp, and source Slack message reference.
objects.behavior: When a formatted task message is received, create a new task in Google Tasks with the provided title and notes. Record the created task details in the audit trail.

steps.text: Slack reaction_added event: user Maya Chen reacted with 📌 to a message in channel #project-updates (channel ID: C04XYZABC). The message (ID: 1712345678.000100, timestamp: 1712345678.000100) was posted by Jordan Lee and reads: 'Reminder: the Q2 roadmap review is scheduled for Friday at 2pm. Please review the shared doc before the meeting and come prepared with your top 3 priorities.'

expect.action: A new Google Task is created with the title referencing the Q2 roadmap reminder and notes containing the full message text from Jordan Lee in #project-updates, recorded in Google Tasks.
expect.reason: slack-events forwards the 📌 reaction event to task-formatter, which queries slack-messages for the message content, formats a Google Task, and sends it to google-tasks to create the task.
