name: Slack Thread Summarizer
raw_steps: User reacts with an emoji to a Slack message.
The automation detects the emoji reaction on the message.
The automation retrieves the full Slack thread content.
An AI service generates a summary of the thread.
The automation sends the summary to the user via Slack DM.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that detects emoji reactions on Slack messages and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Records incoming emoji reaction events including user, message, channel, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 emoji reaction event arrives on a Slack message, forward the event details (reactor user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) to the thread-summarizer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction event details (user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) when a 📝 reaction is detected.

event_sources: webhook: Slack emoji reaction added event

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the full content of a Slack thread given a channel and message timestamp.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for thread content by fetching all messages in a Slack thread. When answering queries, call the `slack_threads_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates thread retrieval, summary generation, and delivery of the summary to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight summarization requests including requesting user, channel, message timestamp, retrieved thread content, and generated summary.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 reaction event arrives, query the slack-threads read service for the full thread content using the channel ID and message timestamp. When thread content is received, send it to the openai-summarizer for summary generation. When a summary is returned, send the summary and the requesting user ID to the slack-dm service for delivery.

peers.relationship: Query for full thread content using channel ID and message timestamp when a reaction event is received.
Send retrieved thread content for summarization once thread content is available.
Send the generated summary and requesting user ID for DM delivery once the summary is ready.

objects.role: Read service that generates a concise summary of a Slack thread using OpenAI.
objects.behavior: Responds to requests containing thread message content by generating a concise, readable summary. When answering queries, call the `openai_summarizer_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that sends a direct message to a Slack user with the thread summary.
objects.state_description: Records all DMs sent including recipient user ID, summary content, and delivery timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a summary and user ID are received, send the summary as a direct message to the specified Slack user.

steps.text: User Maya Chen reacted with the 📝 emoji to a Slack message in channel #product-updates (channel ID: C04XYZABC). The reacted message has timestamp 1718000000.123456 and was posted by Alex Rivera. Maya Chen's user ID is U09MAYACHEN.

expect.action: A direct message is sent to Maya Chen (U09MAYACHEN) containing a concise summary of the Slack thread in #product-updates starting at message timestamp 1718000000.123456.
expect.reason: slack-events forwards the reaction event to thread-summarizer, which retrieves the thread via slack-threads, sends it to openai-summarizer for a summary, and then slack-dm delivers the summary to Maya Chen's user ID.

name: VIP Session AI Summaries to Slack
raw_steps: Monitor Fullstory for VIP user sessions based on defined triggers (price thresholds, user actions, customer segments, session duration, or behavior signals).
Capture real-time session data when a VIP user interaction is detected.
Generate an AI-powered summary of the VIP session.
Deliver the AI summary to Slack to alert the support team.
Provide context and session details to enable immediate support action.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Fullstory for VIP user sessions matching defined trigger criteria and emits session events.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming session events from Fullstory including session replay URL, user ID, account tier, actions taken, session duration, order value, and detected issues.
objects.behavior: When a Fullstory session event arrives matching any VIP trigger (order value above $10,000, checkout abandonment, repeated error encounters, session duration over 20 minutes, or enterprise account segment membership), forward the full session payload to the vip-session-analyzer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full VIP session payload including session replay URL, user ID, account tier, actions taken, session duration, order value, and detected issues when a qualifying VIP session event is detected.

event_sources: webhook: Fullstory session event
external system: Fullstory VIP trigger fired

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates VIP session data, applies trigger classification rules, generates an AI-powered summary of key user actions, friction points, and behavioral signals, and prepares the alert payload.
objects.state_description: Tracks session payloads received, trigger classifications applied, and AI summaries generated per session.
objects.behavior: When a VIP session payload arrives, classify the trigger type (high-value order, checkout abandonment, repeated errors, long session, enterprise segment). Analyze the session actions to identify key user actions, friction points, and behavioral signals. Generate a structured AI summary highlighting these findings. Compose the full alert payload including account name, session duration, detected issues, trigger classification, AI summary, and direct link to the Fullstory session replay URL. Send the composed alert payload to the slack-vip-alerts peer.

peers.relationship: Send the composed VIP alert payload including AI summary, account name, session duration, detected issues, trigger classification, and session replay link when analysis is complete.

objects.role: Write service object that delivers VIP session AI summaries and context to the #vip-customer-alerts Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all VIP alert messages posted to the #vip-customer-alerts Slack channel, including timestamp, account name, session ID, and summary content.
objects.behavior: When an alert payload is received, post a formatted message to the #vip-customer-alerts Slack channel containing the AI-generated session summary, account name, session duration, detected issues, trigger classification, and a direct link to the Fullstory session replay. Record the posted message in the audit trail.

steps.text: Fullstory VIP trigger fired for session belonging to Margaret Chen, enterprise account 'Apex Global Solutions' (account tier: Enterprise). Session details: session ID FS-9921847, session duration 24 minutes, order value $13,500 initiated at checkout but abandoned before completion, 4 repeated JavaScript errors encountered on the payment page. Session replay URL: https://app.fullstory.com/ui/replay/FS-9921847. User ID: usr_4821093. Actions taken: browsed product catalog, added 3 items to cart, reached payment step, encountered errors, exited without completing purchase.

expect.action: A formatted message is posted to the #vip-customer-alerts Slack channel containing an AI-generated session summary for Margaret Chen of Apex Global Solutions, including trigger classifications (checkout abandonment, high-value order above $10,000, repeated errors, long session duration), key friction points on the payment page, session duration of 24 minutes, detected issues (4 JavaScript errors, checkout abandonment, $13,500 order), and a direct link to the Fullstory session replay at https://app.fullstory.com/ui/replay/FS-9921847.
expect.reason: fullstory-monitor emits the VIP session event to vip-session-analyzer, which classifies triggers, generates an AI summary of actions and friction points, and passes the alert payload to slack-vip-alerts, which posts the formatted message to #vip-customer-alerts.

name: Slack to Jira Engineering Request Triage
raw_steps: Monitor Slack for emoji reactions on messages.
Capture the reacted message and conversation context.
Send the conversation context to ChatGPT to identify work requirements.
Generate a structured title, description, and acceptance criteria using ChatGPT.
Create a Jira issue with the AI-generated content.
Route the issue through a triage process.
Handle follow-up notifications and assignments.

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service object that monitors the #engineering-requests channel for :ticket: emoji reactions and posts follow-up messages back to the channel.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming emoji reaction events with associated message text and thread context; records outbound follow-up messages posted to the channel.
objects.behavior: When a :ticket: emoji reaction event arrives on a message in #engineering-requests, capture the reacted message text and the full surrounding thread conversation, then forward the captured content to request-triage via peer message. When instructed to post a follow-up message with a Jira issue link and assignee, post it to the #engineering-requests channel.

peers.relationship: Forward captured message text and thread conversation when a :ticket: emoji reaction is detected
Receive Jira issue link and assignee info to post as a follow-up message in #engineering-requests

event_sources: slack: emoji reaction added (ticket) in #engineering-requests

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes Slack thread content to identify engineering requirements and produces structured Jira issue artifacts.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight triage requests including original thread content, identified requirements, generated Jira title, description, and acceptance criteria.
objects.behavior: When thread conversation content arrives, send it to chatgpt-analysis with a prompt to identify specific engineering work requirements, scope, and constraints. When analysis results return, use them to generate a structured Jira issue title, detailed description, and acceptance criteria — send to chatgpt-analysis for generation. When the structured Jira content is ready, send it to jira-issues to create a Story with Medium priority in the Engineering project. Send the Jira issue details including link and on-call triage lead assignment instruction to slack-requests for follow-up posting. Also send routing instruction to jira-triage-board to place the new issue on the Engineering Triage board.

peers.relationship: Send thread conversation for requirements identification and then structured content generation
Send generated title, description, acceptance criteria, issue type Story, and priority Medium to create a new Jira issue
Send new Jira issue reference to route it to the Engineering Triage board for review
Send Jira issue link and on-call triage lead assignee info for follow-up message posting

objects.role: Read service that calls ChatGPT to analyze thread content for engineering requirements and generate structured Jira issue content.
objects.behavior: When answering queries, call the chatgpt_analysis_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates Jira issues in the Engineering project with the specified fields and records the created issue details.
objects.state_description: Tracks created Jira issues including issue key, title, description, acceptance criteria, type, priority, and assignee.
objects.behavior: When issue creation details arrive (title, description, acceptance criteria, type Story, priority Medium), create the Jira issue in the Engineering project and record the resulting issue key and link. When assignment instructions arrive, update the issue assignee to the on-call Engineering Triage Lead.

objects.role: Write service that routes Jira issues onto the Engineering Triage board for review and prioritization by the Engineering Lead.
objects.state_description: Tracks issues routed to the Engineering Triage board including issue references and routing timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a Jira issue reference arrives, move or add the issue to the Engineering Triage board column for pending review and prioritization.

steps.text: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'We need a bulk CSV import feature for the customer onboarding flow — product team is blocked without it. The importer should handle up to 10,000 rows, validate email formats, skip duplicates, and surface errors inline. Deadline is end of Q2.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Sarah Chen (Product Manager): 'Confirmed, this is blocking 3 enterprise onboarding deals.', Marcus Webb (Backend Engineer): 'We'd need to add async job processing and a new import_jobs table in the DB.', and Priya Nair (QA Lead): 'Acceptance criteria should include error reporting per row and a summary email on completion.' The message was posted in thread ts=1717200000.123456 in channel #engineering-requests by Sarah Chen.

expect.action: A follow-up message posted in #engineering-requests containing the newly created Jira issue link (e.g., ENG-4821) and the issue assigned to the on-call Engineering Triage Lead, notifying the channel that the request has been triaged and routed to the Engineering Triage board.
expect.reason: slack-requests receives the final Jira issue details from request-triage and posts a follow-up message back to #engineering-requests per its behavior and peer relationship with request-triage

mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to chatgpt-analysis.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify jira-issues.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to jira-issues.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify jira-triage-board.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to jira-triage-board.

mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to slack-requests.

name: Automate Employment Verification Letters
raw_steps: Employee submits employment verification letter request through self-service interface.
System automatically retrieves employee data from HRIS.
System generates professionally formatted employment verification document.
Document is routed through proper approval channels.
Approved document is delivered to employee.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the HR self-service portal that receives employee employment verification letter requests.
objects.state_description: Records of submitted verification letter requests including employee ID, requester details, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an employee submits an employment verification letter request through the portal, forward the request details to the verification-letter-policy object. Record the submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward employee verification request details (employee ID, requester info, purpose) when a new request is submitted

event_sources: external system: employee submits employment verification letter request via HR self-service portal

objects.role: Read service representing Workday that retrieves employee data on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for employee data including name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and manager. When answering queries, call the `workday_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employment verification letter rules, orchestrates data retrieval, generates the letter, and routes it for approval.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight verification requests with their status (data-retrieved, letter-generated, pending-approval, approved, delivered), associated employee data, and generated document references.
objects.behavior: When a verification request arrives from hr-portal, query workday-directory for the employee's full name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and salary (if authorized). Once employee data is returned, use the generate-pdf-letter skill to produce a professionally formatted employment verification letter PDF. Then forward the generated letter and request context to bamboohr-approval for HR Manager review. When an approval decision arrives from bamboohr-approval: if approved, send the letter PDF and employee email address to email-delivery; if rejected, update state to rejected and notify hr-portal of the outcome.

peers.relationship: Query for employee data (name, title, department, start date, status) when a verification request is received
Send generated letter PDF and request context for HR Manager approval after letter is generated
Send approved letter PDF and employee email address for delivery when approval is confirmed
Notify with rejection outcome when approval is denied

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing BambooHR workflow that routes documents for HR Manager approval and returns approval decisions.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending and completed approval tasks with document reference, approver, decision, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a letter and request context arrive from verification-letter-policy, create an approval task in BambooHR for the HR Manager and record it in state. When an HR Manager approval or rejection event arrives from BambooHR, forward the decision (approved or rejected) along with the document reference back to verification-letter-policy via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward HR Manager approval or rejection decision with document reference when a decision event is received

event_sources: external system: HR Manager submits approval or rejection decision in BambooHR workflow

objects.role: Write service that delivers the approved employment verification letter PDF to the employee via email.
objects.state_description: Log of delivered emails including recipient address, document reference, delivery timestamp, and status.
objects.behavior: When an approved letter PDF and employee email address are received, send the letter as an email attachment to the employee and record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Thompson requests an employment verification letter through the HR self-service portal. Employee ID: MT-4821, email: marcus.thompson@company.com. The letter is for a standard employment verification (no salary disclosure requested).

expect.action: Employment verification letter delivered to marcus.thompson@company.com as a PDF attachment, confirming Marcus Thompson's employment details including job title, department, employment start date, and employment status, following HR Manager approval via BambooHR workflow.
expect.reason: hr-portal forwards the request to verification-letter-policy, which retrieves employee data from workday-directory, generates the PDF letter, routes it through bamboohr-approval for HR Manager sign-off, and upon approval email-delivery sends the letter to the employee's email address.

steps.text: HR Manager Jennifer Okafor approves the employment verification letter request for Marcus Thompson in the BambooHR workflow. Approval decision: Approved. Task ID: BHRAPP-20241.

expect.action: Approved employment verification letter PDF sent to marcus.thompson@company.com as an email attachment via email-delivery.
expect.reason: bamboohr-approval receives the HR Manager's approval decision and forwards the approved letter and employee email address to email-delivery, which sends the letter to the employee.

name: Employment Verification Letter Generator
raw_steps: Employee submits a travel verification letter request.
System retrieves employee details from the HRIS.
System generates a professional verification letter using a customizable template.
System delivers the completed letter to the employee.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the online HR portal form that receives employee verification letter requests.
objects.state_description: Records submitted verification letter requests including employee ID, request timestamp, and request details.
objects.behavior: When a verification letter request is submitted via the HR portal form, forward the request payload to the verification-letter-policy business logic object. Record the submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward employee verification letter request details when a new form submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: employee submits verification letter request via HR portal form

objects.role: Read service representing Workday that retrieves employee details on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for employee details such as name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and manager. When answering queries, call the `workday_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies verification letter rules, orchestrates data retrieval, and drives letter generation and delivery.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight verification letter requests including employee ID, retrieved employee details, generated document reference, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a verification letter request arrives from hr-portal, query workday-directory for the employee's details (name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status). Once employee details are retrieved, send them to google-docs-generator to produce the formatted verification letter. When the generated document reference is returned from google-docs-generator, send the document reference and employee email address to email-delivery to deliver the completed letter to the employee.

peers.relationship: Query for employee details when a new verification letter request is received
Send employee details and letter template instructions to generate the verification letter
Send the completed letter document reference and employee email address to deliver the letter

objects.role: Read/write service representing Google Docs that generates a professional verification letter from a customizable template and returns the document reference.
objects.state_description: Tracks generated documents with their document IDs, employee references, and generation timestamps.
objects.behavior: When employee details and template instructions are received, create a professional verification letter using the customizable Google Docs template populated with the provided employee data. Return the generated document reference (URL or ID) to the caller. When answering queries for generated document references, call the `google_docs_generator_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service representing the email notification system that delivers the completed verification letter to the employee.
objects.state_description: Records sent emails including recipient address, document reference, send timestamp, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a document reference and recipient email address are received, send the verification letter to the employee via email with the document attached or linked. Record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Thompson, Senior Financial Analyst in the Finance department, submits a verification letter request via the HR portal form. Request includes: employee name Marcus Thompson, employee email marcus.thompson@company.com, purpose of letter: international travel visa application.

expect.action: Verification letter emailed to marcus.thompson@company.com containing Marcus Thompson's employment details (job title: Senior Financial Analyst, department: Finance, employment start date, employment status) generated from the Google Docs template, with the document attached or linked.
expect.reason: hr-portal forwards the request to verification-letter-policy, which queries workday-directory for employee details, instructs google-docs-generator to produce the letter, and triggers email-delivery to send the completed letter to marcus.thompson@company.com.

name: AI-Powered Contract Risk Assessment
raw_steps: Receive purchase requests or contract documents for review.
Use AI to perform initial risk assessment on the documents.
Categorize documents based on type and content.
Flag potential issues based on custom risk criteria.
Route documents to appropriate team members based on risk level.
Enable legal team to focus on negotiation strategy and high-value work.
Notify stakeholders of document status to prevent procurement delays.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives contract documents and purchase requests submitted to the #contract-review Slack channel
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming messages and file attachments received in the #contract-review channel
objects.behavior: When a message or file is posted to the #contract-review channel, forward the document and submitter metadata to the contract-risk-assessment business logic object. Record all received submissions.

peers.relationship: Forward document content and submitter details when a new submission arrives in the channel

event_sources: slack: message posted to #contract-review channel

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives contract documents and purchase requests submitted via email
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming emails with attachments received in the contract review inbox
objects.behavior: When an email with a contract document or purchase request arrives, forward the document content, sender, and subject to the contract-risk-assessment business logic object. Record all received submissions.

peers.relationship: Forward document content and sender metadata when a new contract email arrives

event_sources: external system: email received in contract review inbox

objects.role: Core business logic object that classifies contract documents, assesses risk, flags issues, and routes to the appropriate legal counsel
objects.state_description: Tracks each document under review with its type classification, risk level, flagged issues, submitter, source channel, assigned reviewer, and current review stage
objects.behavior: When a document arrives from any entry-point, use the gpt4-analysis skill to perform initial risk assessment on the document content. Classify the document into one of: Vendor Agreement, Non-Disclosure Agreement, Statement of Work, or Purchase Order based on its content and structure. Apply risk flagging rules: flag indemnification clauses that are overly broad or one-sided, flag absent or insufficient liability caps, flag payment terms exceeding net-60 days, and flag auto-renewal provisions without adequate notice periods. Determine overall risk level: low risk if zero or one minor flags with no high-severity issues, medium risk if two or more flags or one moderate-severity flag, high risk if any high-severity flag or three or more flags of any kind. Route based on risk level: send low-risk documents to Junior Legal Counsel, medium-risk documents to Senior Legal Counsel, and high-risk documents to General Counsel. Send document summary, classification, risk level, and all flagged issues to the legal-routing write service. Send status notification to the stakeholder-notifications service simultaneously.

peers.relationship: Send document classification, risk assessment, flagged issues, risk level, and assigned counsel when assessment is complete
Send status update with document details, risk level, and assigned reviewer at each stage of the review process

objects.role: Write service that records document routing assignments and maintains the audit trail of legal counsel assignments
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all document routing decisions including document ID, type, risk level, flagged issues, assigned counsel tier, timestamp, and source channel
objects.behavior: When a routing record arrives, store it with full metadata including document classification, risk level, flags, assigned counsel, submitter, and timestamp. Record is appended to the routing log.

objects.role: Write service that sends email notifications to submitters and stakeholders about document status at each stage of the review process
objects.state_description: Log of all notifications sent including recipient, document reference, status message, risk level, assigned reviewer, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send an email to the specified stakeholder(s) with the document status, risk classification, assigned legal counsel, and any relevant flags. Record the notification in the outbound log.

steps.text: A message has been posted to the #contract-review Slack channel by Marcus Chen, Procurement Manager (user ID: U04XKQP21, channel ID: C07CONTRACTREV, thread ID: 1718200000.000100). The message reads: 'Hi Legal Team, please review the attached Vendor Agreement with Apex Solutions Inc. for IT infrastructure services. Contract value is $450,000 annually. Key concerns: the indemnification clause appears broad, liability cap is set at $50,000, and there is an auto-renewal provision with 90-day notice required to cancel. Payment terms are net-75. Attached: Apex_Solutions_Vendor_Agreement_2024.pdf'

expect.action: Routing record written to legal-routing assigning the Apex Solutions Inc. Vendor Agreement to General Counsel (high-risk) due to flagged issues: broad indemnification clause, liability cap below contract value, payment terms exceeding net-60 (net-75), and auto-renewal provision. Notification email sent via stakeholder-notifications to Marcus Chen confirming receipt, document classification as Vendor Agreement, risk level high, assigned counsel General Counsel, and listing the flagged risk items.
expect.reason: contract-risk-assessment classifies the document, flags multiple risk criteria (indemnification, liability cap, net-75 payment terms, auto-renewal), assigns high-risk routing to General Counsel via legal-routing, and triggers stakeholder-notifications to email the submitter with document status.

steps.text: An email has arrived in the contract review inbox from Sandra Okafor, VP of Business Development (sandra.okafor@partnercorp.com), subject: 'NDA Review Request – PartnerCorp & Meridian Tech'. The email body reads: 'Please find attached a Non-Disclosure Agreement between PartnerCorp and Meridian Tech for a potential joint product development initiative. The NDA includes a mutual confidentiality clause, a 2-year term with auto-renewal unless terminated with 30-day notice, and standard liability language with no explicit cap. Please advise on any concerns. Attached: PartnerCorp_Meridian_NDA_2024.pdf'

expect.action: Routing record written to legal-routing assigning the PartnerCorp–Meridian Tech Non-Disclosure Agreement to Senior Legal Counsel (medium-risk) due to flagged issues: auto-renewal provision and absence of a liability cap. Notification email sent via stakeholder-notifications to Sandra Okafor (sandra.okafor@partnercorp.com) confirming receipt, document classification as Non-Disclosure Agreement, risk level medium, assigned counsel Senior Legal Counsel, and listing the flagged risk items.
expect.reason: contract-risk-assessment classifies the document as an NDA, flags auto-renewal provision and missing liability cap as medium-risk criteria, routes to Senior Legal Counsel via legal-routing, and triggers stakeholder-notifications to email the submitter with document status.

name: Fathom Meetings to Slack Summaries
raw_steps: Capture team meeting transcripts from Fathom.
Generate AI-powered summaries with action items and owners.
Post the summaries and meeting notes to the team Slack channel.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures meeting transcripts and AI summaries from Fathom for the Engineering team.
objects.state_description: Tracks received meeting events including transcript content, AI-generated summaries, action items, and owners.
objects.behavior: When a new meeting summary event arrives from Fathom, forward the transcript, AI summary, action items, and owner assignments to the meeting-summary-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward meeting transcript, AI summary, action items, and owners when a new Fathom meeting summary is available.

event_sources: external system: Fathom meeting completed with AI summary

objects.role: Business logic object that processes Fathom meeting data, structures summaries with action items and owners, and prepares formatted content for Slack posting.
objects.state_description: Tracks processed meeting summaries including formatted output, action item lists, and owner assignments.
objects.behavior: When a meeting summary arrives from fathom-meetings, extract and validate the AI-generated summary, action items, and owners. Format the content into a well-structured Slack message including meeting title, date, summary paragraph, and a bulleted list of action items each with their assigned owner. Send the formatted Slack message to the slack-engineering-channel peer.

peers.relationship: Send formatted meeting summary with action items and owners for posting to the #engineering-team-meetings channel.

objects.role: Write service object that posts meeting summaries and notes to the #engineering-team-meetings Slack channel.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all messages posted to #engineering-team-meetings including timestamps and content.
objects.behavior: When a formatted meeting summary message is received, post it to the #engineering-team-meetings Slack channel. Record each post in the audit trail with timestamp and content.

steps.text: Fathom meeting completed for Engineering team. Meeting title: 'Engineering Sprint Planning Q3 - Week 8'. Attendees: Sarah Chen (Engineering Manager), Marcus Webb (Backend Engineer), Priya Nair (Frontend Engineer), Jordan Lee (DevOps Engineer). Duration: 52 minutes. Transcript: [full meeting transcript attached]. AI Summary: The team reviewed sprint progress, identified two blockers in the authentication service, and aligned on deployment timelines for the new API gateway. Action items: 1) Marcus Webb to resolve the OAuth token refresh bug by Friday EOD. 2) Priya Nair to complete the dashboard performance audit by next Monday. 3) Jordan Lee to set up staging environment for API gateway by Wednesday. 4) Sarah Chen to schedule a follow-up architecture review for next Thursday.

expect.action: Formatted meeting summary message posted to the #engineering-team-meetings Slack channel, including meeting title 'Engineering Sprint Planning Q3 - Week 8', AI-generated summary, and structured action items with owners: Marcus Webb (OAuth token refresh bug by Friday EOD), Priya Nair (dashboard performance audit by next Monday), Jordan Lee (staging environment for API gateway by Wednesday), and Sarah Chen (architecture review follow-up next Thursday).
expect.reason: fathom-meetings forwards the transcript and AI summary to meeting-summary-processor, which formats the content into a structured Slack message and sends it to slack-engineering-channel, which posts it to #engineering-team-meetings.

name: Granola Notes to Slack Summaries
raw_steps: Trigger: Detect new or updated meeting notes in Granola
Extract meeting notes content from Granola
Process notes through AI-powered summarization to highlight key decisions and action items
Format the summary for Slack
Post the formatted summary to designated Slack channels

objects.role: Entry-point service object that detects new or updated meeting notes in Granola and forwards them to the summarization pipeline
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen meeting note IDs and timestamps to detect new or updated notes
objects.behavior: When a new or updated meeting note is detected, forward the full meeting notes content to the meeting-summarizer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full meeting notes content when a new or updated note is detected

event_sources: external system: Granola meeting note created or updated

objects.role: Business logic object that processes raw meeting notes to extract key decisions and action items and formats the result for Slack
objects.state_description: Tracks notes currently being processed including their source meeting ID and processing status
objects.behavior: When raw meeting notes arrive, analyze the content to identify and extract key decisions made and action items assigned. Produce a concise structured summary highlighting these elements. Format the summary for Slack using clear sections (e.g., *Key Decisions* and *Action Items*) with bullet points and appropriate Slack markdown. Send the formatted summary to the slack-meeting-summaries peer.

peers.relationship: Send the formatted Slack summary after processing meeting notes

objects.role: Write service object that posts formatted meeting summaries to the #meeting-summaries Slack channel
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all summaries posted, including meeting source, timestamp, and message content
objects.behavior: When a formatted summary message is received, post it to the #meeting-summaries Slack channel. Record the posted message details in state as an audit entry.

steps.text: New meeting note detected in Granola: Meeting title 'Q3 Product Roadmap Review', attendees: Sarah Chen (Head of Product), Marcus Webb (Engineering Lead), Priya Nair (Design Lead), Jordan Kim (CEO). Meeting date: today. Notes content: 'Discussed Q3 priorities. Key decisions: (1) Launch v2.0 feature set by September 15th, (2) Deprioritize mobile app redesign until Q4, (3) Allocate 2 additional engineers to the payments team. Action items: Sarah to finalize feature specs by July 10th, Marcus to identify engineers for payments team reallocation by July 12th, Priya to deliver updated design system components by July 20th, Jordan to communicate roadmap changes to board by July 15th.'

expect.action: A formatted summary message is posted to the #meeting-summaries Slack channel. The message highlights key decisions: (1) Launch v2.0 feature set by September 15th, (2) Deprioritize mobile app redesign until Q4, (3) Allocate 2 additional engineers to the payments team. Action items listed: Sarah Chen to finalize feature specs by July 10th, Marcus Webb to identify engineers for payments team reallocation by July 12th, Priya Nair to deliver updated design system components by July 20th, Jordan Kim to communicate roadmap changes to board by July 15th.
expect.reason: granola forwards meeting notes to meeting-summarizer, which extracts decisions and action items and formats a summary, then passes it to slack-meeting-summaries which posts to #meeting-summaries

name: Target Account Engagement Sales Handoff
raw_steps: Detect when a target account downloads marketing assets.
Enrich the lead data with AI-powered research.
Generate a personalized outreach email draft.
Deliver the alert and email draft to the sales rep.
Sales rep reviews and sends the outreach message to the prospect.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that detects when a target account downloads a whitepaper or case study from the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks download events including asset name, prospect email, company domain, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a whitepaper or case study download event is detected, forward the download event payload (prospect email, company domain, asset downloaded, timestamp) to the lead-enrichment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward download event payload when a target account download is detected

event_sources: webhook: asset download event from company website

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates lead enrichment, email draft generation, and alert delivery for target account download events.
objects.state_description: Tracks enrichment requests with prospect email, company domain, asset downloaded, enriched company profile, generated email draft, and assigned Account Executive.
objects.behavior: When a download event arrives, request enrichment data from the clearbit-enrichment read service using the company domain and prospect email. Once enriched data is received (company size, industry, key decision-maker details), send the enriched lead profile and downloaded asset name to the email-draft-generator peer to produce a personalized outreach email draft. When the email draft is returned, send the complete alert package (prospect details, enriched profile, asset downloaded, email draft) to the slack-alerts write service for delivery to the assigned Account Executive.

peers.relationship: Send company domain and prospect email to retrieve enriched company and decision-maker data
Send enriched lead profile and downloaded asset details to generate a personalized outreach email draft
Send alert package with prospect details, enriched profile, and email draft when ready for Account Executive review

objects.role: Read service that retrieves company size, industry, and key decision-maker details from Clearbit using company domain and prospect email.
objects.behavior: Responds to enrichment queries by looking up company and contact data from Clearbit. When answering queries, call the `clearbit_enrichment_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that generates a personalized outreach email draft using OpenAI GPT-4 based on the downloaded asset and enriched lead profile.
objects.state_description: Tracks draft generation requests with enriched lead profile, asset downloaded, and generated email draft output.
objects.behavior: When an enriched lead profile and downloaded asset details are received, compose a prompt incorporating the prospect's company size, industry, decision-maker details, and the specific asset they downloaded. Generate a personalized outreach email draft using GPT-4 that references the downloaded content and speaks to the prospect's likely business context. Return the generated email draft to the lead-enrichment peer.

peers.relationship: Return the generated personalized email draft when composition is complete

objects.role: Write service that delivers target account alert messages and email drafts to the #target-account-alerts Slack channel for Account Executive review.
objects.state_description: Tracks all posted alerts including prospect details, enriched profile summaries, email drafts, timestamp, and assigned Account Executive.
objects.behavior: When an alert package is received, post a formatted Slack message to the #target-account-alerts channel containing the prospect's name, company, downloaded asset, enriched company profile highlights, and the personalized email draft. Tag the assigned Account Executive in the message. When the Account Executive approves and submits the email draft via Slack, forward the send instruction and email draft to the gmail-outreach peer.

peers.relationship: Forward the approved email draft and prospect contact details when the Account Executive confirms sending via Slack

event_sources: external system: Slack interactive action (Account Executive approves email send in #target-account-alerts)

objects.role: Write service that sends the approved personalized outreach email to the prospect via Gmail on behalf of the Account Executive.
objects.state_description: Tracks all sent outreach emails including recipient, subject, body, sender Account Executive, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an approved email draft and prospect contact details are received, send the outreach email to the prospect via Gmail using the Account Executive's account. Record the sent email details including recipient, subject, body, and timestamp.

steps.text: A whitepaper download event has been detected from the company website. Prospect: Sarah Chen, VP of Engineering at Meridian Technologies (domain: meridiantech.io, email: schen@meridiantech.io). Asset downloaded: 'Scaling Microservices in Regulated Industries' whitepaper. Timestamp: 2024-01-15T14:32:00Z.

expect.action: A Slack message is posted to #target-account-alerts mentioning Sarah Chen at Meridian Technologies, the downloaded 'Scaling Microservices in Regulated Industries' whitepaper, enriched company profile highlights, and a personalized outreach email draft generated by GPT-4 addressed to schen@meridiantech.io, delivered to the assigned Account Executive for review.
expect.reason: website-downloads forwards the download event to lead-enrichment, which enriches via clearbit-enrichment, generates a draft via email-draft-generator, and delivers the alert package to slack-alerts for posting to #target-account-alerts

steps.text: Account Executive Marcus Rivera approves the outreach email draft in the #target-account-alerts Slack channel for prospect Sarah Chen at Meridian Technologies (schen@meridiantech.io). The approved email draft subject is 'How Meridian Technologies Can Scale Microservices Compliantly' and the message body references the whitepaper download and Meridian's regulated industry context. Slack action timestamp: 2024-01-15T14:45:00Z.

expect.action: Outreach email sent to schen@meridiantech.io via Marcus Rivera's Gmail account with subject 'How Meridian Technologies Can Scale Microservices Compliantly', and the sent email details are recorded.
expect.reason: slack-alerts receives the Slack interactive approval action and forwards the approved email draft and prospect contact details to gmail-outreach, which sends the email via Gmail on behalf of the Account Executive

name: Intelligent Multi-Channel Message Routing
raw_steps: Collect new emails from multiple email inboxes.
Collect form submissions from web forms.
Collect messages from social media channels.
Use AI to automatically categorize each message.
Use AI to summarize each message.
Use AI to prioritize each message.
Route each message to the appropriate team based on categorization.
Send instant notifications to team members through their preferred channels.
Allow team members to update message status as they work.
Maintain visibility across all messages and their current status.

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new emails from Gmail and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected email message IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Gmail, forward the full message payload (sender, subject, body, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new Gmail email payload when a new message is received

event_sources: external system: Gmail new email received

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new emails from Outlook and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected email message IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Outlook, forward the full message payload (sender, subject, body, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new Outlook email payload when a new message is received

event_sources: external system: Outlook new email received

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new support tickets from Zendesk and forwards them for processing.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected Zendesk ticket IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new ticket is created in Zendesk, forward the full ticket payload (requester, subject, description, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new Zendesk ticket payload when a new ticket is created

event_sources: external system: Zendesk ticket created

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects Contact Us form submissions from the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission IDs and their raw payloads.
objects.behavior: When a Contact Us form is submitted, forward the full submission payload (name, email, message, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Contact Us form submission payload when a new submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Contact Us form submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects Request a Demo form submissions from the company website.
objects.state_description: Tracks received form submission IDs and their raw payloads.
objects.behavior: When a Request a Demo form is submitted, forward the full submission payload (name, email, company, message, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Request a Demo form submission payload when a new submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: Request a Demo form submitted

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new messages from the company Facebook Page.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected Facebook message IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new message arrives on the Facebook Page, forward the full message payload (sender, content, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new Facebook Page message payload when a new message is received

event_sources: external system: Facebook Page new message received

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new messages and mentions from the company Twitter account.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected Twitter message/mention IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new message or mention arrives on Twitter, forward the full payload (sender, content, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new Twitter message or mention payload when received

event_sources: external system: Twitter new message or mention received

objects.role: Entry-point service that collects new messages from the company LinkedIn account.
objects.state_description: Tracks last-polled timestamp and list of collected LinkedIn message IDs.
objects.behavior: When a new message arrives on LinkedIn, forward the full message payload (sender, content, timestamp) to message-triage as a peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward new LinkedIn message payload when a new message is received

event_sources: external system: LinkedIn new message received

objects.role: Business logic object that categorizes, summarizes, prioritizes, and routes incoming messages from all channels to the appropriate team.
objects.state_description: Tracks each processed message with its source channel, raw content, AI-assigned category (Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing, General Feedback), one-sentence summary, priority (High, Medium, Low), assigned team, and current status (In Progress, Awaiting Response, Resolved).
objects.behavior: When a message arrives from any source channel, apply AI to categorize it as one of: Sales Inquiry, Technical Support, Billing, or General Feedback. Apply AI to produce a one-sentence summary of the message. Apply AI to assign a priority of High, Medium, or Low based on urgency and content signals such as keywords, tone, and context. Determine the destination team: Sales Inquiry → Sales team; Technical Support → Technical Support team; Billing → Finance team; General Feedback → Customer Success team. Send the enriched message record (original content, source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, initial status of New) to airtable-dashboard. For messages routed to Sales or Technical Support teams, send a Slack notification to slack-notifications. For messages routed to Finance or Customer Success teams, send an email notification to email-notifications. When a team member submits a status update (In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved) for a message, update the message status in internal state and send the updated record to airtable-dashboard.

peers.relationship: Send enriched message record with category, summary, priority, assigned team, and status on every new message and on every status update
Send Slack notification payload with message summary, category, priority, and assigned team when routing to Sales or Technical Support teams
Send email notification payload with message summary, category, priority, and assigned team when routing to Finance or Customer Success teams

objects.role: Write service that maintains the shared Airtable dashboard with all messages and their current statuses.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all message records written, including source, category, summary, priority, assigned team, and current status.
objects.behavior: When a message record or status update is received, write or update the corresponding record in the Airtable base to keep the shared dashboard current.

objects.role: Write service that sends instant Slack notifications to Sales and Technical Support team members.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent, including recipient team, message summary, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification payload is received, post the message to the appropriate Slack channel for the designated team.

objects.role: Write service that sends email notifications to Finance and Customer Success team members.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all email notifications sent, including recipient team, message summary, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification payload is received, send an email to the appropriate team distribution list with the message summary, category, and priority details.

steps.text: A new email arrives in Gmail from Marcus Delgado (marcus.delgado@techcorp.com) with subject 'Urgent: Production API down – need immediate support' and body: 'Our production environment has been completely down for the past 2 hours due to what appears to be an API authentication failure. This is severely impacting our operations and we need emergency assistance ASAP. We have over 500 users affected.' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:14:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Marcus Delgado's email categorized as Technical Support, summarized as 'Production API authentication failure causing a 2-hour outage affecting 500 users requires emergency support,' prioritized as High; Slack notification posted to the Technical Support team channel mentioning the message from marcus.delgado@techcorp.com with subject 'Urgent: Production API down – need immediate support', category Technical Support, and priority High.
expect.reason: gmail-inbox forwards the email to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to slack-notifications (Technical Support) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

steps.text: A new email arrives in Outlook from Sandra Okafor (sandra.okafor@globalventures.com) with subject 'Interested in Enterprise Plan pricing' and body: 'Hi, we are a 300-person company currently evaluating CRM solutions. Could you send us pricing details for your Enterprise Plan and schedule a call with your sales team? We are looking to make a decision within the next two weeks.' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:22:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Sandra Okafor's email categorized as Sales Inquiry, summarized as 'A 300-person company is requesting Enterprise Plan pricing and a sales call with a two-week decision timeline,' prioritized as High; Slack notification posted to the Sales team channel mentioning the message from sandra.okafor@globalventures.com with subject 'Interested in Enterprise Plan pricing', category Sales Inquiry, and priority High.
expect.reason: outlook-inbox forwards the email to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to slack-notifications (Sales) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

steps.text: A new support ticket is created in Zendesk from Priya Nair (priya.nair@retailplus.io), subject 'Overcharged on last invoice – Invoice #INV-20240531', description: 'I was charged $1,200 on invoice INV-20240531 but my contract states a monthly fee of $800. Please review and issue a corrected invoice or refund the difference of $400.' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:35:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Priya Nair's Zendesk ticket categorized as Billing, summarized as 'Customer was overcharged $400 on invoice INV-20240531 and is requesting a corrected invoice or refund,' prioritized as High; email notification sent to the Finance team distribution list with message summary, category Billing, and priority High for Priya Nair's ticket.
expect.reason: zendesk-inbox forwards the ticket to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to email-notifications (Finance) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

steps.text: A Contact Us form is submitted on the company website by Daniel Ferreira (daniel.ferreira@homedesigns.com), message: 'I just wanted to say that your product has genuinely transformed how our small team collaborates. The interface is intuitive and the customer support has been outstanding. Keep up the great work!' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T09:48:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Daniel Ferreira's Contact Us submission categorized as General Feedback, summarized as 'A customer praises the product for improving team collaboration, citing an intuitive interface and outstanding support,' prioritized as Low; email notification sent to the Customer Success team distribution list with message summary, category General Feedback, and priority Low for Daniel Ferreira's submission.
expect.reason: contact-form forwards the submission to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to email-notifications (Customer Success) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

steps.text: A Request a Demo form is submitted on the company website by Aisha Kowalski (aisha.kowalski@finbridge.com), company: FinBridge Solutions, message: 'We are a fintech startup with 80 employees looking to automate our customer communication workflows. I would like to schedule a live demo with your team to see how your platform can fit our needs.' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T10:02:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Aisha Kowalski's demo request categorized as Sales Inquiry, summarized as 'An 80-person fintech startup wants to schedule a live demo to evaluate the platform for automating customer communication workflows,' prioritized as High; Slack notification posted to the Sales team channel mentioning the demo request from aisha.kowalski@finbridge.com at FinBridge Solutions, category Sales Inquiry, and priority High.
expect.reason: demo-request-form forwards the submission to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to slack-notifications (Sales) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

steps.text: A new message arrives on the company Facebook Page from Carlos Mendes (Facebook user ID: fb_carlos_mendes_4821), content: 'Hey, I have been trying to reset my password for 3 days and keep getting an error saying my account is locked. I have emailed support twice with no response. This is really frustrating.' Timestamp: 2024-06-10T10:15:00Z.

expect.action: Airtable record created for Carlos Mendes's Facebook message categorized as Technical Support, summarized as 'A user has been unable to reset their password for three days due to an account lock error and has received no response from support,' prioritized as High; Slack notification posted to the Technical Support team channel mentioning the message from Facebook user Carlos Mendes (fb_carlos_mendes_4821), category Technical Support, and priority High.
expect.reason: facebook-messages forwards the message to message-triage, which categorizes, summarizes, and prioritizes it, then routes it to slack-notifications (Technical Support) and airtable-dashboard per its peer declarations.

name: G2 Reviews to Productboard Pipeline
raw_steps: Trigger: Monitor G2 for new reviews
Collect review data from G2
Enrich review data with user information when available
Send enriched reviews to Productboard
Track sentiment changes over time in Productboard

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors G2 for new reviews on the company product profile and forwards them into the pipeline
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen review timestamp and review IDs already processed to avoid duplicates
objects.behavior: When a new review is detected on the G2 product profile, extract reviewer name, rating, review text, and submission date from the event payload, then forward the review data to the review-enrichment peer.

peers.relationship: Forward raw review data (reviewer name, rating, review text, submission date) when a new review is detected

event_sources: polling: G2 product profile new review

objects.role: Read service that retrieves user and account information from Salesforce by reviewer identity
objects.behavior: Responds to lookup queries for user or account information by reviewer name or email. When answering queries, call the `salesforce_crm_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that enriches raw G2 review data with Salesforce user information and prepares the final enriched feedback payload
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight enrichment requests with their review data and Salesforce lookup results
objects.behavior: When a raw review arrives, attempt to look up the reviewer in Salesforce using the reviewer name or any available identifying information. If a Salesforce record is found, merge the CRM data (account name, customer tier, contact details) with the review data to produce an enriched payload. If no Salesforce record is found, mark the review as unenriched and proceed with the original review data. Once the enriched or unenriched payload is ready, send it to productboard-feedback for ingestion.

peers.relationship: Query for user and account information using reviewer name when a new review arrives
Send enriched review payload (review text, rating, reviewer name, submission date, CRM data if available, source tag G2) once enrichment is complete

objects.role: Write service that records enriched G2 review data as user feedback notes in Productboard tagged with the G2 source
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all feedback notes submitted to Productboard including review ID, rating, reviewer, submission date, enrichment status, and Productboard note ID
objects.behavior: When an enriched review payload arrives, record it as a user feedback note in Productboard with the G2 source tag, reviewer details, rating, review text, and any available CRM enrichment. Log the resulting Productboard note ID and submission timestamp in state.

steps.text: New review detected on G2 product profile: Reviewer: Marcus Delgado, Senior Product Manager at Brightline Technologies. Rating: 4 out of 5 stars. Review text: 'We've been using this product for about eight months now and overall the experience has been positive. The onboarding was smooth and the support team is responsive. The analytics dashboard still has some rough edges but the core functionality is solid and has saved our team significant time.' Submission date: 2024-06-12.

name: Automated Offline Conversion Tracking
raw_steps: Monitor CRM for new conversion events.
Extract conversion data from CRM.
Deduplicate conversion records to prevent duplicates.
Send conversion data to Facebook Ads platform.
Send conversion data to Google Ads platform.
Send conversion data to TikTok Ads platform.
Send conversion data to LinkedIn Ads platform.
Retry failed conversions with built-in retry logic.
Confirm successful conversion sync across all platforms.

objects.role: Entry-point service that monitors Salesforce for new conversion events and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last polled timestamp and list of conversion event IDs seen to detect new arrivals.
objects.behavior: When a new conversion event is detected in Salesforce, forward the full conversion payload to the conversion-dedup peer. Emit one message per new conversion event.

peers.relationship: Forward raw conversion payload when a new Salesforce conversion event is detected

event_sources: external system: Salesforce conversion event created

objects.role: Business logic object that deduplicates incoming conversion records before they are distributed to ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Maintains a set of seen conversion record identifiers (e.g., Salesforce opportunity or lead IDs combined with event type and timestamp) to detect and discard duplicates.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload arrives, extract the unique conversion identifier. If the identifier has already been processed, discard the record and take no further action. If the identifier is new, record it as seen and forward the deduplicated conversion data to all ad platform sync peers simultaneously. Send to facebook-ads-sync, google-ads-sync, tiktok-ads-sync, and linkedin-ads-sync.

peers.relationship: Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the Facebook Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for Facebook Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the Facebook Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the Google Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for Google Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the Google Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the TikTok Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for TikTok Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the TikTok Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the LinkedIn Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for LinkedIn Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the LinkedIn Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that records and aggregates cross-platform conversion sync outcomes as the authoritative audit trail.
objects.state_description: Maintains a log of all conversion sync results per platform, keyed by conversion ID, including status (success/failure), platform name, timestamp, and error details where applicable.
objects.behavior: When a sync outcome message arrives from any ad platform sync object, append the result to the audit log keyed by conversion ID and platform. Track whether all four platforms have reported for a given conversion. When all four platforms have reported for a conversion, compute the overall sync status: fully synced if all succeeded, partially synced if some failed, or fully failed if all failed. Record the aggregate status in state.

steps.text: A new conversion event has been created in Salesforce for contact Marcus Rivera (Lead ID: SF-LEAD-00482, Email: marcus.rivera@techcorp.com, Phone: +1-415-555-0192, Conversion Type: Demo Request, Conversion Value: $1200, Currency: USD, Conversion Timestamp: 2024-06-12T14:23:00Z, Campaign: Q2 Enterprise Outreach, Ad Platform Click IDs: fbclid=Fb8k2mXq9, gclid=Ggl7nPwR3, ttclid=TtK4vLzM1, li_fat_id=Li2xQpNs5).

expect.action: Audit log entries written to conversion-sync-audit for conversion ID SF-LEAD-00482, recording sync outcomes from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and LinkedIn Ads platforms, each marked as success or failure with retry details, keyed by conversion ID SF-LEAD-00482 and respective platform names.
expect.reason: salesforce-conversions forwards the payload to conversion-dedup, which deduplicates and fans out to all four ad sync objects; each sync object sends conversion data to its respective ad platform API with retry logic and reports the outcome to conversion-sync-audit, which appends results to the audit log.

name: Customer Interview Signup Automation
raw_steps: Customer interviews are scheduled and automatically appear in a centralized Zapier Table database.
Team members across the organization browse upcoming interview sessions in the database.
Team members add themselves as attendees to sessions without requiring coordination from researchers.
Calendar events automatically update to reflect new attendees.
Attendance information stays organized in the database.
User researchers can focus on facilitating interviews instead of managing invites and coordination.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Tables database where interview sessions are stored and attendee signups are recorded; also the entry point for new attendee-added events.
objects.state_description: Tracks all interview session records including scheduled time, session details, and current attendee lists as they are updated in Zapier Tables.
objects.behavior: When a new interview session is created and added to Zapier Tables, forward the session details to interview-signup-policy. When a team member adds themselves as an attendee to a session in Zapier Tables, forward the attendee-added event (including session ID and new attendee details) to interview-signup-policy. Record all session and attendee updates received from interview-signup-policy.

peers.relationship: Forward new session creation events and attendee-added events with full session and attendee details

event_sources: external system: Zapier Tables record created (new interview session)
external system: Zapier Tables record updated (attendee added to session)

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces signup rules, validates attendee additions, and coordinates updates to the calendar and database.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight signup events including session ID, attendee details, and the current state of calendar and database update operations.
objects.behavior: When a new interview session event arrives, validate that it contains required fields (session ID, scheduled time, interview topic). When an attendee-added event arrives, confirm the session exists and the attendee is a valid team member from Product, Engineering, or Marketing. Once validated, send the updated attendee list and session details to google-calendar for calendar event update and confirm the attendee record back to interview-sessions for persistence. If validation fails, discard the event and log the reason in state.

peers.relationship: Send updated session details and full attendee list when a validated attendee addition occurs, to update the Google Calendar event
Send confirmed attendee update to persist the finalized attendee record in Zapier Tables

objects.role: Write service object representing Google Calendar; receives updated session and attendee information and updates the corresponding calendar event.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all calendar event update requests received, including session ID, attendee lists, and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When an update request is received containing session details and attendee list, record the update and apply it to the corresponding Google Calendar event to reflect the new attendees. No confirmation is sent back to callers.

steps.text: A new interview session has been created and added to Zapier Tables. Session details: Session ID: IS-2047, Scheduled Time: 2024-02-15 at 2:00 PM PST, Interview Topic: Mobile App Onboarding Experience, Interviewer: Dr. Sarah Chen (User Researcher), Meeting Link: https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij, Google Calendar Event ID: gcal_event_88321.

expect.action: Session IS-2047 details validated and stored; Google Calendar event gcal_event_88321 confirmed as linked to session IS-2047 with topic 'Mobile App Onboarding Experience' scheduled for 2024-02-15 at 2:00 PM PST.
expect.reason: interview-signup-policy validates required fields (session ID, scheduled time, interview topic) on new session creation and coordinates with google-calendar to register the event.

steps.text: A team member has added themselves as an attendee to an existing interview session in Zapier Tables. Session ID: IS-2047, New Attendee: Marcus Rivera, Product Manager. Updated attendee list: Dr. Sarah Chen (User Researcher), Marcus Rivera (Product Manager).

expect.action: Google Calendar event gcal_event_88321 updated to include Marcus Rivera (Product Manager) as an attendee, reflecting the full attendee list: Dr. Sarah Chen and Marcus Rivera.
expect.reason: interview-signup-policy confirms session IS-2047 exists and the attendee is valid, then forwards the updated attendee list to google-calendar, which applies the update to the corresponding calendar event.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify google-calendar.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to google-calendar.

mock_tools.description: Write or notify interview-sessions.
mock_tools.response_template: Delivered to interview-sessions.
