name: AI Image Generator with OpenAI
raw_steps: Create a customizable form in the Zapier Interface to collect user inputs such as image style and art style preferences.
Submit the form data as a trigger to initiate the automation.
Map the form fields into a prompt and send the prompt to OpenAI's DALL-E API to generate an image.
Receive the generated image and its URL from OpenAI.
Store the image link and prompt details in a Table for viewing on the Interface.
Optionally upload the image to an external storage service like Google Drive to preserve it beyond OpenAI's expiration window.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interface form that collects user inputs for image generation.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted form entries including image subject, art style, and color palette preferences.
objects.behavior: When a user submits the form with image subject, art style (Photorealistic, Watercolor, Oil Painting, Sketch, Anime), and color palette preferences, forward all field values to the image-prompt-builder peer.

peers.relationship: Forward form submission data (image subject, art style, color palette) when a new form is submitted.

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interface form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that constructs DALL-E prompts from form inputs and coordinates image generation and storage.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current prompt being built, the associated form inputs, and the status of image generation and storage operations.
objects.behavior: When form data arrives, combine the image subject, art style, and color palette into a well-structured DALL-E prompt (e.g., 'A [subject] in [art style] style with [color palette] color palette'). Send the constructed prompt to the dalle-api peer for image generation. When the generated image URL and prompt details are received back, send the image URL and prompt details simultaneously to zapier-table and google-drive.

peers.relationship: Send the constructed image prompt when ready for image generation.
Send the image URL and prompt details for storage after image generation is complete.
Send the image URL and prompt details for upload after image generation is complete.

objects.role: Service object representing the OpenAI DALL-E API that generates images from text prompts.
objects.state_description: Tracks prompts sent and the resulting image URLs and metadata returned by the API.
objects.behavior: When a prompt is received, call the DALL-E API to generate an image. Return the generated image URL and associated metadata to the calling object. When answering queries, call the `dalle_api_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Return the generated image URL and metadata after successful image generation.

objects.role: Write service object representing the Zapier Table that stores image links and prompt details for viewing in the Zapier Interface.
objects.state_description: Accumulates records of image prompts, art styles, color palettes, and generated image URLs submitted by the automation.
objects.behavior: When image URL and prompt details are received, record the image subject, art style, color palette, constructed prompt, and image URL as a new row in the Zapier Table.

objects.role: Write service object representing Google Drive where generated images are uploaded for long-term preservation.
objects.state_description: Accumulates uploaded image files and their associated metadata stored in the designated Google Drive folder.
objects.behavior: When an image URL and prompt details are received, download the image from the provided URL and upload it to the designated Google Drive folder, preserving the image beyond OpenAI's expiration window.

steps.text: User submits the image generation form with the following inputs: image subject 'a majestic wolf howling at the moon', art style 'Watercolor', and color palette 'cool blues and purples'.

expect.action: Image uploaded to Google Drive folder with filename reflecting 'a majestic wolf howling at the moon' in Watercolor style, and a new row recorded in Zapier Table containing the image subject 'a majestic wolf howling at the moon', art style 'Watercolor', color palette 'cool blues and purples', constructed prompt 'A majestic wolf howling at the moon in Watercolor style with cool blues and purples color palette', and the generated DALL-E image URL.
expect.reason: image-form forwards form fields to image-prompt-builder, which constructs the prompt and sends it to dalle-api; upon receiving the image URL, image-prompt-builder writes the record to zapier-table and uploads the image to google-drive.

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from dalle api data. When a prompt is received, call the DALL-E API to generate an image. Return the generated image URL and associated metadata to the calling object. When answering queries, call the `dalle_api_data` too
mock_tools.response_template: {"dalle_api_data": {"request": {"image_subject": "a majestic wolf howling at the moon", "art_style": "Watercolor", "color_palette": "cool blues and purples", "prompt_constructed": "A majestic wolf howling at the moon, rendered in watercolor style with a cool blues and purples color palette", "model": "dall-e-3", "size": "1024x1024", "quality": "standard", "n": 1}, "response": {"image_id": "img-7Kx92mNpQr4TvLwZ3dYc", "image_url": "https://oaidalleapiprodscus.blob.core.windows.net/private/org-4RtNmXkLpQ2vBsYcWdJh/user-generated/img-7Kx92mNpQr4TvLwZ3dYc.png", "revised_prompt": "A majestic wolf standing on a rocky hillside, head raised and howling at a large full moon glowing in the night sky, painted in a flowing watercolor style with soft washes of cool blues and deep purples, misty atmospheric background, ethereal and serene mood", "created_at": "2024-11-14T18:32:07Z", "expires_at": "2024-11-14T20:32:07Z", "format": "png", "resolution": "1024x1024", "file_size_kb": 1248}, "metadata": {"art_style": "Watercolor", "color_palette": "cool blues and purples", "subject_tags": ["wolf", "moon", "nature", "wildlife", "night"], "style_tags": ["watercolor", "painterly", "ethereal", "atmospheric"], "generation_status": "success", "usage": {"prompt_tokens": 34, "total_tokens": 34}}}}

name: Looker to Salesloft Account Enrichment
raw_steps: Monitor for changes in lead or account status in Looker.
Retrieve customer signals and engagement metrics from Looker.
Push the retrieved data into Salesloft to enrich account records.
Trigger automated task assignments in Salesloft Play based on the updated account data.
Notify sales representatives to take action on the enriched account information.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Looker Enterprise Accounts dashboard for changes in lead or account status and retrieves customer signals and engagement metrics.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen state of account and lead records in the Looker Enterprise Accounts dashboard to detect changes.
objects.behavior: When a change in lead or account status is detected in the Looker Enterprise Accounts dashboard, retrieve the relevant customer signals and engagement metrics and forward them to the account-enrichment peer. When answering queries, call the `looker_dashboard_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward detected account/lead status changes along with retrieved customer signals and engagement metrics for enrichment processing.

event_sources: external system: Looker Enterprise Accounts dashboard account/lead status change

objects.role: Business logic object that applies enrichment rules to incoming Looker account signals and determines what data to push to Salesloft and which Salesloft Play tasks to trigger.
objects.state_description: Tracks enrichment decisions made per account, including which signals were applied and which Salesloft Play tasks were triggered.
objects.behavior: When account signals and engagement metrics arrive from the Looker dashboard, evaluate the data to determine the appropriate enrichment payload for Salesloft account records. Identify which Salesloft Play automation rules apply based on the updated account data. Send the enrichment payload to salesloft-records and the play trigger instructions to salesloft-plays simultaneously. Also send a notification summary to slack-notifications for the Account Executives in the Enterprise Sales team.

peers.relationship: Send enriched account data payload to update Salesloft account records.
Send play trigger instructions based on updated account data to initiate automated task assignments.
Send enriched account summary and action prompt for Account Executives in the Enterprise Sales team.

objects.role: Write service object that receives enriched account data and persists it into Salesloft account records.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all account enrichment payloads received and written to Salesloft, keyed by account ID and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an enriched account data payload is received, record it as a Salesloft account record update in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object that receives play trigger instructions and initiates automated task assignments in Salesloft Play.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Salesloft Play trigger instructions received, including account ID, play name, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When play trigger instructions are received, record them and initiate the corresponding automated task assignments in Salesloft Play.

objects.role: Write service object that posts enriched account update notifications to the #account-updates Slack channel for Account Executives in the Enterprise Sales team.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all notifications posted to #account-updates, including account name, summary, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification message is received, post it to the #account-updates Slack channel to alert Account Executives in the Enterprise Sales team.

steps.text: A change in lead or account status has been detected in the Looker Enterprise Accounts dashboard. Account: Meridian Health Systems. Status change: Lead upgraded to Active Account. Customer signals: 3 product demo sessions in the last 7 days, 85% engagement score, expansion opportunity flag set to true, contract value estimate $240,000, primary contact: Sarah Okonkwo, VP of Operations.

expect.action: Slack message posted to #account-updates notifying Account Executives in the Enterprise Sales team that Meridian Health Systems (primary contact: Sarah Okonkwo, VP of Operations) has been enriched in Salesloft with updated status, engagement score of 85%, expansion opportunity flag, and contract value estimate of $240,000, and that automated Salesloft Play tasks have been triggered for this account.
expect.reason: looker-dashboard forwards signals to account-enrichment, which pushes enriched data to salesloft-records and play instructions to salesloft-plays, then triggers slack-notifications to post to #account-updates

name: Email to Asana Tasks with AI
raw_steps: Monitor Gmail for starred emails.
Extract action items and content from the starred email using AI.
Calculate smart due dates based on email content using AI.
Create a structured task in Asana with the extracted information.
Attach email files to the Asana task automatically.
Send a Slack notification to the team with a direct link to the new Asana task.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for starred emails and forwards them into the automation pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen starred email ID and timestamp to avoid duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new starred email is detected, extract the email metadata (sender, subject, body, attachments) and forward it to the email-task-extractor business logic object. When answering queries, call the gmail_monitor_data tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward full starred email payload (sender, subject, body, attachment references) when a new starred email is detected.

event_sources: gmail: email starred

objects.role: Business logic object that uses OpenAI to extract action items and content from starred emails and derives smart due dates.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current email being processed, extracted action items, and calculated due dates.
objects.behavior: When a starred email payload arrives, call OpenAI to extract structured action items, task titles, descriptions, and relevant content from the email body. Then call OpenAI again to calculate smart due dates based on urgency signals, deadlines mentioned, and context in the email content. When extraction and due date calculation are complete, send the structured task data along with attachment references simultaneously to asana-tasks and slack-notifier.

peers.relationship: Send structured task data (title, description, action items, due date, attachment references) after extraction and due date calculation are complete.
Send task summary and Asana task link once the task creation data is ready.

objects.role: Write service object that creates structured tasks in Asana and attaches email files to those tasks.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Asana tasks created, including task IDs, titles, due dates, and attached file references.
objects.behavior: When structured task data arrives, create a new Asana task in the designated project with the provided title, description, action items, and due date. Automatically attach any referenced email files to the newly created Asana task. Record the created task ID and direct task URL in state.

objects.role: Write service object that sends notifications to the Slack #project-updates channel with a direct link to the new Asana task.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent, including channel, message content, and Asana task links.
objects.behavior: When task summary data and an Asana task link arrive, post a formatted notification message to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task. Record the notification in state.

steps.text: A new email has been starred in Gmail. Email details: From: Marcus Chen, Senior Product Manager (marcus.chen@techcorp.com), Subject: 'Q3 Launch Checklist – Action Items Needed', Body: 'Hi team, following our sync today I need everyone to complete the following before end of month: 1) Finalize feature spec doc by July 18, 2) Coordinate with design team for UI sign-off by July 20, 3) Submit QA test plan by July 22, 4) Schedule stakeholder demo for July 25. Please confirm ownership ASAP. Attachments: Q3_Launch_Spec_v2.pdf, UI_Mockups_Final.zip. Thanks, Marcus'

expect.action: A new Asana task titled 'Q3 Launch Checklist – Action Items Needed' is created in the designated project with extracted action items (finalize feature spec doc due July 18, coordinate with design for UI sign-off due July 20, submit QA test plan due July 22, schedule stakeholder demo due July 25), smart due dates, and attachments Q3_Launch_Spec_v2.pdf and UI_Mockups_Final.zip attached to the task. A Slack notification is posted to #project-updates with the task title, summary, and a direct link to the new Asana task.
expect.reason: gmail-monitor detects the starred email and forwards it to email-task-extractor, which extracts action items and calculates due dates via OpenAI, then forwards structured data to asana-tasks (which creates the task and attaches files) and slack-notifier (which posts to #project-updates with the Asana task link).

name: AI-Powered Stakeholder Update Automation
raw_steps: Project manager submits weekly project updates via a Zapier Interfaces form.
Submitted updates are stored in Zapier Tables for status tracking.
AI Agent uses a project charter and role mapping stored in Notion to identify the appropriate stakeholders for the update.
AI Agent drafts a tailored status update email based on the project information and stakeholder roles.
Draft email is routed to the PM via Slack for approval before sending.
Once approved, the email is automatically delivered to the identified stakeholders via Gmail.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interfaces form where the project manager submits weekly project updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted form payloads including project name, update content, submission timestamp, and submitting PM identity.
objects.behavior: When a project manager submits a weekly update via the form, forward the full update payload to the stakeholder-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full project update payload when a new form submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: form submitted via Zapier Interfaces

objects.role: Write service representing Zapier Tables where submitted project updates are stored for status tracking.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all submitted project updates including project name, update content, PM identity, submission timestamp, approval status, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a project update record is received, store it with its current status. When an approval or delivery status change is received, update the corresponding record's status field.

objects.role: Read service representing the Notion database storing the project charter and stakeholder role mappings.
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves project charter details and stakeholder role mappings on demand.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about project charters and stakeholder role mappings by project. When answering queries, call the `notion_charter_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that identifies appropriate stakeholders for a given project update and orchestrates the drafting and approval workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight update requests including project update content, identified stakeholders and their roles, drafted email content, and approval state per update.
objects.behavior: When a project update payload arrives, store it to update-store and query notion-charter for the project charter and stakeholder role mappings to identify the appropriate stakeholders. Once stakeholder roles are resolved, draft a tailored status update email customized to each stakeholder role and the project context. Send the draft email to slack-approval for PM review. When an approval confirmation arrives from slack-approval, send the finalized email to gmail-delivery addressed to the identified stakeholders. When a rejection or revision request arrives from slack-approval, revise the draft and re-send to slack-approval.

peers.relationship: Send project update record for storage immediately upon receiving a new submission, and send status updates when approval or delivery state changes
Query for project charter and stakeholder role mappings when a new update arrives
Send drafted email and stakeholder list to PM for approval review
Send approved email content and recipient list once PM approval is confirmed

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing Slack, used to send draft emails to the PM for approval and to receive the PM's approval or rejection response.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending approval requests sent to the PM including draft content, stakeholder list, and approval state.
objects.behavior: When a draft email and stakeholder list are received from stakeholder-routing, post them to the PM's Slack channel requesting approval or revision. When the PM responds with an approval or rejection action via Slack, forward the response to stakeholder-routing via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward PM approval or rejection response when the PM acts on a draft email review message

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action event (PM approval or rejection response)

objects.role: Write service object representing Gmail, used to deliver approved stakeholder update emails to identified recipients.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all emails sent including recipient list, subject, body, project reference, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an approved email with recipient list is received, send the email via Gmail to all identified stakeholders and record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Project manager Sarah Chen submits a weekly project update via the Zapier Interfaces form. Project: 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Update details: Phase 2 backend integration is 75% complete, on track for the August 15 milestone. Key risks: third-party API rate limits may slow data sync. Blockers: none currently. Next steps: finalize data mapping with vendor by end of week.

expect.action: Draft stakeholder update email prepared for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' and posted to Sarah Chen's Slack channel requesting approval, along with the identified stakeholder recipient list derived from the Notion project charter.
expect.reason: project-form forwards the update payload to stakeholder-routing, which stores the record in update-store, queries notion-charter for stakeholder role mappings, drafts a tailored email, and routes it to slack-approval to request PM approval.

steps.text: Project manager Sarah Chen responds via Slack interactive action approving the draft stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Slack channel: #pm-approvals, thread_ts: 1712345678.000100. Response: 'Approved — looks good, please send.'

expect.action: Approved stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' delivered via Gmail to all identified stakeholders, and the corresponding record in Zapier Tables updated to 'delivered' status.
expect.reason: slack-approval receives the PM's approval response and forwards the approved email and recipient list to stakeholder-routing, which triggers gmail-delivery to send the email and update-store to update the record status.

name: Databricks to Salesloft Signals Automation
raw_steps: Extract customer engagement data from Databricks including usage trends, engagement levels, and feature adoption.
Send the extracted analytics data to Salesloft Signals.
Monitor for customer usage spikes or drops in the incoming data.
Trigger Salesloft Plays automatically when critical signals are detected.
Deliver actionable insights to sales representatives at the right moment for decision-making.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing Databricks, emitting customer engagement data including usage trends, engagement levels, and feature adoption
objects.state_description: Records of extracted engagement data events received from Databricks
objects.behavior: Receives customer engagement data events from Databricks. When a data event arrives, forward it to the engagement-signal-analyzer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward extracted customer engagement data including usage trends, engagement levels, and feature adoption when a new data event arrives

event_sources: webhook: customer engagement data export
external system: Databricks data pipeline event

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes customer engagement data, detects usage spikes or drops, and determines when critical signals warrant triggering Salesloft Plays or delivering insights to Account Executives
objects.state_description: Tracks current engagement signal state per customer including recent usage trends, spike/drop thresholds, engagement levels, and feature adoption metrics
objects.behavior: When customer engagement data arrives from Databricks, analyze usage trends, engagement levels, and feature adoption. Apply signal detection rules: if a usage spike exceeds the defined threshold or a usage drop falls below the defined threshold, classify as a critical signal. If a critical signal is detected, send the signal details to salesloft-signals for recording and to salesloft-plays for triggering the appropriate Play. Always send actionable insights derived from the analysis to account-executive-insights for delivery to Account Executives. If no critical signal is detected but engagement data is noteworthy, still forward insights to account-executive-insights.

peers.relationship: Send extracted analytics data and detected signal details when engagement data is processed
Send critical signal details and trigger instructions when a usage spike or drop is detected
Send actionable insights derived from engagement analysis for delivery to Account Executives

objects.role: Write service object representing Salesloft Signals, recording incoming analytics data and detected engagement signals
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all analytics data and signals sent to Salesloft Signals, initially empty
objects.behavior: Receives analytics data and signal details and records them into Salesloft Signals. Maintains an audit trail of all received signal events.

objects.role: Write service object representing Salesloft Plays, receiving trigger instructions to automatically activate the appropriate Play when critical signals are detected
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Play trigger events received, initially empty
objects.behavior: Receives critical signal details and trigger instructions and activates the corresponding Salesloft Play. Records each triggered Play event in the audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object that delivers actionable insights to Account Executives at the right moment for decision-making
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all insight notifications delivered to Account Executives, initially empty
objects.behavior: Receives actionable insights and delivers them to the appropriate Account Executives via the configured notification channel. Records each delivered insight in the audit trail.

steps.text: Customer engagement data export from Databricks data pipeline: Account 'Meridian Technologies' (Account ID: MT-8821) shows a usage spike of 340% above baseline over the past 7 days. Engagement level: Critical-High. Feature adoption: Advanced Analytics module activated by 18 new users this week. Usage trend: accelerating. Signal timestamp: 2024-06-12T09:15:00Z.

expect.action: Analytics data and critical usage spike signal for Meridian Technologies (Account ID: MT-8821, 340% above baseline, Critical-High engagement, Advanced Analytics module adopted by 18 new users) recorded in Salesloft Signals; Salesloft Play triggered for critical usage spike signal for Meridian Technologies; actionable insight delivered to Account Executives noting the usage spike and feature adoption details for Meridian Technologies.
expect.reason: Databricks forwards the engagement data to engagement-signal-analyzer, which detects a usage spike exceeding the defined threshold, causing it to send analytics data to salesloft-signals, trigger a Play via salesloft-plays, and deliver insights via account-executive-insights.
